921#[cfg(test)]
922mod tests {
923    use super::{build_daily_note_relative_path, list_trash_items_internal, restore_trash_item_internal_for_tests};
924    use chrono::TimeZone;
925    use std::fs;
926    use tempfile::tempdir;
927    
928    #[test]
929    fn test_build_daily_note_relative_path() {
930        let now = chrono::Local
931            .with_ymd_and_hms(2026, 3, 11, 9, 0, 0)
932            .unwrap();
933        let path = build_daily_note_relative_path("Daily/{YYYY}/{MM}/{DD}.md", now);
934        assert_eq!(path, "Daily/2026/03/11.md");
935    }
936    
937    #[test]
938    fn test_build_daily_note_relative_path_adds_md() {
939        let now = chrono::Local
940            .with_ymd_and_hms(2026, 3, 11, 9, 0, 0)
941            .unwrap();
942        let path = build_daily_note_relative_path("Daily/{YYYY}-{MM}-{DD}", now);
943        assert_eq!(path, "Daily/2026-03-11.md");
944    }
945    
946    #[test]
947    fn list_trash_items_returns_clean_names_sorted_newest_first() {
948        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
949        let vault = dir.path();
950        let trash = vault.join(".trash");
951        fs::create_dir_all(&trash).unwrap();
952        fs::write(trash.join("Alpha (Root) 1000.md"), "").unwrap();
953        fs::write(trash.join("Beta (Root) 2000.md"), "").unwrap();
954        
955        let listed = list_trash_items_internal(vault).unwrap();
956        
957        assert_eq!(listed.len(), 2);
958        assert_eq!(listed[0].display_name, "Beta.md");
959        assert_eq!(listed[1].display_name, "Alpha.md");
960    }
961    
962    #[test]
963    fn restore_trash_item_moves_file_back_to_root_with_clean_name() {
964        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
965        let vault = dir.path();
966        let trash = vault.join(".trash");
967        fs::create_dir_all(&trash).unwrap();
968        let trashed = trash.join("Note (Root) 1740681450123.md");
969        fs::write(&trashed, "restored").unwrap();
970        
971        let restored_path = restore_trash_item_internal_for_tests(vault, &trashed).unwrap();
972        
973        assert_eq!(restored_path, vault.join("Note.md"));
974        assert_eq!(fs::read_to_string(vault.join("Note.md")).unwrap(), "restored");
975        assert!(!trashed.exists());
976    }
977    
978    #[test]
979    fn restore_trash_item_uses_restored_suffix_when_destination_exists() {
980        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
981        let vault = dir.path();
982        let trash = vault.join(".trash");
983        fs::create_dir_all(&trash).unwrap();
984        fs::write(vault.join("Note.md"), "existing").unwrap();
985        let trashed = trash.join("Note (Root) 1740681450123.md");
986        fs::write(&trashed, "restored").unwrap();
987        
988        let restored_path = restore_trash_item_internal_for_tests(vault, &trashed).unwrap();
989        
990        assert_eq!(restored_path, vault.join("Note (Restored).md"));
991        assert_eq!(fs::read_to_string(restored_path).unwrap(), "restored");
992    }
993    
994    #[test]
995    fn restore_trashed_folder_recursively_restores_child_names() {
996        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
997        let vault = dir.path();
998        let trash = vault.join(".trash");
999        let trashed_dir = trash.join("Project (Root) 1740681450123");
1000        fs::create_dir_all(&trashed_dir).unwrap();
1001        fs::write(
1002            trashed_dir.join("Child Note (Project) 1740681450123.md"),
1003            "nested",
1004        )
1005            .unwrap();
1006        
1007        let restored_path = restore_trash_item_internal_for_tests(vault, &trashed_dir).unwrap();
1008        
1009        assert!(restored_path.join("Child Note.md").exists());
1010        assert_eq!(
1011            fs::read_to_string(restored_path.join("Child Note.md")).unwrap(),
1012            "nested"
1013        );
1014    }
1015}
pdf_export.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib\commands/
pdf_export.rs

1use crate::error::TessellumError;
2use lopdf::{Dictionary, Document, Object, ObjectId};
3use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
4use std::env;
5use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
6use tempfile::tempdir;
7use tokio::process::Command;
8use url::Url;
9
10const EXPORT_PAGE_HEIGHT_PX: f32 = 1122.0;
11const EXPORT_PAGE_MARGIN_TOP_PX: f32 = 28.0;
12const EXPORT_PAGE_MARGIN_BOTTOM_PX: f32 = 36.0;
13const EXPORT_PAGE_CONTENT_HEIGHT_PX: f32 =
14    EXPORT_PAGE_HEIGHT_PX - EXPORT_PAGE_MARGIN_TOP_PX - EXPORT_PAGE_MARGIN_BOTTOM_PX;
15const CSS_PX_TO_PDF_PT: f32 = 72.0 / 96.0;
16
17#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
18#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
19pub struct PdfExportOutlineItem {
20    pub title: String,
21    pub level: u32,
22    pub line_number: u32,
23    pub page: u32,
24    pub offset_within_page_px: f32,
25}
26
27#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
28#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
29pub struct PdfExportRequest {
30    pub destination_path: String,
31    pub document_title: String,
32    pub html: String,
33    pub outline: Vec<PdfExportOutlineItem>,
34}
35
36#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
37struct NormalizedOutlineEntry {
38    title: String,
39    level: u32,
40    page: u32,
41    offset_within_page_px: f32,
42    parent_index: Option<usize>,
43}
44
45fn validate_export_request(request: &PdfExportRequest) -> Result<(), TessellumError> {
46    if request.destination_path.trim().is_empty() {
47        return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
48            "PDF destination path cannot be empty".to_string(),
49        ));
50    }
51
52    if request.html.trim().is_empty() {
53        return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
54            "PDF HTML payload cannot be empty".to_string(),
55        ));
56    }
57
58    Ok(())
59}
60
61fn normalize_outline_entries(
62    outline: &[PdfExportOutlineItem],
63    total_pages: u32,
64) -> Vec<NormalizedOutlineEntry> {
65    if total_pages == 0 {
66        return Vec::new();
67    }
68
69    let mut parent_stack: Vec<(u32, usize)> = Vec::new();
70    let mut normalized = Vec::with_capacity(outline.len());
71
72    for item in outline {
73        let level = item.level.max(1);
74        let page = item.page.clamp(1, total_pages);
75
76        while parent_stack.last().is_some_and(|(parent_level, _)| *parent_level >= level) {
77            parent_stack.pop();
78        }
79
80        let parent_index = parent_stack.last().map(|(_, index)| *index);
81        normalized.push(NormalizedOutlineEntry {
82            title: item.title.clone(),
83            level,
84            page,
85            offset_within_page_px: item.offset_within_page_px.clamp(0.0, EXPORT_PAGE_CONTENT_HEIGHT_PX),
86            parent_index,
87        });
88        parent_stack.push((level, normalized.len() - 1));
89    }
90
91    normalized
92}
93
94fn encode_outline_title(title: &str) -> Object {
95    if title.is_ascii() {
96        return Object::string_literal(title.as_bytes().to_vec());
97    }
98
99    let mut bom = vec![0xFE, 0xFF];
100    bom.extend(title.encode_utf16().flat_map(u16::to_be_bytes));
101    Object::string_literal(bom)
102}
103
104fn resolve_page_height_points(document: &Document, page_id: ObjectId) -> f32 {
105    let default_height = 842.0;
106
107    let Ok(page) = document.get_dictionary(page_id) else {
108        return default_height;
109    };
110    let Ok(media_box) = page.get(b"MediaBox").and_then(Object::as_array) else {
111        return default_height;
112    };
113    if media_box.len() != 4 {
114        return default_height;
115    }
116
117    let bottom = media_box[1].as_float().unwrap_or(0.0);
118    let top = media_box[3].as_float().unwrap_or(default_height);
119    let height = top - bottom;
120
121    if height.is_finite() && height > 0.0 {
122        height
123    } else {
124        default_height
125    }
126}
127
128fn heading_top_position_points(page_height_points: f32, offset_within_page_px: f32) -> f32 {
129    let top_margin_points = EXPORT_PAGE_MARGIN_TOP_PX * CSS_PX_TO_PDF_PT;
130    let bottom_margin_points = EXPORT_PAGE_MARGIN_BOTTOM_PX * CSS_PX_TO_PDF_PT;
131    let content_height_points = (page_height_points - top_margin_points - bottom_margin_points).max(1.0);
132    let clamped_offset = offset_within_page_px.clamp(0.0, EXPORT_PAGE_CONTENT_HEIGHT_PX);
133    let scaled_offset = (clamped_offset / EXPORT_PAGE_CONTENT_HEIGHT_PX) * content_height_points;
134    let top_position = page_height_points - top_margin_points - scaled_offset;
135
136    top_position.clamp(0.0, page_height_points)
137}
138
139fn build_outline_action(page_id: ObjectId, top_position_points: f32) -> Dictionary {
140    let mut action = Dictionary::new();
141    action.set("D", vec![
142        Object::Reference(page_id),
143        Object::Name(b"XYZ".to_vec()),
144        Object::Null,
145        Object::Real(top_position_points),
146        Object::Null,
147    ]);
148    action.set("S", Object::Name(b"GoTo".to_vec()));
149    action
150}
151
152fn build_outline_objects(
153    document: &mut Document,
154    outline: &[NormalizedOutlineEntry],
155    page_ids: &[ObjectId],
156) -> Result<ObjectId, TessellumError> {
157    let outlines_id = document.new_object_id();
158    if outline.is_empty() {
159        document.set_object(outlines_id, Dictionary::new());
160        return Ok(outlines_id);
161    }
162
163    let item_ids: Vec<_> = outline.iter().map(|_| document.new_object_id()).collect();
164    let action_ids: Vec<_> = outline.iter().map(|_| document.new_object_id()).collect();
165    let mut children_by_parent = vec![Vec::<usize>::new(); outline.len()];
166    let mut root_children = Vec::<usize>::new();
167
168    for (index, entry) in outline.iter().enumerate() {
169        if let Some(parent_index) = entry.parent_index {
170            children_by_parent[parent_index].push(index);
171        } else {
172            root_children.push(index);
173        }
174    }
175
176    for (index, entry) in outline.iter().enumerate() {
177        let page_id = page_ids[(entry.page - 1) as usize];
178        let page_height_points = resolve_page_height_points(document, page_id);
179        let top_position_points =
180            heading_top_position_points(page_height_points, entry.offset_within_page_px);
181        document.set_object(action_ids[index], build_outline_action(page_id, top_position_points));
182    }
183
184    for (index, entry) in outline.iter().enumerate() {
185        let siblings = if let Some(parent_index) = entry.parent_index {
186            &children_by_parent[parent_index]
187        } else {
188            &root_children
189        };
190        let sibling_position = siblings.iter().position(|candidate| *candidate == index).unwrap_or(0);
191
192        let mut item = Dictionary::new();
193        item.set(
194            "Parent",
195            Object::Reference(entry.parent_index.map(|parent_index| item_ids[parent_index]).unwrap_or(outlines_id)),
196        );
197        item.set("Title", encode_outline_title(&entry.title));
198        item.set("A", Object::Reference(action_ids[index]));
199        item.set("F", Object::Integer(0));
200        item.set("C", vec![Object::Real(0.0), Object::Real(0.0), Object::Real(0.0)]);
201
202        if sibling_position > 0 {
203            item.set("Prev", Object::Reference(item_ids[siblings[sibling_position - 1]]));
204        }
205        if sibling_position + 1 < siblings.len() {
206            item.set("Next", Object::Reference(item_ids[siblings[sibling_position + 1]]));
207        }
208
209        let children = &children_by_parent[index];
210        if !children.is_empty() {
211            item.set("First", Object::Reference(item_ids[children[0]]));
212            item.set("Last", Object::Reference(item_ids[*children.last().unwrap()]));
213            item.set("Count", Object::Integer(children.len() as i64));
214        }
215
216        document.set_object(item_ids[index], item);
217    }
218
219    let mut outlines = Dictionary::new();
220    outlines.set("First", Object::Reference(item_ids[root_children[0]]));
221    outlines.set("Last", Object::Reference(item_ids[*root_children.last().unwrap()]));
222    outlines.set("Count", Object::Integer(root_children.len() as i64));
223    document.set_object(outlines_id, outlines);
224
225    Ok(outlines_id)
226}
227
228fn inject_outline_into_pdf(
229    destination_path: &Path,
230    outline: &[PdfExportOutlineItem],
231) -> Result<(), TessellumError> {
232    if outline.is_empty() {
233        return Ok(());
234    }
235
236    let mut document = Document::load(destination_path)
237        .map_err(|error| TessellumError::Internal(format!("Failed to open generated PDF: {error}")))?;
238    let pages = document.get_pages();
239    if pages.is_empty() {
240        return Ok(());
241    }
242
243    let page_ids: Vec<_> = pages.values().copied().collect();
244    let normalized = normalize_outline_entries(outline, page_ids.len() as u32);
245    let outlines_id = build_outline_objects(&mut document, &normalized, &page_ids)?;
246    document
247        .catalog_mut()
248        .map_err(|error| TessellumError::Internal(format!("Failed to access PDF catalog: {error}")))?
249        .set("Outlines", Object::Reference(outlines_id));
250    document
251        .save(destination_path)
252        .map_err(|error| TessellumError::Internal(format!("Failed to save outlined PDF: {error}")))?;
253
254    Ok(())
255}
256
257fn candidate_browser_paths() -> Vec<PathBuf> {
258    let mut candidates = Vec::new();
259
260    for root in [
261        env::var_os("PROGRAMFILES"),
262        env::var_os("PROGRAMFILES(X86)"),
263        env::var_os("LOCALAPPDATA"),
264    ]
265    .into_iter()
266    .flatten()
267    {
268        let root = PathBuf::from(root);
269        candidates.push(root.join("Microsoft").join("Edge").join("Application").join("msedge.exe"));
270        candidates.push(root.join("Google").join("Chrome").join("Application").join("chrome.exe"));
271    }
272
273    candidates
274}
275
276fn find_print_browser() -> Result<PathBuf, TessellumError> {
277    candidate_browser_paths()
278        .into_iter()
279        .find(|path| path.exists())
280        .ok_or_else(|| {
281            TessellumError::Internal(
282                "No compatible browser was found for PDF export. Install Microsoft Edge or Google Chrome."
283                    .to_string(),
284            )
285        })
286}
287
288fn build_print_pdf_args(html_url: &str, destination_path: &Path) -> Vec<String> {
289    vec![
290        "--headless".to_string(),
291        "--disable-gpu".to_string(),
292        "--allow-file-access-from-files".to_string(),
293        "--no-first-run".to_string(),
294        "--no-pdf-header-footer".to_string(),
295        "--print-to-pdf-no-header".to_string(),
296        format!("--print-to-pdf={}", destination_path.display()),
297        html_url.to_string(),
298    ]
299}
300
301#[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
302async fn render_html_to_pdf(html_path: &Path, destination_path: &Path) -> Result<(), TessellumError> {
303    let browser_path = find_print_browser()?;
304    let html_url = Url::from_file_path(html_path).map_err(|_| {
305        TessellumError::Internal("Failed to convert export HTML path into a file URL".to_string())
306    })?;
307    let args = build_print_pdf_args(html_url.as_str(), destination_path);
308
309    let status = Command::new(browser_path)
310        .args(args)
311        .status()
312        .await
313        .map_err(TessellumError::Io)?;
314
315    if !status.success() || !destination_path.exists() {
316        return Err(TessellumError::Internal(
317            "Browser PDF generation failed".to_string(),
318        ));
319    }
320
321    Ok(())
322}
323
324#[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
325async fn render_html_to_pdf(_html_path: &Path, _destination_path: &Path) -> Result<(), TessellumError> {
326    Err(TessellumError::Internal(
327        "PDF export is currently supported on Windows only".to_string(),
328    ))
329}
330
331#[tauri::command]
332pub async fn export_markdown_pdf(request: PdfExportRequest) -> Result<(), TessellumError> {
333    validate_export_request(&request)?;
334
335    let destination_path = PathBuf::from(&request.destination_path);
336    let parent = destination_path.parent().ok_or_else(|| {
337        TessellumError::Validation("PDF destination must include a parent directory".to_string())
338    })?;
339    if !parent.exists() {
340        return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
341            "PDF destination directory does not exist".to_string(),
342        ));
343    }
344
345    let temp_dir = tempdir().map_err(TessellumError::Io)?;
346    let html_path = temp_dir.path().join("export.html");
347    tokio::fs::write(&html_path, &request.html)
348        .await
349        .map_err(TessellumError::Io)?;
350
351    render_html_to_pdf(&html_path, &destination_path).await?;
352    inject_outline_into_pdf(&destination_path, &request.outline)?;
353
354    Ok(())
355}
356
357#[cfg(test)]
358mod tests {
359    use super::{
360        build_print_pdf_args, heading_top_position_points, normalize_outline_entries,
361        validate_export_request, PdfExportOutlineItem, PdfExportRequest,
362    };
363    use std::path::Path;
364
365    #[test]
366    fn pdf_export_validation_rejects_empty_destination() {
367        let result = validate_export_request(&PdfExportRequest {
368            destination_path: String::new(),
369            document_title: "Doc".to_string(),
370            html: "<html></html>".to_string(),
371            outline: Vec::new(),
372        });
373
374        assert!(result.is_err());
375    }
376
377    #[test]
378    fn pdf_export_validation_rejects_empty_html() {
379        let result = validate_export_request(&PdfExportRequest {
380            destination_path: "C:/tmp/doc.pdf".to_string(),
381            document_title: "Doc".to_string(),
382            html: "   ".to_string(),
383            outline: Vec::new(),
384        });
385
386        assert!(result.is_err());
387    }
388
389    #[test]
390    fn normalize_outline_entries_preserves_hierarchy_and_clamps_pages() {
391        let normalized = normalize_outline_entries(
392            &[
393                PdfExportOutlineItem {
394                    title: "Intro".to_string(),
395                    level: 1,
396                    line_number: 1,
397                    page: 1,
398                    offset_within_page_px: 10.0,
399                },
400                PdfExportOutlineItem {
401                    title: "Details".to_string(),
402                    level: 2,
403                    line_number: 4,
404                    page: 9,
405                    offset_within_page_px: 24.0,
406                },
407                PdfExportOutlineItem {
408                    title: "Appendix".to_string(),
409                    level: 1,
410                    line_number: 10,
411                    page: 0,
412                    offset_within_page_px: 50.0,
413                },
414            ],
415            3,
416        );
417
418        assert_eq!(
419            normalized,
420            vec![
421                super::NormalizedOutlineEntry {
422                    title: "Intro".to_string(),
423                    level: 1,
424                    page: 1,
425                    offset_within_page_px: 10.0,
426                    parent_index: None,
427                },
428                super::NormalizedOutlineEntry {
429                    title: "Details".to_string(),
430                    level: 2,
431                    page: 3,
432                    offset_within_page_px: 24.0,
433                    parent_index: Some(0),
434                },
435                super::NormalizedOutlineEntry {
436                    title: "Appendix".to_string(),
437                    level: 1,
438                    page: 1,
439                    offset_within_page_px: 50.0,
440                    parent_index: None,
441                },
442            ]
443        );
444    }
445
446    #[test]
447    fn heading_top_position_points_tracks_heading_offset_within_page() {
448        let first_heading = heading_top_position_points(792.0, 0.0);
449        let lower_heading = heading_top_position_points(792.0, 400.0);
450
451        assert!(first_heading < 792.0);
452        assert!(lower_heading < first_heading);
453        assert!(lower_heading > 0.0);
454    }
455
456    #[test]
457    fn build_print_pdf_args_disables_browser_headers_and_footers() {
458        let args = build_print_pdf_args("file:///tmp/export.html", Path::new("C:/tmp/doc.pdf"));
459
460        assert!(args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--no-pdf-header-footer"));
461        assert!(args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--print-to-pdf-no-header"));
462        assert!(args.iter().any(|arg| arg == "--print-to-pdf=C:/tmp/doc.pdf"));
463    }
464}

Files

tessellum_lib\commands/
search.rs

1use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
2use std::collections::HashSet;
3use std::path::Path;
4use tauri::State;
5
6use crate::commands::indexer::run_sync_vault;
7use crate::error::TessellumError;
8use crate::grafeo_projection::ManagedGrafeoConnection;
9use crate::models::{AppState, SearchReadinessState, SearchReadinessStatus};
10use crate::utils::is_hidden_or_special;
11use crate::utils::normalize_path;
12use walkdir::WalkDir;
13
14const SEARCH_MAX_ATTEMPTS: u32 = 10;
15const SEARCH_RETRY_DELAY_MS: u64 = 5_000;
16const SEARCH_MISMATCH_THRESHOLD_RATIO: f64 = 0.01;
17
18fn log_readiness_transition(
19	vault_path: &str,
20	from: &SearchReadinessStatus,
21	to: &SearchReadinessStatus,
22	attempt: u32,
23	detail: &str,
24) {
25	if from == to {
26		log::debug!(
27			"[search-readiness] vault={} status={} attempt={} detail={}",
28			vault_path,
29			status_to_string(to),
30			attempt,
31			detail,
32		);
33		return;
34	}
35
36	log::info!(
37		"[search-readiness] vault={} {} -> {} attempt={} detail={}",
38		vault_path,
39		status_to_string(from),
40		status_to_string(to),
41		attempt,
42		detail,
43	);
44}
45
46#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
47pub struct TagFilter {
48	pub tags: Vec<String>,
49	pub match_mode: TagMatchMode,
50}
51
52#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
53pub enum TagMatchMode {
54	All,
55	Any,
56}
57
58#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
59pub struct FullTextSearchRequest {
60	pub query: String,
61	pub limit: Option<u32>,
62	pub offset: Option<u32>,
63	pub include_snippets: Option<bool>,
64	pub tag_filter: Option<TagFilter>,
65}
66
67#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
68pub struct TagSearchRequest {
69	pub tags: Vec<String>,
70	pub match_mode: TagMatchMode,
71	pub limit: Option<u32>,
72	pub offset: Option<u32>,
73}
74
75#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
76pub struct SearchHit {
77	pub path: String,
78	pub relative_path: String,
79	pub title: String,
80	pub score: f32,
81	pub snippet: Option<String>,
82	pub tags: Vec<String>,
83}
84
85#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
86pub struct FullTextSearchResponse {
87	pub total: u32,
88	pub hits: Vec<SearchHit>,
89}
90
91#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
92pub struct TagSearchResponse {
93	pub total: u32,
94	pub hits: Vec<SearchHit>,
95}
96
97#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
98pub struct IndexRebuildResult {
99	pub indexed_files: usize,
100	pub deleted_files: usize,
101	pub duration_ms: u128,
102}
103
104#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
105pub struct SearchReadinessResponse {
106	pub status: String,
107	pub attempt_count: u32,
108	pub max_attempts: u32,
109	pub retry_delay_ms: u64,
110	pub reopen_required: bool,
111	pub last_error: Option<String>,
112}
113
114struct CoherenceResult {
115	expected_markdown_count: usize,
116	indexed_markdown_count: usize,
117	mismatch_count: usize,
118	mismatch_threshold: usize,
119}
120
121fn status_to_string(status: &SearchReadinessStatus) -> String {
122	match status {
123		SearchReadinessStatus::Idle => "idle",
124		SearchReadinessStatus::Warming => "warming",
125		SearchReadinessStatus::Ready => "ready",
126		SearchReadinessStatus::Failed => "failed",
127	}
128		.to_string()
129}
130
131fn readiness_response(state: &SearchReadinessState) -> SearchReadinessResponse {
132	SearchReadinessResponse {
133		status: status_to_string(&state.status),
134		attempt_count: state.attempt_count,
135		max_attempts: state.max_attempts,
136		retry_delay_ms: SEARCH_RETRY_DELAY_MS,
137		reopen_required: state.attempt_count >= state.max_attempts,
138		last_error: state.last_error.clone(),
139	}
140}
141
142fn mismatch_threshold(expected_markdown_count: usize) -> usize {
143	if expected_markdown_count == 0 {
144		return 1;
145	}
146	let threshold = (expected_markdown_count as f64 * SEARCH_MISMATCH_THRESHOLD_RATIO).ceil() as usize;
147	threshold.max(1)
148}
149
150fn is_markdown_path(path: &str) -> bool {
151	path.to_ascii_lowercase().ends_with(".md")
152}
153
154fn count_mismatches_with_early_exit(
155	expected_paths: &HashSet<String>,
156	indexed_paths: &HashSet<String>,
157	stop_after: usize,
158) -> usize {
159	let mut mismatches = 0usize;
160	for path in expected_paths {
161		if !indexed_paths.contains(path) {
162			mismatches += 1;
163			if mismatches >= stop_after {
164				return mismatches;
165			}
166		}
167	}
168	for path in indexed_paths {
169		if !expected_paths.contains(path) {
170			mismatches += 1;
171			if mismatches >= stop_after {
172				return mismatches;
173			}
174		}
175	}
176	mismatches
177}
178
179async fn compute_markdown_coherence(state: &State<'_, AppState>) -> Result<CoherenceResult, TessellumError> {
180	let db = state.db.clone();
181	let expected_search_files = db
182		.get_all_search_files()
183		.await
184		.map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
185	let expected_markdown_paths: HashSet<String> = expected_search_files
186		.into_iter()
187		.filter(|(_, _, is_markdown)| *is_markdown == 1)
188		.map(|(path, _, _)| normalize_path(&path))
189		.collect();
190	let expected_markdown_count = expected_markdown_paths.len();
191	let threshold = mismatch_threshold(expected_markdown_count);
192
193	let search_index = state.search_index.clone();
194	let indexed_paths = tauri::async_runtime::spawn_blocking(move || {
195		let guard = tauri::async_runtime::block_on(search_index.lock());
196		guard.indexed_paths()
197	})
198	.await
199	.map_err(|e| TessellumError::Internal(format!("Index coherence task failed: {e}")))?
200	.map_err(TessellumError::Internal)?;
201
202	let indexed_markdown_paths: HashSet<String> = indexed_paths
203		.into_iter()
204		.filter(|path| is_markdown_path(path))
205		.collect();
206	let indexed_markdown_count = indexed_markdown_paths.len();
207
208	let mismatches = count_mismatches_with_early_exit(
209		&expected_markdown_paths,
210		&indexed_markdown_paths,
211		threshold,
212	);
213
214	Ok(CoherenceResult {
215		expected_markdown_count,
216		indexed_markdown_count,
217		mismatch_count: mismatches,
218		mismatch_threshold: threshold,
219	})
220}
221
222fn needs_rebuild(coherence: &CoherenceResult) -> bool {
223	if coherence.expected_markdown_count == 0 {
224		return coherence.indexed_markdown_count > 0;
225	}
226	coherence.mismatch_count >= coherence.mismatch_threshold
227}
228
229#[tauri::command]
230pub async fn get_search_readiness(
231	state: State<'_, AppState>,
232	vault_path: String,
233) -> Result<SearchReadinessResponse, TessellumError> {
234	let mut readiness = state.search_readiness.lock().await;
235	if readiness.vault_path.as_deref() != Some(vault_path.as_str()) {
236		*readiness = SearchReadinessState::default();
237		readiness.max_attempts = SEARCH_MAX_ATTEMPTS;
238		readiness.vault_path = Some(vault_path.clone());
239	}
240	Ok(readiness_response(&readiness))
241}
242
243#[tauri::command]
244pub async fn reset_search_readiness_attempts(
245	state: State<'_, AppState>,
246	vault_path: String,
247) -> Result<SearchReadinessResponse, TessellumError> {
248	let mut readiness = state.search_readiness.lock().await;
249	if readiness.vault_path.as_deref() != Some(vault_path.as_str()) {
250		*readiness = SearchReadinessState::default();
251		readiness.vault_path = Some(vault_path.clone());
252	}
253	let previous = readiness.status.clone();
254	readiness.attempt_count = 0;
255	readiness.status = SearchReadinessStatus::Idle;
256	readiness.last_error = None;
257	readiness.max_attempts = SEARCH_MAX_ATTEMPTS;
258	log_readiness_transition(
259		&vault_path,
260		&previous,
261		&readiness.status,
262		readiness.attempt_count,
263		"attempts reset",
264	);
265	Ok(readiness_response(&readiness))
266}
267
268#[tauri::command]
269pub async fn ensure_search_ready(
270	state: State<'_, AppState>,
271	kuzu_state: State<'_, ManagedGrafeoConnection>,
272	vault_path: String,
273) -> Result<SearchReadinessResponse, TessellumError> {
274	{
275		let mut readiness = state.search_readiness.lock().await;
276		if readiness.vault_path.as_deref() != Some(vault_path.as_str()) {
277			let previous = readiness.status.clone();
278			*readiness = SearchReadinessState::default();
279			readiness.vault_path = Some(vault_path.clone());
280			log_readiness_transition(
281				&vault_path,
282				&previous,
283				&readiness.status,
284				readiness.attempt_count,
285				"vault scope changed",
286			);
287		}
288		readiness.max_attempts = SEARCH_MAX_ATTEMPTS;
289
290		if readiness.status == SearchReadinessStatus::Ready {
291			log_readiness_transition(
292				&vault_path,
293				&readiness.status,
294				&readiness.status,
295				readiness.attempt_count,
296				"already ready",
297			);
298			return Ok(readiness_response(&readiness));
299		}
300		if readiness.status == SearchReadinessStatus::Warming {
301			log_readiness_transition(
302				&vault_path,
303				&readiness.status,
304				&readiness.status,
305				readiness.attempt_count,
306				"warm-up already in progress",
307			);
308			return Ok(readiness_response(&readiness));
309		}
310		if readiness.attempt_count >= readiness.max_attempts {
311			let previous = readiness.status.clone();
312			readiness.status = SearchReadinessStatus::Failed;
313			log_readiness_transition(
314				&vault_path,
315				&previous,
316				&readiness.status,
317				readiness.attempt_count,
318				"retry budget exhausted",
319			);
320			return Ok(readiness_response(&readiness));
321		}
322
323		let previous = readiness.status.clone();
324		readiness.attempt_count += 1;
325		readiness.status = SearchReadinessStatus::Warming;
326		readiness.last_error = None;
327		log_readiness_transition(
328			&vault_path,
329			&previous,
330			&readiness.status,
331			readiness.attempt_count,
332			"starting warm-up",
333		);
334	}
335
336	let warm_result: Result<(), TessellumError> = async {
337		let sync_result = run_sync_vault(state.inner(), kuzu_state.inner(), &vault_path).await?;
338		if !sync_result.success {
339			return Err(TessellumError::Internal(
340				sync_result
341					.error
342					.unwrap_or_else(|| "sync_vault reported failure".to_string()),
343			));
344		}
345
346		let coherence = compute_markdown_coherence(&state).await?;
347		if needs_rebuild(&coherence) {
348			rebuild_search_index_internal(state.inner(), vault_path.clone()).await?;
349		}
350		Ok(())
351	}
352	.await;
353
354	let mut readiness = state.search_readiness.lock().await;
355	match warm_result {
356		Ok(()) => {
357			let previous = readiness.status.clone();
358			readiness.status = SearchReadinessStatus::Ready;
359			readiness.last_error = None;
360			log_readiness_transition(
361				&vault_path,
362				&previous,
363				&readiness.status,
364				readiness.attempt_count,
365				"warm-up complete",
366			);
367		}
368		Err(error) => {
369			let previous = readiness.status.clone();
370			readiness.status = SearchReadinessStatus::Failed;
371			readiness.last_error = Some(error.to_string());
372			log_readiness_transition(
373				&vault_path,
374				&previous,
375				&readiness.status,
376				readiness.attempt_count,
377				"warm-up failed",
378			);
379		}
380	}
381
382	Ok(readiness_response(&readiness))
383}
384
385#[tauri::command]
386pub async fn search_full_text(
387	state: State<'_, AppState>,
388	vault_path: String,
389	request: FullTextSearchRequest,
390) -> Result<FullTextSearchResponse, TessellumError> {
391	let limit = request.limit.unwrap_or(25) as usize;
392	let offset = request.offset.unwrap_or(0) as usize;
393	let include_snippets = request.include_snippets.unwrap_or(false);
394	
395	let tags = request
396		.tag_filter
397		.as_ref()
398		.map(|f| f.tags.clone())
399		.unwrap_or_default();
400	let match_all = request
401		.tag_filter
402		.as_ref()
403		.map(|f| f.match_mode == TagMatchMode::All)
404		.unwrap_or(true);
405	
406	let search_index = state.search_index.clone();
407	let query = request.query.clone();
408	let vault_root = vault_path.clone();
409	
410	let results = tauri::async_runtime::spawn_blocking(move || {
411		let guard = tauri::async_runtime::block_on(search_index.lock());
412		guard.search(&query, &tags, match_all, limit, offset)
413	})
414		.await
415		.map_err(|e| TessellumError::Internal(format!("Search task failed: {e}")))?
416		.map_err(|e| TessellumError::Internal(e))?;
417	
418	let mut hits = Vec::new();
419	for (doc, score) in results {
420		let relative_path = make_relative_path(&vault_root, &doc.path);
421		let snippet = if include_snippets {
422			read_snippet(&doc.path, &request.query).await
423		} else {
424			None
425		};
426		
427		hits.push(SearchHit {
428			path: doc.path,
429			relative_path,
430			title: doc.title,
431			score,
432			snippet,
433			tags: doc.tags,
434		});
435	}
436	
437	Ok(FullTextSearchResponse {
438		total: hits.len() as u32,
439		hits,
440	})
441}
442
443#[tauri::command]
444pub async fn search_tags(
445	state: State<'_, AppState>,
446	vault_path: String,
447	request: TagSearchRequest,
448) -> Result<TagSearchResponse, TessellumError> {
449	let limit = request.limit.unwrap_or(50);
450	let offset = request.offset.unwrap_or(0);
451	let match_all = request.match_mode == TagMatchMode::All;
452	
453	let db = state.db.clone();
454	let (paths, total) = db
455		.search_notes_by_tags(&request.tags, match_all, limit, offset)
456		.await
457		.map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
458	
459	let mut hits = Vec::new();
460	for path in paths {
461		let title = Path::new(&path)
462			.file_name()
463			.unwrap_or_default()
464			.to_string_lossy()
465			.to_string()
466			.trim_end_matches(".md")
467			.to_string();
468		hits.push(SearchHit {
469			path: normalize_path(&path),
470			relative_path: make_relative_path(&vault_path, &path),
471			title,
472			score: 0.0,
473			snippet: None,
474			tags: Vec::new(),
475		});
476	}
477	
478	Ok(TagSearchResponse { total, hits })
479}
480
481#[tauri::command]
482pub async fn rebuild_search_index(
483	state: State<'_, AppState>,
484	vault_path: String,
485) -> Result<IndexRebuildResult, TessellumError> {
486	rebuild_search_index_internal(state.inner(), vault_path).await
487}
488
489async fn rebuild_search_index_internal(
490	state: &AppState,
491	vault_path: String,
492) -> Result<IndexRebuildResult, TessellumError> {
493	let search_index = state.search_index.clone();
494	let vault_clone = vault_path.clone();
495	
496	let result = tauri::async_runtime::spawn_blocking(move || {
497		let guard = tauri::async_runtime::block_on(search_index.lock());
498		guard.clear()?;
499		let mut docs = Vec::new();
500		let mut seen_paths = Vec::new();
501		
502		for entry in WalkDir::new(&vault_clone).into_iter().filter_map(|e| e.ok()) {
503			let path = entry.path();
504			let rel_path = path.strip_prefix(&vault_clone).unwrap_or(path);
505			if is_hidden_or_special(rel_path) {
506				continue;
507			}
508			if !path.is_file() {
509				continue;
510			}
511			
512			let path_str = normalize_path(&path.to_string_lossy());
513			let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
514			let modified = metadata
515				.modified()
516				.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?
517				.duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
518				.unwrap_or_default()
519				.as_secs() as i64;
520			
521			let is_markdown = path.extension().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) == Some("md");
522			let title = path
523				.file_name()
524				.unwrap_or_default()
525				.to_string_lossy()
526				.to_string();
527			
528			if is_markdown {
529				if let Ok(content) = std::fs::read_to_string(path) {
530					let tags = crate::utils::extract_tags(&content);
531					let body = if let Some((_, _)) = crate::utils::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter(&content) {
532						crate::utils::frontmatter::strip_frontmatter(&content).to_string()
533					} else {
534						content.clone()
535					};
536					docs.push(crate::search::SearchDoc {
537						path: path_str.clone(),
538						title: title.trim_end_matches(".md").to_string(),
539						body,
540						tags,
541					});
542				}
543			} else {
544				docs.push(crate::search::SearchDoc {
545					path: path_str.clone(),
546					title,
547					body: String::new(),
548					tags: Vec::new(),
549				});
550			}
551			
552			seen_paths.push((path_str, modified, is_markdown));
553		}
554		
555		guard.index_batch(&docs, &[])?;
556		Ok::<(usize, Vec<(String, i64, bool)>), String>((docs.len(), seen_paths))
557	})
558		.await
559		.map_err(|e| TessellumError::Internal(format!("Rebuild task failed: {e}")))?
560		.map_err(|e| TessellumError::Internal(e))?;
561	
562	let (indexed_count, seen_paths) = result;
563	let db = state.db.clone();
564	let existing = db
565		.get_all_search_files()
566		.await
567		.map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
568	let existing_set: std::collections::HashSet<String> =
569		existing.into_iter().map(|(p, _, _)| p).collect();
570	let seen_set: std::collections::HashSet<String> =
571		seen_paths.iter().map(|(p, _, _)| p.clone()).collect();
572	let deleted: Vec<String> = existing_set
573		.difference(&seen_set)
574		.cloned()
575		.collect();
576	if !deleted.is_empty() {
577		db
578			.delete_search_files(&deleted)
579			.await
580			.map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
581	}
582	for (path, modified, is_md) in seen_paths {
583		db
584			.upsert_search_file(&path, modified, is_md)
585			.await
586			.map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
587	}
588	
589	Ok(IndexRebuildResult {
590		indexed_files: indexed_count,
591		deleted_files: 0,
592		duration_ms: 0,
593	})
594}
595
596fn make_relative_path(vault_path: &str, full_path: &str) -> String {
597	let vault_root = Path::new(vault_path);
598	let path = Path::new(full_path);
599	if let Ok(rel) = path.strip_prefix(vault_root) {
600		normalize_path(&rel.to_string_lossy())
601	} else {
602		normalize_path(full_path)
603	}
604}
605
606async fn read_snippet(path: &str, query: &str) -> Option<String> {
607	let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(path).await.ok()?;
608	let body = if let Some((_, _)) = crate::utils::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter(&content) {
609		crate::utils::frontmatter::strip_frontmatter(&content).to_string()
610	} else {
611		content
612	};
613	
614	let query_term = query.split_whitespace().next().unwrap_or("");
615	if query_term.is_empty() {
616		return None;
617	}
618	
619	let lower_body = body.to_lowercase();
620	let lower_query = query_term.to_lowercase();
621	if let Some(pos) = lower_body.find(&lower_query) {
622		let start = pos.saturating_sub(40);
623		let end = (pos + 120).min(body.len());
624		return Some(body[start..end].trim().to_string());
625	}
626	
627	Some(body.split_whitespace().take(24).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(" "))
628}
629
630#[cfg(test)]
631mod tests {
632	use super::{
633		count_mismatches_with_early_exit,
634		is_markdown_path,
635		mismatch_threshold,
636		needs_rebuild,
637		CoherenceResult,
638	};
639	use std::collections::HashSet;
640
641	#[test]
642	fn mismatch_threshold_uses_one_percent_floor_of_one() {
643		assert_eq!(mismatch_threshold(0), 1);
644		assert_eq!(mismatch_threshold(10), 1);
645		assert_eq!(mismatch_threshold(99), 1);
646		assert_eq!(mismatch_threshold(100), 1);
647		assert_eq!(mismatch_threshold(101), 2);
648		assert_eq!(mismatch_threshold(250), 3);
649	}
650
651	#[test]
652	fn mismatch_counter_short_circuits_at_threshold() {
653		let expected = HashSet::from([
654			"a.md".to_string(),
655			"b.md".to_string(),
656			"c.md".to_string(),
657			"d.md".to_string(),
658		]);
659		let indexed = HashSet::from(["a.md".to_string()]);
660		let mismatches = count_mismatches_with_early_exit(&expected, &indexed, 2);
661		assert_eq!(mismatches, 2);
662	}
663
664	#[test]
665	fn needs_rebuild_respects_threshold_for_markdown_entries() {
666		let below = CoherenceResult {
667			expected_markdown_count: 500,
668			indexed_markdown_count: 498,
669			mismatch_count: 4,
670			mismatch_threshold: 5,
671		};
672		let at_threshold = CoherenceResult {
673			expected_markdown_count: 500,
674			indexed_markdown_count: 495,
675			mismatch_count: 5,
676			mismatch_threshold: 5,
677		};
678		assert!(!needs_rebuild(&below));
679		assert!(needs_rebuild(&at_threshold));
680	}
681
682	#[test]
683	fn markdown_path_detection_is_case_insensitive() {
684		assert!(is_markdown_path("note.md"));
685		assert!(is_markdown_path("NOTE.MD"));
686		assert!(!is_markdown_path("asset.png"));
687	}
688}

Files

tessellum_lib\commands/
templates.rs

1use std::fs;
2use std::path::Path;
3
4use chrono::{DateTime, Local};
5use serde::Serialize;
6use tauri::State;
7
8use crate::error::TessellumError;
9use crate::models::AppState;
10use crate::utils::{normalize_path, sanitize_string, validate_path_in_vault};
11
12#[derive(Serialize)]
13pub struct TemplateInfo {
14	pub name: String,
15	pub path: String,
16}
17
18pub(crate) fn templates_dir(vault_path: &str) -> std::path::PathBuf {
19	Path::new(vault_path).join(".tessellum").join("templates")
20}
21
22pub(crate) fn apply_placeholders(
23	content: &str,
24	title: &str,
25	vault_path: &str,
26	now: DateTime<Local>,
27) -> String {
28	let date = now.format("%Y-%m-%d").to_string();
29	let time = now.format("%H:%M").to_string();
30	let datetime = now.format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M").to_string();
31	let vault = normalize_path(vault_path);
32	
33	content
34		.replace("{{date}}", &date)
35		.replace("{{time}}", &time)
36		.replace("{{datetime}}", &datetime)
37		.replace("{{title}}", title)
38		.replace("{{vault}}", &vault)
39}
40
41#[tauri::command]
42pub async fn list_templates(vault_path: String) -> Result<Vec<TemplateInfo>, TessellumError> {
43	validate_path_in_vault(&vault_path, &vault_path).map_err(TessellumError::Validation)?;
44	
45	let dir = templates_dir(&vault_path);
46	if !dir.exists() {
47		fs::create_dir_all(&dir).map_err(TessellumError::Io)?;
48	}
49	
50	let mut templates = Vec::new();
51	
52	for entry in fs::read_dir(&dir).map_err(TessellumError::Io)? {
53		let entry = match entry {
54			Ok(e) => e,
55			Err(e) => {
56				log::warn!("Failed to read templates entry: {}", e);
57				continue;
58			}
59		};
60		
61		let path = entry.path();
62		if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) != Some("md") {
63			continue;
64		}
65		
66		let name = match path.file_stem().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) {
67			Some(stem) => stem.to_string(),
68			None => continue,
69		};
70		
71		templates.push(TemplateInfo {
72			name,
73			path: normalize_path(&path.to_string_lossy()),
74		});
75	}
76	
77	templates.sort_by(|a, b| a.name.to_lowercase().cmp(&b.name.to_lowercase()));
78	
79	Ok(templates)
80}
81
82#[tauri::command]
83pub async fn create_note_from_template(
84	state: State<'_, AppState>,
85	vault_path: String,
86	target_dir: String,
87	template_path: String,
88	title: String,
89) -> Result<String, TessellumError> {
90	validate_path_in_vault(&vault_path, &vault_path).map_err(TessellumError::Validation)?;
91	validate_path_in_vault(&target_dir, &vault_path).map_err(TessellumError::Validation)?;
92	validate_path_in_vault(&template_path, &vault_path).map_err(TessellumError::Validation)?;
93	
94	let template_content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(&template_path)
95		.await
96		.map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
97	
98	let clean_title = sanitize_string(title);
99	if clean_title.trim().is_empty() {
100		return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
101			"Title cannot be empty".to_string(),
102		));
103	}
104	
105	let mut filename = if clean_title.to_lowercase().ends_with(".md") {
106		clean_title.clone()
107	} else {
108		format!("{}.md", clean_title)
109	};
110	let mut file_path = Path::new(&target_dir).join(&filename);
111	let mut collision_index = 1;
112	
113	while file_path.exists() {
114		let stem = clean_title.strip_suffix(".md").unwrap_or(&clean_title);
115		filename = format!("{} ({}).md", stem, collision_index);
116		file_path = Path::new(&target_dir).join(&filename);
117		collision_index += 1;
118	}
119	
120	let processed_content =
121		apply_placeholders(&template_content, &clean_title, &target_dir, Local::now());
122	
123	tokio::fs::write(&file_path, &processed_content)
124		.await
125		.map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
126	
127	let path_str = normalize_path(&file_path.to_string_lossy());
128	
129	let db = state.db.clone();
130	db
131		.index_file(&path_str, 0, 0, None, None, &[])
132		.await
133		.unwrap_or_else(|e| log::warn!("Failed to index new file: {}", e));
134	
135	let mut idx_guard = state.file_index.lock().await;
136	*idx_guard = None;
137	let mut asset_guard = state.asset_index.lock().await;
138	*asset_guard = None;
139	
140	Ok(path_str)
141}
142
143#[cfg(test)]
144mod tests {
145	use super::apply_placeholders;
146	use chrono::{Local, TimeZone};
147	
148	#[test]
149	fn test_apply_placeholders_replaces_core_tokens() {
150		let now = Local.with_ymd_and_hms(2026, 3, 11, 14, 5, 0).unwrap();
151		let content = "Date: {{date}}\nTime: {{time}}\nDT: {{datetime}}\nTitle: {{title}}\nVault: {{vault}}";
152		let out = apply_placeholders(content, "My Note", "C:\\Vault", now);
153		
154		assert!(out.contains("Date: 2026-03-11"));
155		assert!(out.contains("Time: 14:05"));
156		assert!(out.contains("DT: 2026-03-11 14:05"));
157		assert!(out.contains("Title: My Note"));
158		assert!(out.contains("Vault: C:/Vault"));
159	}
160	
161	#[test]
162	fn test_apply_placeholders_leaves_unknown_tokens() {
163		let now = Local.with_ymd_and_hms(2026, 3, 11, 14, 5, 0).unwrap();
164		let content = "Hello {{unknown}} {{date}}";
165		let out = apply_placeholders(content, "X", "/vault", now);
166		
167		assert!(out.contains("{{unknown}}"));
168		assert!(out.contains("2026-03-11"));
169	}
170	
171	#[test]
172	fn test_apply_placeholders_multiple_occurrences() {
173		let now = Local.with_ymd_and_hms(2026, 3, 11, 14, 5, 0).unwrap();
174		let content = "{{date}} {{date}} {{time}} {{time}}";
175		let out = apply_placeholders(content, "X", "/vault", now);
176		
177		assert_eq!(out, "2026-03-11 2026-03-11 14:05 14:05");
178	}
179}
180
181
vault.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib\commands/
vault.rs

1use std::fs::metadata;
2use std::path::Path;
3use std::time::UNIX_EPOCH;
4use tauri_plugin_fs::FsExt;
5use walkdir::WalkDir;
6
7use crate::error::TessellumError;
8use crate::grafeo_projection::ManagedGrafeoConnection;
9use crate::models::FileMetadata;
10use crate::search::SearchDoc;
11use crate::trash::purge_expired_trash;
12use crate::utils::{extract_tags, is_hidden_or_special, sanitize_string, validate_path_in_vault};
13
14const FEATURE_DEMO_FILENAME: &str = "FEATURE_DEMO.md";
15const FEATURE_DEMO_CONTENT: &str = include_str!("../../../FEATURE_DEMO.md");
16
17/// Rewrite `[[OldStem]]` and `[[OldStem|alias]]` to `[[NewStem]]` / `[[NewStem|alias]]`
18/// in all files whose paths are listed in `backlinks`.
19/// Escaped links (`\[[OldStem]]`) are left unchanged.
20async fn rewrite_backlinks(
21    backlinks: &[String],
22    old_stem: &str,
23    new_stem: &str,
24) -> Result<(), TessellumError> {
25    if backlinks.is_empty() {
26        return Ok(());
27    }
28    
29    // Build a regex that matches [[OldStem]] and [[OldStem|alias]],
30    // with an optional leading backslash to detect escaped links.
31    let escaped = regex::escape(old_stem);
32    let pattern = format!(r"(\\?)\[\[{escaped}(\|[^\]]+)?\]\]");
33    let re = regex::Regex::new(&pattern)
34        .map_err(|e| TessellumError::Internal(format!("Link-rewrite regex error: {e}")))?;
35    
36    for source_path in backlinks {
37        let content = match tokio::fs::read_to_string(source_path).await {
38            Ok(c) => c,
39            Err(e) => {
40                log::warn!("rewrite_backlinks: could not read '{source_path}': {e}");
41                continue;
42            }
43        };
44        
45        let new_content = re.replace_all(&content, |caps: &regex::Captures<'_>| {
46            // If preceded by a backslash, the link is escaped â€” leave it
47            // verbatim.
48            if caps.get(1).map_or(false, |m| m.as_str() == "\\") {
49                return caps[0].to_string();
50            }
51            let alias = caps.get(2).map_or("", |m| m.as_str());
52            format!("[[{new_stem}{alias}]]")
53        });
54        
55        if new_content != content {
56            if let Err(e) = tokio::fs::write(source_path, new_content.as_bytes()).await {
57                log::warn!("rewrite_backlinks: could not write '{source_path}': {e}");
58            }
59        }
60    }
61    
62    Ok(())
63}
64
65fn derive_renamed_filename(old_path: &Path, clean_name: &str) -> String {
66    if old_path.is_dir() {
67        return clean_name.to_string();
68    }
69
70    // If the user typed an explicit extension, respect it.
71    if Path::new(clean_name).extension().is_some() {
72        return clean_name.to_string();
73    }
74
75    // Otherwise preserve the original file extension (e.g. .md, .png, .pdf).
76    if let Some(ext) = old_path.extension().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) {
77        return format!("{}.{}", clean_name, ext);
78    }
79
80    clean_name.to_string()
81}
82
83/// Lists all files and directories within the specified vault path and retrieves their metadata.
84///
85/// # Arguments
86///
87/// * `vault_path` - A `String` specifying the path of the directory to scan.
88///
89/// # Returns
90///
91/// * `Ok(Vec<FileMetadata>)` containing a vector of `FileMetadata` structs.
92/// * `Err(String)` containing an error message if the vault path does not exist.
93#[tauri::command]
94pub fn list_files(vault_path: String) -> Result<Vec<FileMetadata>, TessellumError> {
95    let mut files = Vec::new();
96    
97    // Check if path exists
98    if !Path::new(&vault_path).exists() {
99        return Err(TessellumError::NotFound(
100            "Vault path does not exist".to_string(),
101        ));
102    }
103    
104    // For each entry in the vault directory that does not give an error, add it to the list
105    for entry in WalkDir::new(&vault_path)
106        .min_depth(1)
107        .into_iter()
108        .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
109    {
110        // Get the file path
111        let path = entry.path();
112        let path_str = path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
113        
114        // Ignore hidden files/dirs (.git, .trash, etc.)
115        if is_hidden_or_special(path) {
116            continue;
117        }
118        
119        // If able to get metadata, add it to the list
120        if let Ok(meta) = metadata(path) {
121            // Get the last modified time in milliseconds
122            let modified_time = meta
123                .modified()
124                .unwrap_or(UNIX_EPOCH)
125                .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
126                .unwrap_or_default()
127                .as_millis() as i64;
128            
129            // Push the file metadata to the list
130            files.push(FileMetadata {
131                path: crate::utils::normalize_path(&path_str),
132                filename: path
133                    .file_name()
134                    .unwrap_or_default()
135                    .to_string_lossy()
136                    .to_string(),
137                is_dir: meta.is_dir(),
138                size: meta.len(),
139                last_modified: modified_time,
140            });
141        }
142    }
143    
144    Ok(files)
145}
146
147#[tauri::command]
148pub async fn ensure_feature_demo_in_empty_vault(vault_path: String) -> Result<bool, TessellumError> {
149    let vault = Path::new(&vault_path);
150    if !vault.exists() || !vault.is_dir() {
151        return Err(TessellumError::NotFound(
152            "Vault path does not exist".to_string(),
153        ));
154    }
155
156    if !list_files(vault_path.clone())?.is_empty() {
157        return Ok(false);
158    }
159
160    let demo_path = vault.join(FEATURE_DEMO_FILENAME);
161    if demo_path.exists() {
162        return Ok(false);
163    }
164
165    tokio::fs::write(demo_path, FEATURE_DEMO_CONTENT)
166        .await
167        .map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
168
169    Ok(true)
170}
171
172/// Asynchronous Tauri command to rename a file or folder.
173///
174/// # Parameters
175/// - `vault_path`: The root vault path for security validation.
176/// - `old_path`: The current path of the item to be renamed.
177/// - `new_name`: The new name for the item.
178///
179/// # Returns
180/// - `Ok(String)`: The new path of the renamed item.
181/// - `Err(String)`: An error message if the operation fails.
182#[tauri::command]
183pub async fn rename_file(
184    state: tauri::State<'_, crate::models::AppState>,
185    _grafeo_state: tauri::State<'_, ManagedGrafeoConnection>,
186    vault_path: String,
187    old_path: String,
188    new_name: String,
189) -> Result<String, TessellumError> {
190    // Validate old_path is inside the vault (using canonicalize to prevent traversal)
191    validate_path_in_vault(&old_path, &vault_path).map_err(|e| TessellumError::Validation(e))?;
192    
193    let vault_root = Path::new(&vault_path);
194    let old = Path::new(&old_path);
195    
196    let parent = old.parent().ok_or_else(|| {
197        TessellumError::Validation("Invalid path: No parent directory".to_string())
198    })?;
199    
200    let clean_name = sanitize_string(new_name);
201    
202    if clean_name.trim().is_empty() {
203        return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
204            "Invalid name: Filename cannot be empty".to_string(),
205        ));
206    }
207    
208    // Check before the rename while the path still exists on disk
209    let is_file = old.is_file();
210    
211    let final_filename = derive_renamed_filename(old, &clean_name);
212    
213    let new_path = parent.join(&final_filename);
214    
215    // Validate destination is also inside the vault
216    let vault_canonical = vault_root
217        .canonicalize()
218        .map_err(|_| TessellumError::Validation("Invalid vault path".to_string()))?;
219    let new_canonical = new_path
220        .parent()
221        .ok_or_else(|| TessellumError::Validation("Invalid path: No parent directory".to_string()))?
222        .canonicalize()
223        .map_err(|_| TessellumError::Validation("Invalid destination path".to_string()))?
224        .join(&final_filename);
225    if !new_canonical.starts_with(&vault_canonical) {
226        return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
227            "Security Error: Cannot rename file to outside the vault".to_string(),
228        ));
229    }
230    
231    if new_path.exists() {
232        return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
233            "A file or folder with that name already exists".to_string(),
234        ));
235    }
236    
237    // Capture stems before the rename (path no longer exists on disk after)
238    let old_stem = old.file_stem().and_then(|s| s.to_str()).map(str::to_string);
239    let new_stem = new_path
240        .file_stem()
241        .and_then(|s| s.to_str())
242        .map(str::to_string);
243    
244    // Rename on the filesystem
245    tokio::fs::rename(old, &new_path)
246        .await
247        .map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
248    
249    let db = state.db.clone();
250    
251    // Rewrite [[OldStem]] -> [[NewStem]] in all files that link to this note
252    if is_file {
253        if let (Some(os), Some(ns)) = (&old_stem, &new_stem) {
254            if os != ns {
255                let backlinks = db
256                    .get_backlinks(&old_path)
257                    .await
258                    .map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
259                
260                rewrite_backlinks(&backlinks, os, ns).await?;
261            }
262        }
263    }
264    
265    // Update the DB index so backlinks and graph stay correct
266    db
267        .update_file_path(&old_path, &new_path.to_string_lossy())
268        .await
269        .map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
270    db
271        .update_search_file_path(&old_path, &new_path.to_string_lossy())
272        .await
273        .map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
274
275    // Note: Grafeo sync happens automatically via file watcher/write_file command
276    // No need for full sync on individual file rename
277
278    // Invalidate the cache since path has changed
279    let mut idx_guard = state.file_index.lock().await;
280    *idx_guard = None;
281    let mut asset_guard = state.asset_index.lock().await;
282    *asset_guard = None;
283    
284    if is_file {
285        let search_index = state.search_index.clone();
286        let old_path = old_path.clone();
287        let new_path = new_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
288        tauri::async_runtime::spawn_blocking(move || {
289            let guard = tauri::async_runtime::block_on(search_index.lock());
290            let _ = guard.delete_path(&old_path);
291            if let Ok(content) = std::fs::read_to_string(&new_path) {
292                let title = Path::new(&new_path)
293                    .file_name()
294                    .unwrap_or_default()
295                    .to_string_lossy()
296                    .to_string()
297                    .trim_end_matches(".md")
298                    .to_string();
299                let tags = extract_tags(&content);
300                let body = if let Some((_, _)) = crate::utils::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter(&content) {
301                    crate::utils::frontmatter::strip_frontmatter(&content).to_string()
302                } else {
303                    content
304                };
305                let doc = SearchDoc {
306                    path: crate::utils::normalize_path(&new_path),
307                    title,
308                    body,
309                    tags,
310                };
311                let _ = guard.index_batch(&[doc], &[]);
312            }
313        });
314    }
315    
316    Ok(new_path.to_string_lossy().to_string())
317}
318
319#[tauri::command]
320pub async fn move_items(
321    state: tauri::State<'_, crate::models::AppState>,
322    _grafeo_state: tauri::State<'_, ManagedGrafeoConnection>,
323    vault_path: String,
324    item_paths: Vec<String>,
325    dest_dir: String,
326) -> Result<Vec<String>, TessellumError> {
327    if item_paths.is_empty() {
328        return Ok(Vec::new());
329    }
330    
331    validate_path_in_vault(&dest_dir, &vault_path)
332        .map_err(|e| TessellumError::Validation(e))?;
333    
334    let dest_path = Path::new(&dest_dir);
335    let dest_meta = tokio::fs::metadata(dest_path).await.map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
336    if !dest_meta.is_dir() {
337        return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
338            "Destination must be a folder".to_string(),
339        ));
340    }
341    
342    let normalized_dest = crate::utils::normalize_path(&dest_dir);
343    
344    let mut planned: Vec<(String, String)> = Vec::new();
345    let mut seen_targets: std::collections::HashSet<String> = std::collections::HashSet::new();
346    
347    for item_path in item_paths {
348        validate_path_in_vault(&item_path, &vault_path)
349            .map_err(|e| TessellumError::Validation(e))?;
350        
351        let normalized_item = crate::utils::normalize_path(&item_path);
352        if normalized_dest == normalized_item
353            || normalized_dest.starts_with(&(normalized_item.clone() + "/"))
354        {
355            return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
356                "Cannot move a folder into itself".to_string(),
357            ));
358        }
359        
360        let old_path = Path::new(&item_path);
361        let file_name = old_path.file_name().ok_or_else(|| {
362            TessellumError::Validation("Invalid path: no filename".to_string())
363        })?;
364        let new_path = dest_path.join(file_name);
365        
366        let new_path_str = new_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
367        let normalized_new = crate::utils::normalize_path(&new_path_str);
368        if normalized_new == normalized_item {
369            continue;
370        }
371        
372        if !seen_targets.insert(normalized_new.clone()) {
373            return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
374                "Multiple items share the same name in the destination".to_string(),
375            ));
376        }
377        
378        if new_path.exists() {
379            return Err(TessellumError::Validation(
380                "A file or folder with that name already exists in the destination".to_string(),
381            ));
382        }
383        
384        planned.push((item_path, new_path_str));
385    }
386    
387    for (old_path, new_path) in planned.iter() {
388        tokio::fs::rename(old_path, new_path)
389            .await
390            .map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
391    }
392    
393    let db = state.db.clone();
394    for (old_path, new_path) in planned.iter() {
395        db
396            .update_file_path(old_path, new_path)
397            .await
398            .map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
399        db
400            .update_search_file_path(old_path, new_path)
401            .await
402            .map_err(TessellumError::from)?;
403    }
404
405    // Note: Grafeo sync happens automatically via file watcher/write_file command
406    // No need for full sync on batch file move
407
408    let search_index = state.search_index.clone();
409    let planned_files = planned.clone();
410    tauri::async_runtime::spawn_blocking(move || {
411        let guard = tauri::async_runtime::block_on(search_index.lock());
412        for (old_path, new_path) in planned_files {
413            let _ = guard.delete_path(&old_path);
414            if !Path::new(&new_path).is_file() {
415                continue;
416            }
417            if let Ok(content) = std::fs::read_to_string(&new_path) {
418                let title = Path::new(&new_path)
419                    .file_name()
420                    .unwrap_or_default()
421                    .to_string_lossy()
422                    .to_string()
423                    .trim_end_matches(".md")
424                    .to_string();
425                let tags = extract_tags(&content);
426                let body = if let Some((_, _)) = crate::utils::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter(&content) {
427                    crate::utils::frontmatter::strip_frontmatter(&content).to_string()
428                } else {
429                    content
430                };
431                let doc = SearchDoc {
432                    path: crate::utils::normalize_path(&new_path),
433                    title,
434                    body,
435                    tags,
436                };
437                let _ = guard.index_batch(&[doc], &[]);
438            }
439        }
440    });
441    
442    let mut idx_guard = state.file_index.lock().await;
443    *idx_guard = None;
444    let mut asset_guard = state.asset_index.lock().await;
445    *asset_guard = None;
446    
447    Ok(planned.into_iter().map(|(_, new_path)| new_path).collect())
448}
449use serde::Serialize;
450use std::collections::HashMap;
451use tauri::Manager;
452
453#[derive(Serialize, Clone)]
454pub struct TreeNode {
455    pub id: String,
456    pub name: String,
457    pub is_dir: bool,
458    pub children: Vec<TreeNode>,
459    pub file: Option<FileMetadata>,
460}
461
462#[tauri::command]
463pub fn list_files_tree(vault_path: String) -> Result<Vec<TreeNode>, TessellumError> {
464    let files = list_files(vault_path.clone())?;
465    
466    let mut tree_nodes: HashMap<String, TreeNode> = HashMap::new();
467    
468    // First, map all items
469    for file in files {
470        let normalized = crate::utils::normalize_path(&file.path);
471        tree_nodes.insert(
472            normalized.clone(),
473            TreeNode {
474                id: normalized,
475                name: file.filename.clone(),
476                is_dir: file.is_dir,
477                children: Vec::new(),
478                file: Some(file),
479            },
480        );
481    }
482    
483    let mut paths: Vec<String> = tree_nodes.keys().cloned().collect();
484    // Sort paths by length descending so that deep children are processed before their parents.
485    paths.sort_by_key(|a| std::cmp::Reverse(a.len()));
486    
487    let mut root_nodes = Vec::new();
488    
489    for path in paths {
490        let node = tree_nodes.remove(&path).unwrap();
491        
492        let parent_path = crate::utils::normalize_path(
493            &Path::new(&path)
494                .parent()
495                .unwrap_or(Path::new(""))
496                .to_string_lossy(),
497        );
498        
499        if let Some(parent) = tree_nodes.get_mut(&parent_path) {
500            parent.children.push(node);
501        } else {
502            root_nodes.push(node);
503        }
504    }
505    
506    // Recursive sort lambda-like equivalent function
507    fn sort_nodes(nodes: &mut Vec<TreeNode>) {
508        nodes.sort_by(|a, b| {
509            if a.is_dir == b.is_dir {
510                a.name.cmp(&b.name)
511            } else if a.is_dir {
512                std::cmp::Ordering::Less
513            } else {
514                std::cmp::Ordering::Greater
515            }
516        });
517        
518        for child in nodes.iter_mut() {
519            sort_nodes(&mut child.children);
520        }
521    }
522    
523    sort_nodes(&mut root_nodes);
524    
525    Ok(root_nodes)
526}
527
528#[tauri::command]
529pub fn set_vault_path(app: tauri::AppHandle, path: String) -> Result<(), String> {
530    let path = std::path::PathBuf::from(&path);
531    
532    app.asset_protocol_scope().
533        allow_directory(&path, true)
534        .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
535    
536    app.fs_scope()
537        .allow_directory(&path, true)
538        .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
539    
540    spawn_trash_retention_cleanup(path);
541    
542    Ok(())
543}
544
545fn spawn_trash_retention_cleanup(vault_path: std::path::PathBuf) {
546    tauri::async_runtime::spawn_blocking(move || {
547        let report = purge_expired_trash(&vault_path.to_string_lossy(), 30);
548        if report.deleted > 0 || report.skipped_invalid_name > 0 || report.errors > 0 {
549            log::info!(
550                "Trash retention cleanup for '{}': deleted={}, skipped_invalid_name={}, errors={}",
551                vault_path.display(),
552                report.deleted,
553                report.skipped_invalid_name,
554                report.errors
555            );
556        }
557    });
558}
559
560#[cfg(test)]
561mod tests {
562    use super::derive_renamed_filename;
563    use super::spawn_trash_retention_cleanup;
564    use std::fs;
565    use std::path::Path;
566    use std::thread;
567    use std::time::Duration;
568    use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};
569    use tempfile::tempdir;
570    
571    #[test]
572    fn startup_cleanup_task_deletes_expired_top_level_trash_entry() {
573        let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
574        let vault = temp.path().to_path_buf();
575        let trash = vault.join(".trash");
576        fs::create_dir_all(&trash).unwrap();
577        
578        let now_ms = SystemTime::now()
579            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
580            .unwrap_or_default()
581            .as_millis();
582        let expired_ts = now_ms.saturating_sub(31_u128 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
583        let expired_file = trash.join(format!("Expired (Root) {}.md", expired_ts));
584        fs::write(&expired_file, "old").unwrap();
585        
586        spawn_trash_retention_cleanup(vault);
587        
588        // The cleanup task is async; poll briefly for completion.
589        let mut deleted = false;
590        for _ in 0..20 {
591            if !expired_file.exists() {
592                deleted = true;
593                break;
594            }
595            thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(25));
596        }
597        
598        assert!(deleted, "expected startup cleanup to remove expired trash file");
599    }
600
601    #[test]
602    fn derive_renamed_filename_preserves_existing_extension_when_missing() {
603        let media_old = Path::new("C:/vault/assets/cover.png");
604        let note_old = Path::new("C:/vault/Note.md");
605
606        assert_eq!(derive_renamed_filename(media_old, "hero"), "hero.png");
607        assert_eq!(derive_renamed_filename(note_old, "Renamed Note"), "Renamed Note.md");
608    }
609
610    #[test]
611    fn derive_renamed_filename_respects_explicit_extension() {
612        let media_old = Path::new("C:/vault/assets/cover.png");
613        assert_eq!(derive_renamed_filename(media_old, "hero.webp"), "hero.webp");
614    }
615
616    #[test]
617    fn integration_rename_media_without_extension_preserves_original_ext() {
618        let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
619        let old_path = temp.path().join("cover.png");
620        fs::write(&old_path, b"png").unwrap();
621
622        let final_name = derive_renamed_filename(&old_path, "hero");
623        let new_path = temp.path().join(final_name);
624        fs::rename(&old_path, &new_path).unwrap();
625
626        assert!(!old_path.exists());
627        assert!(new_path.exists());
628        assert_eq!(new_path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()), Some("hero.png"));
629    }
630
631    #[test]
632    fn integration_rename_markdown_without_extension_keeps_md() {
633        let temp = tempdir().unwrap();
634        let old_path = temp.path().join("Old.md");
635        fs::write(&old_path, b"# note").unwrap();
636
637        let final_name = derive_renamed_filename(&old_path, "Renamed");
638        let new_path = temp.path().join(final_name);
639        fs::rename(&old_path, &new_path).unwrap();
640
641        assert!(!old_path.exists());
642        assert!(new_path.exists());
643        assert_eq!(new_path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()), Some("Renamed.md"));
644    }
645}

watcher.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib\commands/
watcher.rs

1use notify::{Config, Error, Event, RecommendedWatcher, RecursiveMode, Watcher};
2use std::path::Path;
3use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
4use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
5use tauri::{AppHandle, Emitter, State};
6
7use crate::error::TessellumError;
8use crate::models::AppState;
9
10/// Debounce window: ignore events within this duration of the last emit.
11const DEBOUNCE_MS: u64 = 200;
12
13fn should_emit_change(last_emit: &mut Instant, now: Instant) -> bool {
14    if now.duration_since(*last_emit) < Duration::from_millis(DEBOUNCE_MS) {
15        return false;
16    }
17
18    *last_emit = now;
19    true
20}
21
22/// Watches a directory and emits a debounced event to the frontend whenever
23/// a file within the directory changes.
24///
25/// This function initializes a file system watcher for the specified directory (`vault_path`) and listens for changes
26/// such as file creation, modification, or deletion. Upon detecting a change, the function emits a `file-changed`
27/// event to the frontend, debounced to prevent event flooding.
28#[tauri::command]
29pub async fn watch_vault(
30    vault_path: String,
31    handle: AppHandle,
32    state: State<'_, AppState>,
33) -> Result<(), TessellumError> {
34    // Initialize or replace the watcher so vault switching and dev reloads
35    // do not keep stale watchers alive.
36    let mut watcher_guard = state.watcher.lock().await;
37    *watcher_guard = None;
38
39    let app_handle_clone = handle.clone();
40    let file_index_clone = state.file_index.clone();
41    let asset_index_clone = state.asset_index.clone();
42    let notify_config = Config::default();
43    let last_emit = Arc::new(Mutex::new(
44        Instant::now() - Duration::from_millis(DEBOUNCE_MS),
45    ));
46
47    let mut watcher = RecommendedWatcher::new(
48        move |res: Result<Event, Error>| {
49            match res {
50                Ok(_) => {
51                    // Debounce: only emit if enough time has passed
52                    let mut last = last_emit.lock().unwrap();
53                    let now = Instant::now();
54                    if should_emit_change(&mut last, now) {
55
56                        // Invalidate caches
57                        let fi = file_index_clone.clone();
58                        let ai = asset_index_clone.clone();
59                        tauri::async_runtime::spawn(async move {
60                            let mut guard = fi.lock().await;
61                            *guard = None;
62                            let mut asset_guard = ai.lock().await;
63                            *asset_guard = None;
64                        });
65                        let _ = app_handle_clone.emit("file-changed", ());
66                    }
67                }
68                Err(e) => log::error!("watch error: {:?}", e),
69            }
70        },
71        notify_config,
72    )
73        .map_err(|e| TessellumError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
74
75    watcher
76        .watch(Path::new(&vault_path), RecursiveMode::Recursive)
77        .map_err(|e| TessellumError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
78
79    *watcher_guard = Some(watcher);
80
81    Ok(())
82}
83
84#[tauri::command]
85pub async fn unwatch_vault(state: State<'_, AppState>) -> Result<(), TessellumError> {
86    let mut watcher_guard = state.watcher.lock().await;
87    *watcher_guard = None;
88    Ok(())
89}
90
91#[cfg(test)]
92mod tests {
93    use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
94
95    use super::should_emit_change;
96
97    #[test]
98    fn emits_when_the_debounce_window_has_elapsed() {
99        let base = Instant::now();
100        let mut last_emit = base - Duration::from_millis(250);
101
102        let emitted = should_emit_change(&mut last_emit, base);
103
104        assert!(emitted);
105        assert_eq!(last_emit, base);
106    }
107
108    #[test]
109    fn suppresses_events_inside_the_debounce_window() {
110        let base = Instant::now();
111        let mut last_emit = base - Duration::from_millis(50);
112
113        let emitted = should_emit_change(&mut last_emit, base);
114
115        assert!(!emitted);
116        assert!(base.duration_since(last_emit) < Duration::from_millis(200));
117    }
118}

Files

tessellum_lib\models/
app_state.rs

1use notify::RecommendedWatcher;
2use std::sync::Arc;
3use tokio::sync::Mutex;
4
5use crate::db::Database;
6use crate::models::{AssetIndex, FileIndex};
7use crate::search::SearchIndex;
8
9#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
10pub enum SearchReadinessStatus {
11    Idle,
12    Warming,
13    Ready,
14    Failed,
15}
16
17#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
18pub struct SearchReadinessState {
19    pub status: SearchReadinessStatus,
20    pub attempt_count: u32,
21    pub max_attempts: u32,
22    pub last_error: Option<String>,
23    pub vault_path: Option<String>,
24}
25
26impl Default for SearchReadinessState {
27    fn default() -> Self {
28        Self {
29            status: SearchReadinessStatus::Idle,
30            attempt_count: 0,
31            max_attempts: 10,
32            last_error: None,
33            vault_path: None,
34        }
35    }
36}
37
38/// Represents the application state that contains shared resources such as
39/// a file watcher and a database connection.
40///
41/// # Fields
42///
43/// * `watcher` - A thread-safe, optional wrapper around a `RecommendedWatcher` instance.
44///   This watcher is typically used for monitoring file system events.
45///   It is wrapped in a `Mutex` to ensure safe concurrent access across threads.
46///
47/// * `db` - A thread-safe, optional shared reference to a `Database` instance.
48///   The `Database` is wrapped in an `Arc` for shared ownership across threads.
49///   Internally, sqlx handles concurrent access through its connection pool.
50///
51/// * `file_index` - Cached FileIndex to resolve links quickly without traversing the FS.
52/// * `asset_index` - Cached AssetIndex for media embeds.
53pub struct AppState {
54    pub watcher: tokio::sync::Mutex<Option<RecommendedWatcher>>,
55    pub db: Arc<Database>,
56    pub file_index: Arc<Mutex<Option<FileIndex>>>,
57    pub asset_index: Arc<Mutex<Option<AssetIndex>>>,
58    pub search_index: Arc<Mutex<SearchIndex>>,
59    pub search_readiness: Mutex<SearchReadinessState>,
60}
61
62impl AppState {
63    pub fn new(db: Database, search_index: SearchIndex) -> Self {
64        Self {
65            db: Arc::new(db),
66            watcher: tokio::sync::Mutex::new(None),
67            file_index: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
68            asset_index: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
69            search_index: Arc::new(Mutex::new(search_index)),
70            search_readiness: Mutex::new(SearchReadinessState::default()),
71        }
72    }
73}
