tessellum_lib\models/
asset_index.rs

1use std::collections::HashMap;
2use std::fs::canonicalize;
3use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
4use walkdir::WalkDir;
5
6use crate::utils::is_hidden_or_special;
7
8const SUPPORTED_EXTS: &[&str] = &[
9	"png", "jpg", "jpeg", "gif", "webp", "svg", "bmp", "tif", "tiff", "avif", "pdf",
10];
11
12#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
13pub struct AssetIndex {
14	name_to_paths: HashMap<String, Vec<PathBuf>>,
15}
16
17impl AssetIndex {
18	pub fn build(vault_path: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {
19		let mut name_to_paths: HashMap<String, Vec<PathBuf>> = HashMap::new();
20		
21		if !Path::new(vault_path).exists() {
22			return Err("Vault path does not exist".to_string());
23		}
24		
25		for entry in WalkDir::new(vault_path).into_iter().filter_map(|e| e.ok()) {
26			let path = entry.path();
27			let rel_path = path.strip_prefix(vault_path).unwrap_or(path);
28			if is_hidden_or_special(rel_path) {
29				continue;
30			}
31			
32			if path.is_file() {
33				let ext = path
34					.extension()
35					.and_then(|s| s.to_str())
36					.unwrap_or("")
37					.to_lowercase();
38				if !SUPPORTED_EXTS.contains(&ext.as_str()) {
39					continue;
40				}
41				
42				if let Some(filename) = path.file_name() {
43					let filename_str = filename.to_string_lossy().to_string();
44					name_to_paths
45						.entry(filename_str.clone())
46						.or_insert_with(Vec::new)
47						.push(path.to_path_buf());
48					
49					if let Some(stem) = path.file_stem() {
50						let stem_str = stem.to_string_lossy().to_string();
51						name_to_paths
52							.entry(stem_str)
53							.or_insert_with(Vec::new)
54							.push(path.to_path_buf());
55					}
56				}
57			}
58		}
59		
60		Ok(Self { name_to_paths })
61	}
62	
63	pub fn resolve(&self, vault_path: &str, link_target: &str) -> Option<PathBuf> {
64		let vault_root = Path::new(vault_path);
65		let canonical_vault_root = canonicalize(vault_root).ok()?;
66		
67		// Reject absolute link targets so they cannot escape the vault via
68		// join.
69		if Path::new(link_target).is_absolute() {
70			return None;
71		}
72		
73		if link_target.contains('/') || link_target.contains('\\') {
74			let mut full_path = vault_root.join(link_target);
75			if full_path.exists() {
76				return Some(full_path);
77			}
78			
79			if full_path.extension().is_none() {
80				for ext in SUPPORTED_EXTS {
81					full_path.set_extension(ext);
82					if full_path.exists() {
83						if let Ok(canonical_full) = canonicalize(&full_path) {
84							return if canonical_full.starts_with
85							(&canonical_vault_root) {
86								Some(full_path)
87							} else {
88								None
89							}
90						}
91					}
92				}
93			}
94			
95			if let Some(filename) = Path::new(link_target).file_name() {
96				let filename_str = filename.to_string_lossy().to_string();
97				if let Some(candidates) = self.name_to_paths.get(&filename_str) {
98					let matching: Vec<_> = candidates
99						.iter()
100						.filter(|p| {
101							if let Ok(rel) = p.strip_prefix(vault_root) {
102								rel.to_string_lossy().contains(link_target)
103							} else {
104								false
105							}
106						})
107						.collect();
108					
109					if !matching.is_empty() {
110						let candidate = matching[0];
111						if let Ok(canonical_candidate) = canonicalize(candidate) {
112							return if canonical_candidate.starts_with
113							(&canonical_vault_root) {
114								Some(candidate.clone())
115							} else {
116								 None
117							}
118						}
119					}
120				}
121			}
122		}
123		
124		if let Some(candidates) = self.name_to_paths.get(link_target) {
125			if candidates.is_empty() {
126				return None;
127			}
128			
129			let best_match = candidates.iter().min_by_key(|p| {
130				p.strip_prefix(vault_root)
131					.ok()
132					.map(|rel| rel.components().count())
133					.unwrap_or(usize::MAX)
134			})?;
135			
136			if let Ok(canonical_best) = canonicalize(best_match) {
137				return if canonical_best.starts_with(&canonical_vault_root) {
138					Some(best_match.clone())
139				} else {
140					None
141				};
142			}
143		}
144		
145		None
146	}
147}
file_index.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib\models/
file_index.rs

1use std::collections::HashMap;
2use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
3use walkdir::WalkDir;
4
5use crate::utils::is_hidden_or_special;
6
7/// In-memory index of files in the vault for fast wikilink resolution.
8#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
9pub struct FileIndex {
10    /// Map: filename -> Vec<full_path>
11    name_to_paths: HashMap<String, Vec<PathBuf>>,
12}
13
14impl FileIndex {
15    /// Build an index from a vault directory
16    pub fn build(vault_path: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {
17        let mut name_to_paths: HashMap<String, Vec<PathBuf>> = HashMap::new();
18        
19        if !Path::new(vault_path).exists() {
20            return Err("Vault path does not exist".to_string());
21        }
22        
23        for entry in WalkDir::new(vault_path).into_iter().filter_map(|e| e.ok()) {
24            let path = entry.path();
25            
26            // Skip hidden files and directories (.git, .trash, etc.)
27            let rel_path = path.strip_prefix(vault_path).unwrap_or(path);
28            if is_hidden_or_special(rel_path) {
29                continue;
30            }
31            
32            // Only index .md files
33            if path.is_file() && path.extension().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) == Some("md") {
34                if let Some(filename) = path.file_name() {
35                    let filename_str = filename.to_string_lossy().to_string();
36                    
37                    // Index both with and without .md extension
38                    name_to_paths
39                        .entry(filename_str.clone())
40                        .or_insert_with(Vec::new)
41                        .push(path.to_path_buf());
42                    
43                    // Also index without extension
44                    if let Some(stem) = path.file_stem() {
45                        let stem_str = stem.to_string_lossy().to_string();
46                        name_to_paths
47                            .entry(stem_str)
48                            .or_insert_with(Vec::new)
49                            .push(path.to_path_buf());
50                    }
51                }
52            }
53        }
54        
55        Ok(Self { name_to_paths })
56    }
57    
58    /// Resolve a wikilink target to a full file path.
59    /// Returns the best match based on Obsidian's resolution rules:
60    /// 1. If the link contains a path (e.g., "folder/Note"), try to match that structure
61    /// 2. If multiple files have the same name, prefer the shortest path (closest to root)
62    /// 3. Return None if no match is found
63    pub fn resolve(&self, vault_path: &str, link_target: &str) -> Option<PathBuf> {
64        let vault_root = Path::new(vault_path);
65        
66        // Check if this is a path-based link (contains /)
67        if link_target.contains('/') {
68            // Try to resolve as a relative path from vault root
69            let mut full_path = vault_root.join(link_target);
70            
71            // Add .md extension if not present
72            if !full_path.extension().map_or(false, |ext| ext == "md") {
73                full_path.set_extension("md");
74            }
75            
76            if full_path.exists() {
77                return Some(full_path);
78            }
79            
80            // Also try matching the filename part only
81            if let Some(filename) = Path::new(link_target).file_name() {
82                let filename_str = filename.to_string_lossy().to_string();
83                if let Some(candidates) = self.name_to_paths.get(&filename_str) {
84                    // Filter candidates that end with the specified path structure
85                    let matching: Vec<_> = candidates
86                        .iter()
87                        .filter(|p| {
88                            if let Ok(rel) = p.strip_prefix(vault_root) {
89                                rel.to_string_lossy().contains(link_target)
90                            } else {
91                                false
92                            }
93                        })
94                        .collect();
95                    
96                    if !matching.is_empty() {
97                        return Some(matching[0].clone());
98                    }
99                }
100            }
101        }
102        
103        // Simple filename lookup
104        let search_key = if link_target.ends_with(".md") {
105            link_target.to_string()
106        } else {
107            link_target.to_string()
108        };
109        
110        if let Some(candidates) = self.name_to_paths.get(&search_key) {
111            if candidates.is_empty() {
112                return None;
113            }
114            
115            // If multiple matches, prefer shortest path (closest to vault root)
116            let best_match = candidates.iter().min_by_key(|p| {
117                p.strip_prefix(vault_root)
118                    .ok()
119                    .map(|rel| rel.components().count())
120                    .unwrap_or(usize::MAX)
121            })?;
122            
123            return Some(best_match.clone());
124        }
125        
126        None
127    }
128    
129    /// Resolve a wikilink target, or generate a default path if the target doesn't exist.
130    ///
131    /// If the target can be resolved to an existing file, returns that path.
132    /// Otherwise, returns a fallback path: `vault_path / target.md`.
133    pub fn resolve_or_default(&self, vault_path: &str, link_target: &str) -> PathBuf {
134        if let Some(resolved) = self.resolve(vault_path, link_target) {
135            return resolved;
136        }
137        
138        // Fallback: vault_path / link_target (with .md extension if not present)
139        let mut target = link_target.to_string();
140        if !target.ends_with(".md") {
141            target.push_str(".md");
142        }
143        Path::new(vault_path).join(target)
144    }
145}

file_metadata.rs

1use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
2
3/// Struct representing metadata information for a file or directory.
4///
5/// This structure contains details about a file or directory, including its path,
6/// filename, whether it's a directory, its size, and the last modified timestamp.
7///
8/// # Fields
9///
10/// * `path` - A `String` representing the full path of the file or directory.
11/// * `filename` - A `String` representing the name of the file or directory.
12/// * `is_dir` - A `bool` indicating whether the path is a directory (`true`) or a file (`false`).
13/// * `size` - A `u64` representing the size of the file in bytes.
14/// * `last_modified` - An `i64` representing the last modified timestamp in Unix epoch time.
15#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug)]
16pub struct FileMetadata {
17    pub path: String,
18    pub filename: String,
19    pub is_dir: bool,
20    pub size: u64,
21    pub last_modified: i64,
22}

6/// * `alias` - Optional display text after the pipe (e.g., "custom text" in [[Note|custom text]])
7#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
8pub struct WikiLink {
9    pub target: String,
10    pub alias: Option<String>,
11}

config.rs

1use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
2use std::fs;
3use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
4
5use crate::error::TessellumError;
6
7#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
8pub struct DailyNotesConfig {
9	#[serde(default = "default_daily_notes_path_template")]
10	pub path_template: String,
11	#[serde(default = "default_daily_notes_template_name")]
12	pub template_name: String,
13}
14
15#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
16pub struct AppConfig {
17	#[serde(default)]
18	pub daily_notes: DailyNotesConfig,
19}
20
21impl Default for DailyNotesConfig {
22	fn default() -> Self {
23		Self {
24			path_template: default_daily_notes_path_template(),
25			template_name: default_daily_notes_template_name(),
26		}
27	}
28}
29
30impl Default for AppConfig {
31	fn default() -> Self {
32		Self {
33			daily_notes: DailyNotesConfig::default(),
34		}
35	}
36}
37
38fn default_daily_notes_path_template() -> String {
39	"Daily/{YYYY}/{MM}/{DD}.md".to_string()
40}
41
42fn default_daily_notes_template_name() -> String {
43	"Daily".to_string()
44}
45
46pub fn config_path(vault_path: &str) -> PathBuf {
47	Path::new(vault_path).join(".tessellum").join("config.json")
48}
49
50pub fn load_or_init_config(vault_path: &str) -> Result<AppConfig, TessellumError> {
51	let path = config_path(vault_path);
52	if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
53		fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;
54	}
55	
56	if path.exists() {
57		match fs::read_to_string(&path) {
58			Ok(raw) => match serde_json::from_str::<AppConfig>(&raw) {
59				Ok(cfg) => return Ok(cfg),
60				Err(e) => {
61					log::warn!("Invalid config.json, using defaults: {}", e);
62				}
63			},
64			Err(e) => {
65				log::warn!("Failed to read config.json, using defaults: {}", e);
66			}
67		}
68	}
69	
70	let cfg = AppConfig::default();
71	write_config(&path, &cfg)?;
72	Ok(cfg)
73}
74
75fn write_config(path: &Path, config: &AppConfig) -> Result<(), TessellumError> {
76	let raw = serde_json::to_string_pretty(config)
77		.map_err(|e| TessellumError::Internal(e.to_string()))?;
78	fs::write(path, raw)?;
79	Ok(())
80}
frontmatter.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib\utils/
frontmatter.rs

1use serde_json::Value;
2
3/// Extracts the raw YAML string and the body (content after frontmatter).
4/// Returns `None` if no frontmatter exists.
5pub fn parse_frontmatter(content: &str) -> Option<(String, String)> {
6	let frontmatter_start = if content.starts_with("---\r\n") {
7		5
8	} else if content.starts_with("---\n") {
9		4
10	} else if content.starts_with("---") {
11		3
12	} else {
13		return None;
14	};
15	
16	let sub = &content[frontmatter_start..];
17	let (idx, delim_len) = if let Some(i) = sub.find("\r\n---") {
18		(i, 5)
19	} else if let Some(i) = sub.find("\n---") {
20		(i, 4)
21	} else if let Some(i) = sub.find("---") {
22		(i, 3)
23	} else {
24		return None;
25	};
26	
27	let yaml_str = content[frontmatter_start..frontmatter_start + idx]
28		.trim()
29		.to_string();
30	
31	let after_start = frontmatter_start + idx + delim_len;
32	let after_dash = &content[after_start..];
33	let body = if let Some(rest) = after_dash.strip_prefix("\r\n") {
34		rest
35	} else if let Some(rest) = after_dash.strip_prefix('\n') {
36		rest
37	} else if let Some(rest) = after_dash.strip_prefix('\r') {
38		rest
39	} else {
40		after_dash
41	};
42	
43	Some((yaml_str, body.to_string()))
44}
45
46/// Converts YAML to a JSON string for database storage.
47pub fn frontmatter_to_json(yaml_str: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
48	let yaml_val: serde_yaml::Value =
49		serde_yaml::from_str(yaml_str).map_err(|e| format!("Failed to parse YAML: {}", e))?;
50	
51	// We only care if it's a mapping at the root
52	if !yaml_val.is_mapping() {
53		return Err("YAML frontmatter must be a mapping/object".to_string());
54	}
55	
56	let json_val: Value = serde_yaml::from_value(yaml_val)
57		.map_err(|e| format!("Failed to convert YAML to JSON: {}", e))?;
58	
59	serde_json::to_string(&json_val).map_err(|e| format!("Failed to serialize JSON: {}", e))
60}
61
62/// Returns the body content without frontmatter (for wikilink extraction, etc.).
63pub fn strip_frontmatter(content: &str) -> &str {
64	let frontmatter_start = if content.starts_with("---\r\n") {
65		5
66	} else if content.starts_with("---\n") {
67		4
68	} else if content.starts_with("---") {
69		3
70	} else {
71		return content;
72	};
73	
74	let sub = &content[frontmatter_start..];
75	let (idx, delim_len) = if let Some(i) = sub.find("\r\n---") {
76		(i, 5)
77	} else if let Some(i) = sub.find("\n---") {
78		(i, 4)
79	} else if let Some(i) = sub.find("---") {
80		(i, 3)
81	} else {
82		return content;
83	};
84	
85	let after_start = frontmatter_start + idx + delim_len;
86	let after_dash = &content[after_start..];
87	if let Some(rest) = after_dash.strip_prefix("\r\n") {
88		return rest;
89	}
90	if let Some(rest) = after_dash.strip_prefix('\n') {
91		return rest;
92	}
93	if let Some(rest) = after_dash.strip_prefix('\r') {
94		return rest;
95	}
96	
97	after_dash
98}
99
100#[cfg(test)]
101mod tests {
102	use super::{parse_frontmatter, strip_frontmatter};
103	
104	#[test]
105	fn parses_frontmatter_with_crlf_delimiters() {
106		let content = "---\r\ntitle: Test\r\n---\r\nBody";
107		let parsed = parse_frontmatter(content).expect("expected frontmatter to parse");
108		
109		assert_eq!(parsed.0, "title: Test");
110		assert_eq!(parsed.1, "Body");
111	}
112	
113	#[test]
114	fn strips_frontmatter_with_crlf_delimiters() {
115		let content = "---\r\ntitle: Test\r\n---\r\nBody";
116		assert_eq!(strip_frontmatter(content), "Body");
117	}
118	
119	#[test]
120	fn parses_frontmatter_without_newlines() {
121		let content = "---title: Test---Body";
122		let parsed = parse_frontmatter(content).expect("expected frontmatter to parse");
123		
124		assert_eq!(parsed.0, "title: Test");
125		assert_eq!(parsed.1, "Body");
126	}
127}

11/// Normalize path separators to forward slashes (for cross-platform consistency)
12pub fn normalize_path(path: &str) -> String {
13	path.replace('\\', "/")
14}
sanitize.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib\utils/
sanitize.rs

1/// Sanitizes a given string by filtering out any characters that are not alphanumeric
2/// or one of the following allowed special characters: space (' '), hyphen ('-'),
3/// underscore ('_'), parentheses, or period.
4///
5/// # Parameters
6/// - `s`: A `String` input containing the text to be sanitized.
7///
8/// # Returns
9/// A new `String` containing only the allowed characters from the input.
10pub fn sanitize_string(s: String) -> String {
11    let sanitized: String = s
12        .chars()
13        .filter(|c| {
14            c.is_alphanumeric()
15                || *c == ' '
16                || *c == '-'
17                || *c == '_'
18                || *c == '('
19                || *c == ')'
20                || *c == '.'
21        })
22        .collect();
23    sanitized
24        .trim_end_matches(|c| c == '.' || c == ' ')
25        .to_string()
26}
27
28#[cfg(test)]
29mod tests {
30    use super::sanitize_string;
31
32    #[test]
33    fn keeps_allowed_characters_and_trims_forbidden_suffixes() {
34        let sanitized = sanitize_string(" Note_Name-01 ().md.. ".to_string());
35
36        assert_eq!(sanitized, " Note_Name-01 ().md");
37    }
38
39    #[test]
40    fn removes_disallowed_characters_but_keeps_inner_spaces() {
41        let sanitized = sanitize_string("Budget: Q2 / Draft #1".to_string());
42
43        assert_eq!(sanitized, "Budget Q2  Draft 1");
44    }
45
46    #[test]
47    fn can_sanitize_to_empty_string() {
48        let sanitized = sanitize_string("...   ".to_string());
49
50        assert_eq!(sanitized, "");
51    }
52}
tags.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib\utils/
tags.rs

1use regex::Regex;
2use std::collections::HashSet;
3
4fn is_fence_line(line: &str) -> bool {
5	let trimmed = line.trim_start();
6	trimmed.starts_with("```") || trimmed.starts_with("~~~")
7}
8
9fn is_block_line(line: &str) -> bool {
10	line.trim_start().starts_with('>')
11}
12
13fn strip_inline_code_spans_for_tag_scan(line: &str) -> String {
14	let chars: Vec<char> = line.chars().collect();
15	let mut result = String::with_capacity(line.len());
16	let mut i = 0;
17	let mut in_code = false;
18	let mut delimiter_len = 0usize;
19
20	while i < chars.len() {
21		if chars[i] == '`' {
22			let mut run_len = 1usize;
23			while i + run_len < chars.len() && chars[i + run_len] == '`' {
24				run_len += 1;
25			}
26
27			if !in_code {
28				in_code = true;
29				delimiter_len = run_len;
30			} else if run_len == delimiter_len {
31				in_code = false;
32				delimiter_len = 0;
33			}
34
35			for _ in 0..run_len {
36				result.push(' ');
37			}
38			i += run_len;
39			continue;
40		}
41
42		result.push(if in_code { ' ' } else { chars[i] });
43		i += 1;
44	}
45
46	result
47}
48
49/// Extract normalized tags from markdown content.
50///
51/// Normalization rules:
52/// - strip leading '#'
53/// - lowercase
54/// - keep '/' for hierarchical tags
55pub fn extract_tags(content: &str) -> Vec<String> {
56	let mut tags = HashSet::new();
57	
58	let mut body_content = content;
59	if let Some((_, _)) = crate::utils::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter(content) {
60		body_content = crate::utils::frontmatter::strip_frontmatter(content);
61	}
62	
63	// Inline tags: #tag-name
64	let tag_regex = Regex::new(r"(?:^|\s)#([a-zA-Z0-9_\-/]+)").unwrap();
65	let mut in_fenced_block = false;
66	for line in body_content.lines() {
67		if is_fence_line(line) {
68			in_fenced_block = !in_fenced_block;
69			continue;
70		}
71
72		if in_fenced_block || is_block_line(line) {
73			continue;
74		}
75		let scan_line = strip_inline_code_spans_for_tag_scan(line);
76
77		for cap in tag_regex.captures_iter(&scan_line) {
78			if let Some(tag_match) = cap.get(1) {
79				let normalized = normalize_tag(tag_match.as_str());
80				if !normalized.is_empty() {
81					tags.insert(normalized);
82				}
83			}
84		}
85	}
86	
87	// Frontmatter tags
88	if let Some((yaml, _)) = crate::utils::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter(content) {
89		if let Ok(json) = crate::utils::frontmatter::frontmatter_to_json(&yaml) {
90			if let Ok(value) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&json) {
91				if let Some(tags_value) = value.get("tags") {
92					match tags_value {
93						serde_json::Value::Array(arr) => {
94							for item in arr {
95								if let Some(tag) = item.as_str() {
96									let normalized = normalize_tag(tag);
97									if !normalized.is_empty() {
98										tags.insert(normalized);
99									}
100								}
101							}
102						}
103						serde_json::Value::String(s) => {
104							for part in s.split(',') {
105								let normalized = normalize_tag(part);
106								if !normalized.is_empty() {
107									tags.insert(normalized);
108								}
109							}
110						}
111						_ => {}
112					}
113				}
114			}
115		}
116	}
117	
118	let mut result: Vec<String> = tags.into_iter().collect();
119	result.sort();
120	result
121}
122
123fn normalize_tag(tag: &str) -> String {
124	tag.trim()
125		.trim_start_matches('#')
126		.to_lowercase()
127}
128
129#[cfg(test)]
130mod tests {
131	use super::extract_tags;
132
133	#[test]
134	fn ignores_tags_in_fenced_blocks_and_blockquotes() {
135		let content = r##"#ok
136
137```ts
138const x = "#ignored_code";
139```
140
141> #ignored_quote
142> [!note] #ignored_callout
143
144final #visible
145"##;
146
147		let tags = extract_tags(content);
148		assert!(tags.contains(&"ok".to_string()));
149		assert!(tags.contains(&"visible".to_string()));
150		assert!(!tags.contains(&"ignored_code".to_string()));
151		assert!(!tags.contains(&"ignored_quote".to_string()));
152		assert!(!tags.contains(&"ignored_callout".to_string()));
153	}
154
155	#[test]
156	fn keeps_frontmatter_tags() {
157		let content = r#"---
158tags: [alpha, beta]
159---
160
161body
162"#;
163		let tags = extract_tags(content);
164		assert!(tags.contains(&"alpha".to_string()));
165		assert!(tags.contains(&"beta".to_string()));
166	}
167
168	#[test]
169	fn ignores_tags_inside_inline_code_spans() {
170		let content = r##"normal #visible `#ignored_inline`
171text ``#also_ignored`` and #kept
172"##;
173
174		let tags = extract_tags(content);
175		assert!(tags.contains(&"visible".to_string()));
176		assert!(tags.contains(&"kept".to_string()));
177		assert!(!tags.contains(&"ignored_inline".to_string()));
178		assert!(!tags.contains(&"also_ignored".to_string()));
179	}
180}
181
validate.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib\utils/
validate.rs

1use std::path::PathBuf;
2
3/// Validates that a given path resolves to a location inside the vault directory.
4/// Uses `canonicalize()` to resolve symlinks and `..` components, preventing
5/// path traversal attacks (e.g., `../../etc/passwd`).
6pub fn validate_path_in_vault(path: &str, vault_path: &str) -> Result<PathBuf, String> {
7	let resolved = PathBuf::from(path)
8		.canonicalize()
9		.map_err(|e| format!("Invalid path '{}': {}", path, e))?;
10	let vault_root = PathBuf::from(vault_path)
11		.canonicalize()
12		.map_err(|e| format!("Invalid vault path '{}': {}", vault_path, e))?;
13	if !resolved.starts_with(&vault_root) {
14		return Err("Security Error: Cannot access files outside the vault".to_string());
15	}
16	Ok(resolved)
17}
18
19/// Checks if a path contains hidden or special directories/files (starting with `.`).
20/// This covers `.git`, `.trash`, `.obsidian`, etc. in a cross-platform way.
21pub fn is_hidden_or_special(path: &std::path::Path) -> bool {
22	path.components()
23		.any(|c| c.as_os_str().to_string_lossy().starts_with('.'))
24}
25
26#[cfg(test)]
27mod tests {
28	use std::fs;
29
30	use tempfile::tempdir;
31
32	use super::{is_hidden_or_special, validate_path_in_vault};
33
34	#[test]
35	fn validates_paths_that_resolve_inside_the_vault() {
36		let vault = tempdir().unwrap();
37		let notes_dir = vault.path().join("Notes");
38		fs::create_dir_all(&notes_dir).unwrap();
39		let note = notes_dir.join("Note.md");
40		fs::write(&note, "# Note").unwrap();
41
42		let validated = validate_path_in_vault(
43			note.to_str().unwrap(),
44			vault.path().to_str().unwrap(),
45		)
46		.unwrap();
47
48		assert_eq!(validated, note.canonicalize().unwrap());
49	}
50
51	#[test]
52	fn rejects_paths_outside_the_vault() {
53		let vault = tempdir().unwrap();
54		let outside = tempdir().unwrap();
55		let outside_note = outside.path().join("Outside.md");
56		fs::write(&outside_note, "# Outside").unwrap();
57
58		let err = validate_path_in_vault(
59			outside_note.to_str().unwrap(),
60			vault.path().to_str().unwrap(),
61		)
62		.unwrap_err();
63
64		assert!(err.contains("outside the vault"));
65	}
66
67	#[test]
68	fn detects_hidden_and_special_path_components() {
69		assert!(is_hidden_or_special(std::path::Path::new(".git/config")));
70		assert!(is_hidden_or_special(std::path::Path::new("Notes/.trash/Entry.md")));
71		assert!(!is_hidden_or_special(std::path::Path::new("Notes/Entry.md")));
72	}
73}

Files

tessellum_lib/
db.rs

1use std::time::Duration;
2use sqlx::Pool;
3use sqlx::sqlite::{Sqlite, SqliteConnectOptions, SqliteJournalMode, SqlitePoolOptions};
4
5pub struct Database {
6    pool: Pool<Sqlite>,
7}
8
9/// Initializes a new database connection pool and creates the necessary tables if they do not exist.
10impl Database {
11    pub async fn init(db_path: &str) -> Result<Self, sqlx::Error> {
12        let options = SqliteConnectOptions::new()
13            .filename(db_path)
14            .create_if_missing(true)
15            // Allow concurrent readers while writes are happening.
16            .journal_mode(SqliteJournalMode::Wal)
17            // Give SQLite write contention enough time to resolve.
18            .busy_timeout(Duration::from_secs(15));
19        
20        let pool = SqlitePoolOptions::new()
21            .max_connections(5)
22            .connect_with(options)
23            .await?;
24        
25        // Create notes table
26        sqlx::query(
27            "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notes (
28                path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
29                modified_at INTEGER,
30                size INTEGER,
31                frontmatter TEXT
32            );",
33        )
34            .execute(&pool)
35            .await?;
36        
37        // Migrate table if missing the new column (fails silently if column already exists)
38        let _ = sqlx::query("ALTER TABLE notes ADD COLUMN frontmatter TEXT;")
39            .execute(&pool)
40            .await;
41        
42        let _ = sqlx::query("ALTER TABLE notes ADD COLUMN inline_tags TEXT;")
43            .execute(&pool)
44            .await;
45        
46        // Create tags table for normalized tags
47        sqlx::query(
48            "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS note_tags (
49                path TEXT NOT NULL,
50                tag TEXT NOT NULL,
51                PRIMARY KEY (path, tag),
52                FOREIGN KEY(path) REFERENCES notes(path) ON DELETE CASCADE ON
53                 UPDATE CASCADE
54            );",
55        )
56            .execute(&pool)
57            .await?;
58        
59        // Track all files indexed for search (markdown + non-markdown)
60        sqlx::query(
61            "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS search_files (
62                path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
63                modified_at INTEGER,
64                is_markdown INTEGER
65            );",
66        )
67            .execute(&pool)
68            .await?;
69        
70        // Create links table with RESOLVED target paths
71        sqlx::query(
72            "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS links (
73                source_path TEXT,
74                target_path TEXT,
75                PRIMARY KEY (source_path, target_path),
76                FOREIGN KEY(source_path) REFERENCES notes(path) ON DELETE CASCADE
77            );",
78        )
79            .execute(&pool)
80            .await?;
81        
82        // Create index for faster backlink queries
83        sqlx::query("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_links_target ON links(target_path);")
84            .execute(&pool)
85            .await?;
86        
87        // Enable foreign key enforcement (SQLite has it OFF by default)
88        sqlx::query("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;")
89            .execute(&pool)
90            .await?;
91        
92        Ok(Self { pool })
93    }
94    
95    /// Index a file with its metadata and resolved wikilinks.
96    ///
97    /// # Arguments
98    ///
99    /// * `path` - The full path to the source file
100    /// * `modified` - Unix timestamp of last modification
101    /// * `size` - File size in bytes
102    /// * `resolved_links` - Vector of FULL PATHS to target files (already resolved from wikilinks)
103    pub async fn index_file(
104        &self,
105        path: &str,
106        modified: i64,
107        size: u64,
108        frontmatter_json: Option<&str>,
109        inline_tags_json: Option<&str>,
110        resolved_links: &[String],
111    ) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
112        // Insert or update the note metadata
113        sqlx::query(
114            "INSERT INTO notes (path, modified_at, size, frontmatter, inline_tags) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
115             ON CONFLICT(path) DO UPDATE SET modified_at = ?, size = ?, frontmatter = ?, inline_tags = ?",
116        )
117            .bind(path)
118            .bind(modified)
119            .bind(size as i64)
120            .bind(frontmatter_json)
121            .bind(inline_tags_json)
122            .bind(modified)
123            .bind(size as i64)
124            .bind(frontmatter_json)
125            .bind(inline_tags_json)
126            .execute(&self.pool)
127            .await?;
128        
129        // Update links in a transaction
130        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;
131        
132        // Delete old links from this source
133        sqlx::query("DELETE FROM links WHERE source_path = ?")
134            .bind(path)
135            .execute(&mut *tx)
136            .await?;
137        
138        // Deduplicate links - a note can have multiple wikilinks to the same target,
139        // but we only store one link relationship per source-target pair
140        let mut unique_links: Vec<&String> = resolved_links.iter().collect();
141        unique_links.sort();
142        unique_links.dedup();
143        
144        // Insert new resolved links (deduplicated)
145        for target_path in unique_links {
146            sqlx::query("INSERT INTO links (source_path, target_path) VALUES (?, ?)")
147                .bind(path)
148                .bind(target_path)
149                .execute(&mut *tx)
150                .await?;
151        }
152        
153        tx.commit().await?;
154        Ok(())
155    }
156    
157    /// Replace tags for a file (normalized tags).
158    pub async fn set_note_tags(&self, path: &str, tags: &[String]) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
159        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;
160        
161        sqlx::query("DELETE FROM note_tags WHERE path = ?")
162            .bind(path)
163            .execute(&mut *tx)
164            .await?;
165        
166        for tag in tags {
167            sqlx::query("INSERT OR IGNORE INTO note_tags (path, tag) VALUES (?, ?)")
168                .bind(path)
169                .bind(tag)
170                .execute(&mut *tx)
171                .await?;
172        }
173        
174        tx.commit().await?;
175        Ok(())
176    }
177    
178    /// Get all outgoing links from a specific file.
179    ///
180    /// Returns a vector of full paths to files that this file links to.
181    pub async fn get_outgoing_links(&self, source_path: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>, sqlx::Error> {
182        let denormalized = source_path.replace('/', "\\");
183        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String,)>(
184            "SELECT target_path FROM links WHERE source_path = ? OR source_path = ?",
185        )
186            .bind(source_path)
187            .bind(&denormalized)
188            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
189            .await?;
190        
191        Ok(rows
192            .into_iter()
193            .map(|(path,)| crate::utils::normalize_path(&path))
194            .collect())
195    }
196    
197    /// Get all backlinks to a specific file.
198    ///
199    /// Returns a vector of full paths to files that link to this file.
200    pub async fn get_backlinks(&self, target_path: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>, sqlx::Error> {
201        let denormalized = target_path.replace('/', "\\");
202        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String,)>(
203            "SELECT source_path FROM links WHERE target_path = ? OR target_path = ?",
204        )
205            .bind(target_path)
206            .bind(&denormalized)
207            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
208            .await?;
209        
210        Ok(rows
211            .into_iter()
212            .map(|(path,)| crate::utils::normalize_path(&path))
213            .collect())
214    }
215    
216    /// Get all links in the vault (for graph visualization).
217    ///
218    /// Returns a vector of (source_path, target_path) tuples.
219    pub async fn get_all_links(&self) -> Result<Vec<(String, String)>, sqlx::Error> {
220        let rows =
221            sqlx::query_as::<_, (String, String)>("SELECT source_path, target_path FROM links")
222                .fetch_all(&self.pool)
223                .await?;
224        
225        Ok(rows)
226    }
227    
228    /// Update links when a file is renamed/moved.
229    ///
230    /// This updates both:
231    /// 1. Links FROM this file (update source_path)
232    /// 2. Links TO this file (update target_path)
233    pub async fn update_file_path(
234        &self,
235        old_path: &str,
236        new_path: &str,
237    ) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
238        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;
239        
240        // Defer FK checks until commit so we can safely update the notes PK
241        // and then update the referencing links.source_path in the same transaction.
242        sqlx::query("PRAGMA defer_foreign_keys = ON")
243            .execute(&mut *tx)
244            .await?;
245        
246        // 1. Update the record for the file/folder itself
247        // Use OR REPLACE for notes PK in case of orphaned DB entries
248        sqlx::query("UPDATE OR REPLACE notes SET path = ? WHERE path = ?")
249            .bind(new_path)
250            .bind(old_path)
251            .execute(&mut *tx)
252            .await?;
253        
254        // 2. If this is a folder rename, update all child notes
255        // Note: paths are normalized with forward slashes
256        let old_prefix = format!("{}/%", old_path.replace('\\', "/"));
257        sqlx::query(
258            "UPDATE OR REPLACE notes SET path = ? || substr(path, length(?) + 1)
259             WHERE path LIKE ?",
260        )
261            .bind(new_path)
262            .bind(old_path)
263            .bind(&old_prefix)
264            .execute(&mut *tx)
265            .await?;
266        
267        // 3. Update links where this file/folder is the source
268        // Handles exact match
269        sqlx::query("UPDATE OR IGNORE links SET source_path = ? WHERE source_path = ?")
270            .bind(new_path)
271            .bind(old_path)
272            .execute(&mut *tx)
273            .await?;
274        
275        // Handles children if folder
276        sqlx::query(
277            "UPDATE OR IGNORE links SET source_path = ? || substr(source_path, length(?) + 1)
278             WHERE source_path LIKE ?",
279        )
280            .bind(new_path)
281            .bind(old_path)
282            .bind(&old_prefix)
283            .execute(&mut *tx)
284            .await?;
285        
286        // Cleanup merged source links (ones that didn't update because of conflicts)
287        sqlx::query("DELETE FROM links WHERE source_path = ? OR source_path LIKE ?")
288            .bind(old_path)
289            .bind(&old_prefix)
290            .execute(&mut *tx)
291            .await?;
292        
293        // 4. Update links where this file/folder is the target (backlinks)
294        // Handles exact match
295        sqlx::query("UPDATE OR IGNORE links SET target_path = ? WHERE target_path = ?")
296            .bind(new_path)
297            .bind(old_path)
298            .execute(&mut *tx)
299            .await?;
300        
301        // Handles children if folder
302        sqlx::query(
303            "UPDATE OR IGNORE links SET target_path = ? || substr(target_path, length(?) + 1)
304             WHERE target_path LIKE ?",
305        )
306            .bind(new_path)
307            .bind(old_path)
308            .bind(&old_prefix)
309            .execute(&mut *tx)
310            .await?;
311        
312        // Cleanup merged target links
313        sqlx::query("DELETE FROM links WHERE target_path = ? OR target_path LIKE ?")
314            .bind(old_path)
315            .bind(&old_prefix)
316            .execute(&mut *tx)
317            .await?;
318        
319        tx.commit().await?;
320        Ok(())
321    }
322    
323    /// Delete a file from the index.
324    ///
325    /// This also removes all outgoing links from this file due to CASCADE constraints.
326    pub async fn delete_file(&self, path: &str) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
327        sqlx::query("DELETE FROM notes WHERE path = ?")
328            .bind(path)
329            .execute(&self.pool)
330            .await?;
331        
332        Ok(())
333    }
334    
335    /// Delete all files from the index whose path starts with the given prefix.
336    ///
337    /// Useful for removing all notes inside a directory that was trashed.
338    pub async fn delete_files_by_prefix(&self, prefix: &str) -> Result<usize, sqlx::Error> {
339        let result = sqlx::query("DELETE FROM notes WHERE path LIKE ?")
340            .bind(format!("{}%", prefix))
341            .execute(&self.pool)
342            .await?;
343        
344        Ok(result.rows_affected() as usize)
345    }
346    
347    /// Get all orphaned files (files with no incoming or outgoing links).
348    pub async fn get_orphaned_files(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>, sqlx::Error> {
349        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String,)>(
350            "SELECT path FROM notes
351             WHERE path NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT source_path FROM links)
352             AND path NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT target_path FROM links)
353             AND replace(path, '/', '\\') NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT target_path FROM links)",
354        )
355            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
356            .await?;
357        
358        Ok(rows.into_iter().map(|(path,)| path).collect())
359    }
360    
361    /// Get broken links (links pointing to non-existent files).
362    ///
363    /// Returns a vector of (source_path, broken_target_path) tuples.
364    pub async fn get_broken_links(&self) -> Result<Vec<(String, String)>, sqlx::Error> {
365        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String, String)>(
366            "SELECT source_path, target_path FROM links
367             WHERE target_path NOT IN (SELECT path FROM notes)",
368        )
369            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
370            .await?;
371        
372        Ok(rows)
373    }
374    
375    /// Get all indexed file paths with their modified times.
376    ///
377    /// Returns a vector of (path, modified_at) tuples for comparison with filesystem.
378    pub async fn get_all_indexed_files(&self) -> Result<Vec<(String, i64)>, sqlx::Error> {
379        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String, i64)>("SELECT path, modified_at FROM notes")
380            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
381            .await?;
382        
383        Ok(rows)
384    }
385    
386    /// Get all indexed search files (markdown and non-markdown).
387    pub async fn get_all_search_files(&self) -> Result<Vec<(String, i64, i64)>, sqlx::Error> {
388        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String, i64, i64)>(
389            "SELECT path, modified_at, is_markdown FROM search_files",
390        )
391            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
392            .await?;
393        
394        Ok(rows)
395    }
396    
397    /// Upsert search file metadata.
398    pub async fn upsert_search_file(
399        &self,
400        path: &str,
401        modified: i64,
402        is_markdown: bool,
403    ) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
404        sqlx::query(
405            "INSERT INTO search_files (path, modified_at, is_markdown) VALUES (?, ?, ?)
406             ON CONFLICT(path) DO UPDATE SET modified_at = ?, is_markdown = ?",
407        )
408            .bind(path)
409            .bind(modified)
410            .bind(if is_markdown { 1 } else { 0 })
411            .bind(modified)
412            .bind(if is_markdown { 1 } else { 0 })
413            .execute(&self.pool)
414            .await?;
415        
416        Ok(())
417    }
418    
419    /// Delete search files by paths.
420    pub async fn delete_search_files(&self, paths: &[String]) -> Result<usize, sqlx::Error> {
421        if paths.is_empty() {
422            return Ok(0);
423        }
424        
425        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;
426        let mut deleted = 0;
427        for path in paths {
428            let result = sqlx::query("DELETE FROM search_files WHERE path = ?")
429                .bind(path)
430                .execute(&mut *tx)
431                .await?;
432            deleted += result.rows_affected() as usize;
433        }
434        tx.commit().await?;
435        Ok(deleted)
436    }
437    
438    /// Update search file paths when a file or folder is renamed/moved.
439    pub async fn update_search_file_path(
440        &self,
441        old_path: &str,
442        new_path: &str,
443    ) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
444        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;
445        
446        sqlx::query("UPDATE OR REPLACE search_files SET path = ? WHERE path = ?")
447            .bind(new_path)
448            .bind(old_path)
449            .execute(&mut *tx)
450            .await?;
451        
452        let old_prefix = format!("{}/%", old_path.replace('\\', "/"));
453        sqlx::query(
454            "UPDATE OR REPLACE search_files SET path = ? || substr(path, length(?) + 1)
455             WHERE path LIKE ?",
456        )
457            .bind(new_path)
458            .bind(old_path)
459            .bind(&old_prefix)
460            .execute(&mut *tx)
461            .await?;
462        
463        sqlx::query("DELETE FROM search_files WHERE path = ? OR path LIKE ?")
464            .bind(old_path)
465            .bind(&old_prefix)
466            .execute(&mut *tx)
467            .await?;
468        
469        tx.commit().await?;
470        Ok(())
471    }
472    
473    /// Delete multiple files from the index in a single transaction.
474    ///
475    /// More efficient than calling delete_file multiple times.
476    pub async fn batch_delete_files(&self, paths: &[String]) -> Result<usize, sqlx::Error> {
477        if paths.is_empty() {
478            return Ok(0);
479        }
480        
481        let mut tx = self.pool.begin().await?;
482        let mut deleted = 0;
483        
484        for path in paths {
485            let result = sqlx::query("DELETE FROM notes WHERE path = ?")
486                .bind(path)
487                .execute(&mut *tx)
488                .await?;
489            deleted += result.rows_affected() as usize;
490        }
491        
492        tx.commit().await?;
493        Ok(deleted)
494    }
495    
496    /// Search notes by tags.
497    ///
498    /// match_all = true requires all tags, otherwise any tag.
499    pub async fn search_notes_by_tags(
500        &self,
501        tags: &[String],
502        match_all: bool,
503        limit: u32,
504        offset: u32,
505    ) -> Result<(Vec<String>, u32), sqlx::Error> {
506        if tags.is_empty() {
507            return Ok((Vec::new(), 0));
508        }
509        
510        let limit_i64 = limit as i64;
511        let offset_i64 = offset as i64;
512        
513        if match_all {
514            let mut tag_params = String::new();
515            for i in 0..tags.len() {
516                if i > 0 {
517                    tag_params.push_str(", ");
518                }
519                tag_params.push('?');
520            }
521            
522            let count_query = format!(
523                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
524                    SELECT path FROM note_tags
525                    WHERE tag IN ({})
526                    GROUP BY path
527                    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT tag) = ?
528                )",
529                tag_params
530            );
531            
532            let mut count_q = sqlx::query_scalar::<_, i64>(&count_query);
533            for tag in tags {
534                count_q = count_q.bind(tag);
535            }
536            count_q = count_q.bind(tags.len() as i64);
537            let total = count_q.fetch_one(&self.pool).await? as u32;
538            
539            let data_query = format!(
540                "SELECT path FROM note_tags
541                 WHERE tag IN ({})
542                 GROUP BY path
543                 HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT tag) = ?
544                 ORDER BY path
545                 LIMIT ? OFFSET ?",
546                tag_params
547            );
548            
549            let mut data_q = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String,)>(&data_query);
550            for tag in tags {
551                data_q = data_q.bind(tag);
552            }
553            data_q = data_q
554                .bind(tags.len() as i64)
555                .bind(limit_i64)
556                .bind(offset_i64);
557            
558            let rows = data_q.fetch_all(&self.pool).await?;
559            Ok((rows.into_iter().map(|(p,)| p).collect(), total))
560        } else {
561            let mut tag_params = String::new();
562            for i in 0..tags.len() {
563                if i > 0 {
564                    tag_params.push_str(", ");
565                }
566                tag_params.push('?');
567            }
568            
569            let count_query = format!(
570                "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT path) FROM note_tags WHERE tag IN ({})",
571                tag_params
572            );
573            let mut count_q = sqlx::query_scalar::<_, i64>(&count_query);
574            for tag in tags {
575                count_q = count_q.bind(tag);
576            }
577            let total = count_q.fetch_one(&self.pool).await? as u32;
578            
579            let data_query = format!(
580                "SELECT DISTINCT path FROM note_tags
581                 WHERE tag IN ({})
582                 ORDER BY path
583                 LIMIT ? OFFSET ?",
584                tag_params
585            );
586            let mut data_q = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String,)>(&data_query);
587            for tag in tags {
588                data_q = data_q.bind(tag);
589            }
590            data_q = data_q.bind(limit_i64).bind(offset_i64);
591            
592            let rows = data_q.fetch_all(&self.pool).await?;
593            Ok((rows.into_iter().map(|(p,)| p).collect(), total))
594        }
595    }
596    
597    /// Get frontmatter JSON for a specific file.
598    pub async fn get_frontmatter(&self, path: &str) -> Result<Option<String>, sqlx::Error> {
599        let row =
600            sqlx::query_as::<_, (Option<String>,)>("SELECT frontmatter FROM notes WHERE path = ?")
601                .bind(path)
602                .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
603                .await?;
604        
605        Ok(row.and_then(|(frontmatter,)| frontmatter))
606    }
607    
608    /// Get all unique tags from frontmatter metadata and inline tags across all notes.
609    pub async fn get_all_tags(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>, sqlx::Error> {
610        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, (Option<String>, Option<String>)>(
611            "SELECT frontmatter, inline_tags FROM notes WHERE frontmatter IS NOT NULL OR inline_tags IS NOT NULL",
612        )
613            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
614            .await?;
615        
616        let mut all_tags = std::collections::HashSet::new();
617        
618        for (frontmatter_opt, inline_tags_opt) in rows {
619            // Process frontmatter tags
620            if let Some(frontmatter_json) = frontmatter_opt {
621                if let Ok(parsed) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&frontmatter_json) {
622                    if let Some(tags) = parsed.get("tags") {
623                        if let Some(tags_array) = tags.as_array() {
624                            for tag in tags_array {
625                                if let Some(tag_str) = tag.as_str() {
626                                    all_tags.insert(tag_str.to_string());
627                                }
628                            }
629                        } else if let Some(tag_str) = tags.as_str() {
630                            // Support strings (e.g. tags: tag1, tag2)
631                            for t in tag_str.split(',') {
632                                let normalized = t.trim().trim_start_matches('#');
633                                if !normalized.is_empty() {
634                                    all_tags.insert(normalized.to_string());
635                                }
636                            }
637                        }
638                    }
639                }
640            }
641            
642            // Process inline tags
643            if let Some(inline_tags_json) = inline_tags_opt {
644                if let Ok(parsed) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<String>>(&inline_tags_json) {
645                    for tag in parsed {
646                        all_tags.insert(tag);
647                    }
648                }
649            }
650        }
651        
652        let mut sorted_tags: Vec<String> = all_tags.into_iter().collect();
653        sorted_tags.sort();
654        Ok(sorted_tags)
655    }
656    
657    /// Get all tags for each indexed file
658    pub async fn get_files_tags(
659        &self,
660    ) -> Result<std::collections::HashMap<String, Vec<String>>, sqlx::Error> {
661        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String, Option<String>, Option<String>)>(
662            "SELECT path, frontmatter, inline_tags FROM notes",
663        )
664            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
665            .await?;
666        
667        let mut result = std::collections::HashMap::new();
668        
669        for (path, frontmatter_opt, inline_tags_opt) in rows {
670            let mut file_tags = Vec::new();
671            
672            if let Some(frontmatter_json) = frontmatter_opt {
673                if let Ok(parsed) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&frontmatter_json) {
674                    if let Some(tags) = parsed.get("tags") {
675                        if let Some(tags_array) = tags.as_array() {
676                            for tag in tags_array {
677                                if let Some(tag_str) = tag.as_str() {
678                                    file_tags.push(tag_str.to_string());
679                                }
680                            }
681                        } else if let Some(tag_str) = tags.as_str() {
682                            for t in tag_str.split(',') {
683                                file_tags.push(t.trim().to_string());
684                            }
685                        }
686                    }
687                }
688            }
689            
690            if let Some(inline_tags_json) = inline_tags_opt {
691                if let Ok(parsed) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<String>>(&inline_tags_json) {
692                    for tag in parsed {
693                        if !file_tags.contains(&tag) {
694                            file_tags.push(tag);
695                        }
696                    }
697                }
698            }
699            
700            result.insert(path, file_tags);
701        }
702        
703        Ok(result)
704    }
705    /// Get all tags for a specific indexed file.
706    pub async fn get_file_tags(&self, path: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>, sqlx::Error> {
707        let row = sqlx::query_as::<_, (Option<String>, Option<String>)>(
708            "SELECT frontmatter, inline_tags FROM notes WHERE path = ?",
709        )
710            .bind(path)
711            .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
712            .await?;
713        
714        let mut file_tags = Vec::new();
715        
716        if let Some((frontmatter_opt, inline_tags_opt)) = row {
717            if let Some(frontmatter_json) = frontmatter_opt {
718                if let Ok(parsed) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&frontmatter_json) {
719                    if let Some(tags) = parsed.get("tags") {
720                        if let Some(tags_array) = tags.as_array() {
721                            for tag in tags_array {
722                                if let Some(tag_str) = tag.as_str() {
723                                    file_tags.push(tag_str.to_string());
724                                }
725                            }
726                        } else if let Some(tag_str) = tags.as_str() {
727                            for t in tag_str.split(',') {
728                                let normalized = t.trim().trim_start_matches('#');
729                                if !normalized.is_empty() {
730                                    file_tags.push(normalized.to_string());
731                                }
732                            }
733                        }
734                    }
735                }
736            }
737            
738            if let Some(inline_tags_json) = inline_tags_opt {
739                if let Ok(parsed) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<String>>(&inline_tags_json) {
740                    for tag in parsed {
741                        if !file_tags.contains(&tag) {
742                            file_tags.push(tag);
743                        }
744                    }
745                }
746            }
747        }
748        
749        Ok(file_tags)
750    }
751    
752    /// Get all unique property keys from frontmatter metadata across all notes.
753    pub async fn get_all_property_keys(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>, sqlx::Error> {
754        let rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, (Option<String>,)>(
755            "SELECT frontmatter FROM notes WHERE frontmatter IS NOT NULL",
756        )
757            .fetch_all(&self.pool)
758            .await?;
759        
760        let mut all_keys = std::collections::HashSet::new();
761        
762        for (frontmatter_opt,) in rows {
763            if let Some(frontmatter_json) = frontmatter_opt {
764                if let Ok(parsed) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&frontmatter_json) {
765                    if let Some(obj) = parsed.as_object() {
766                        for key in obj.keys() {
767                            all_keys.insert(key.clone());
768                        }
769                    }
770                }
771            }
772        }
773        
774        let mut sorted_keys: Vec<String> = all_keys.into_iter().collect();
775        sorted_keys.sort();
776        Ok(sorted_keys)
777    }
778    
779    /// Read a single note projection for Kuzu sync.
780    /// Returns (id, title, tags) where id is the note path.
781    pub async fn get_note_projection(
782        &self,
783        note_id: &str,
784    ) -> Result<Option<(String, String, Vec<String>)>, sqlx::Error> {
785        let exists = sqlx::query_scalar::<_, i64>("SELECT COUNT(1) FROM notes WHERE path = ?")
786            .bind(note_id)
787            .fetch_one(&self.pool)
788            .await?
789            > 0;
790        
791        if !exists {
792            return Ok(None);
793        }
794        
795        let tags = self.get_file_tags(note_id).await?;
796        let title = crate::grafeo_projection::title_from_note_id(note_id);
797        Ok(Some((note_id.to_string(), title, tags)))
798    }
799    
800    /// Read all note projections for Kuzu full sync.
801    /// Returns (id, title, tags) where id is the note path.
802    pub async fn get_note_projection_rows(
803        &self,
804    ) -> Result<Vec<(String, String, Vec<String>)>, sqlx::Error> {
805        let notes = self.get_all_indexed_files().await?;
806        let file_tags = self.get_files_tags().await?;
807        
808        let projections = notes
809            .into_iter()
810            .map(|(path, _)| {
811                let title = crate::grafeo_projection::title_from_note_id(&path);
812                let tags = file_tags.get(&path).cloned().unwrap_or_default();
813                (path, title, tags)
814            })
815            .collect();
816        
817        Ok(projections)
818    }
819}
820
821#[cfg(test)]
822mod tests {
823    use tempfile::tempdir;
824
825    use super::Database;
826
827    async fn open_test_db() -> Database {
828        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
829        Database::init(dir.path().join("test.sqlite").to_str().unwrap())
830            .await
831            .unwrap()
832    }
833
834    #[tokio::test]
835    async fn indexes_files_deduplicates_links_and_returns_backlinks() {
836        let db = open_test_db().await;
837        db.index_file(
838            "Vault/Alpha.md",
839            10,
840            100,
841            None,
842            None,
843            &[
844                "Vault/Beta.md".to_string(),
845                "Vault/Beta.md".to_string(),
846                "Vault/Gamma.md".to_string(),
847            ],
848        )
849        .await
850        .unwrap();
851        db.index_file("Vault/Beta.md", 20, 120, None, None, &[]).await.unwrap();
852
853        let outgoing = db.get_outgoing_links("Vault/Alpha.md").await.unwrap();
854        assert_eq!(outgoing, vec!["Vault/Beta.md", "Vault/Gamma.md"]);
855
856        let backlinks = db.get_backlinks("Vault/Beta.md").await.unwrap();
857        assert_eq!(backlinks, vec!["Vault/Alpha.md"]);
858    }
859
860    #[tokio::test]
861    async fn updates_file_paths_for_folder_renames() {
862        let db = open_test_db().await;
863        db.index_file(
864            "Vault/Folder/Alpha.md",
865            10,
866            100,
867            None,
868            None,
869            &["Vault/Folder/Beta.md".to_string()],
870        )
871        .await
872        .unwrap();
873        db.index_file("Vault/Folder/Beta.md", 20, 120, None, None, &[]).await.unwrap();
874
875        db.update_file_path("Vault/Folder", "Vault/Renamed").await.unwrap();
876
877        let outgoing = db.get_outgoing_links("Vault/Renamed/Alpha.md").await.unwrap();
878        assert_eq!(outgoing, vec!["Vault/Renamed/Beta.md"]);
879        let backlinks = db.get_backlinks("Vault/Renamed/Beta.md").await.unwrap();
880        assert_eq!(backlinks, vec!["Vault/Renamed/Alpha.md"]);
881    }
882
883    #[tokio::test]
884    async fn searches_tags_and_collects_frontmatter_metadata() {
885        let db = open_test_db().await;
886        db.index_file(
887            "Vault/Alpha.md",
888            10,
889            100,
890            Some(r#"{"tags":["project","alpha"],"status":"open"}"#),
891            Some(r#"["inline"]"#),
892            &[],
893        )
894        .await
895        .unwrap();
896        db.index_file(
897            "Vault/Beta.md",
898            20,
899            120,
900            Some(r#"{"tags":"project, team","owner":"jorge"}"#),
901            None,
902            &[],
903        )
904        .await
905        .unwrap();
906        db.set_note_tags(
907            "Vault/Alpha.md",
908            &["project".to_string(), "alpha".to_string()],
909        )
910        .await
911        .unwrap();
912        db.set_note_tags("Vault/Beta.md", &["project".to_string(), "team".to_string()])
913            .await
914            .unwrap();
915
916        let (all_matches, total_any) = db
917            .search_notes_by_tags(&["project".to_string()], false, 10, 0)
918            .await
919            .unwrap();
920        assert_eq!(total_any, 2);
921        assert_eq!(all_matches, vec!["Vault/Alpha.md", "Vault/Beta.md"]);
922
923        let (match_all, total_all) = db
924            .search_notes_by_tags(&["project".to_string(), "team".to_string()], true, 10, 0)
925            .await
926            .unwrap();
927        assert_eq!(total_all, 1);
928        assert_eq!(match_all, vec!["Vault/Beta.md"]);
929
930        let tags = db.get_all_tags().await.unwrap();
931        assert_eq!(tags, vec!["alpha", "inline", "project", "team"]);
932
933        let keys = db.get_all_property_keys().await.unwrap();
934        assert_eq!(keys, vec!["owner", "status", "tags"]);
935    }
936}

error.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib/
error.rs

1#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
2pub enum TessellumError {
3	#[error("Database error: {0}")]
4	Database(#[from] sqlx::Error),
5	#[error("File not found: {0}")]
6	NotFound(String),
7	#[error("Validation error: {0}")]
8	Validation(String),
9	#[error("I/O error: {0}")]
10	Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
11	#[error("Internal error: {0}")]
12	Internal(String),
13}
14
15impl From<TessellumError> for tauri::ipc::InvokeError {
16	fn from(e: TessellumError) -> Self {
17		tauri::ipc::InvokeError::from(e.to_string())
18	}
19}
grafeo_projection.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib/
grafeo_projection.rs

1// Direct Grafeo integration for graph database features
2
3use grafeo::{GrafeoDB, Config};
4use serde_json::Value;
5use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
6use std::sync::{Mutex, OnceLock};
7
8// Static database connection shared across the application
9static GRAFEO_DB: OnceLock<GrafeoDB> = OnceLock::new();
10
11// Type alias for managed Grafeo connection
12pub type ManagedGrafeoConnection = Mutex<()>;
13
14pub fn title_from_note_id(note_id: &str) -> String {
15    Path::new(note_id)
16        .file_name()
17        .unwrap_or_default()
18        .to_string_lossy()
19        .trim_end_matches(".md")
20        .to_string()
21}
22
23/// Initialize the Grafeo database connection
24pub fn init_connection(db_path: PathBuf) -> Result<(), String> {
25    if GRAFEO_DB.get().is_some() {
26        return Ok(());
27    }
28
29    // Ensure parent directory exists
30    if let Some(parent) = db_path.parent() {
31        std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)
32            .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to create Grafeo database directory: {}", e))?;
33    }
34
35    // Create configuration for Grafeo database
36    let mut config = Config::default();
37    config.path = Some(db_path);
38
39    // Create or open the Grafeo database
40    let db = GrafeoDB::with_config(config)
41        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to initialize Grafeo database: {}", e))?;
42
43    // Test connection with a simple query
44    {
45        let session = db.session();
46        session.execute("RETURN 1 AS test")
47            .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to test Grafeo connection: {}", e))?;
48    }
49
50    GRAFEO_DB.set(db)
51        .map_err(|_| "Failed to set Grafeo database (already initialized)".to_string())?;
52
53    Ok(())
54}
55
56fn get_database() -> Result<&'static GrafeoDB, String> {
57    GRAFEO_DB.get()
58        .ok_or_else(|| "Grafeo database not initialized".to_string())
59}
60
61// Graph sync functions
62
63pub async fn sync_note_upsert(
64    _connection: &Mutex<()>,
65    sqlite: &crate::db::Database,
66    note_id: &str,
67) -> Result<(), String> {
68    let title = title_from_note_id(note_id);
69
70    // Fetch tags from SQLite
71    let tags = sqlite.get_file_tags(note_id)
72        .await
73        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to fetch tags from SQLite: {}", e))?;
74
75    let db = get_database()?;
76    let session = db.session();
77
78    // Escape single quotes in parameters
79    let escaped_id = note_id.replace('\'', "\\'");
80    let escaped_title = title.replace('\'', "\\'");
81
82    // Convert tags to GQL array format: ['tag1', 'tag2']
83    let tags_array = if tags.is_empty() {
84        "[]".to_string()
85    } else {
86        let escaped_tags: Vec<String> = tags
87            .iter()
88            .map(|t| format!("'{}'", t.replace('\'', "\\'")))
89            .collect();
90        format!("[{}]", escaped_tags.join(", "))
91    };
92
93    // Create or update note node with tags using MERGE
94    let gql = format!(
95        "MERGE (n:Note {{id: '{}'}}) SET n.title = '{}', n.tags = {} RETURN n",
96        escaped_id, escaped_title, tags_array
97    );
98
99    session.execute(&gql)
100        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to upsert note: {}", e))?;
101
102    Ok(())
103}
104
105pub fn sync_link_create(_connection: &Mutex<()>, from_id: &str, to_id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
106    let db = get_database()?;
107    let session = db.session();
108
109    let escaped_from = from_id.replace('\'', "\\'");
110    let escaped_to = to_id.replace('\'', "\\'");
111
112    // Create link between two notes
113    let gql = format!(
114        "MERGE (from:Note {{id: '{}'}}) \
115         MERGE (to:Note {{id: '{}'}}) \
116         MERGE (from)-[r:LINKS_TO]->(to) \
117         RETURN r",
118        escaped_from, escaped_to
119    );
120
121    session.execute(&gql)
122        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to create link: {}", e))?;
123
124    Ok(())
125}
126
127pub fn sync_link_delete(_connection: &Mutex<()>, from_id: &str, to_id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
128    let db = get_database()?;
129    let session = db.session();
130
131    let escaped_from = from_id.replace('\'', "\\'");
132    let escaped_to = to_id.replace('\'', "\\'");
133
134    // Delete the specific link between two notes
135    let gql = format!(
136        "MATCH (from:Note {{id: '{}'}})- [r:LINKS_TO]->(to:Note {{id: '{}'}}) \
137         DELETE r",
138        escaped_from, escaped_to
139    );
140
141    session.execute(&gql)
142        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to delete link: {}", e))?;
143
144    Ok(())
145}
146
147pub fn sync_note_delete(_connection: &Mutex<()>, note_id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
148    let db = get_database()?;
149    let session = db.session();
150
151    let escaped_id = note_id.replace('\'', "\\'");
152
153    // Delete note node and all its relationships
154    let gql = format!(
155        "MATCH (n:Note {{id: '{}'}}) DETACH DELETE n",
156        escaped_id
157    );
158
159    session.execute(&gql)
160        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to delete note: {}", e))?;
161
162    Ok(())
163}
164
165pub async fn sync_full(_connection: &Mutex<()>, sqlite: &crate::db::Database) -> Result<(), String> {
166    // Fetch all notes from SQLite and sync them to Grafeo
167    let indexed_files = sqlite.get_all_indexed_files()
168        .await
169        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to fetch notes from SQLite: {}", e))?;
170
171    for (note_path, _modified) in indexed_files {
172        let _title = title_from_note_id(&note_path);
173        // Best-effort sync - log errors but continue
174        if let Err(e) = sync_note_upsert(&Mutex::new(()), sqlite, &note_path).await {
175            log::warn!("Failed to sync note {}: {}", note_path, e);
176        }
177    }
178
179    // Fetch all links from SQLite and sync them to Grafeo
180    let links = sqlite.get_all_links()
181        .await
182        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to fetch links from SQLite: {}", e))?;
183
184    for (from_path, to_path) in links {
185        // Best-effort sync - log errors but continue
186        if let Err(e) = sync_link_create(&Mutex::new(()), &from_path, &to_path) {
187            log::warn!("Failed to sync link {} -> {}: {}", from_path, to_path, e);
188        }
189    }
190
191    Ok(())
192}
193
194/// Execute a GQL/Cypher query and return results as JSON
195pub fn execute_query(query: &str) -> Result<Value, String> {
196    let db = get_database()?;
197    let session = db.session();
198
199    let result = session.execute(query)
200        .map_err(|e| format!("Query execution failed: {}", e))?;
201
202    // Convert Grafeo QueryResult to JSON
203    // Frontend expects an array of objects with column names as keys
204    let mut json_rows = Vec::new();
205
206    for row in result.rows() {
207        let mut json_row = serde_json::Map::new();
208
209        for (i, column_name) in result.columns.iter().enumerate() {
210            if let Some(value) = row.get(i) {
211                json_row.insert(column_name.clone(), grafeo_value_to_json(value));
212            }
213        }
214
215        json_rows.push(Value::Object(json_row));
216    }
217
218    Ok(Value::Array(json_rows))
219}
220
221/// Convert Grafeo Value to serde_json Value
222fn grafeo_value_to_json(value: &grafeo::Value) -> Value {
223    use grafeo::Value as GV;
224
225    match value {
226        GV::Null => Value::Null,
227        GV::Bool(b) => Value::Bool(*b),
228        GV::Int64(i) => Value::Number((*i).into()),
229        GV::Float64(f) => {
230            serde_json::Number::from_f64(*f)
231                .map(Value::Number)
232                .unwrap_or(Value::Null)
233        }
234        GV::String(s) => Value::String(s.to_string()),
235        GV::List(list) => {
236            Value::Array(list.iter().map(grafeo_value_to_json).collect())
237        }
238        GV::Map(map) => {
239            let mut json_map = serde_json::Map::new();
240            for (k, v) in map.iter() {
241                json_map.insert(k.to_string(), grafeo_value_to_json(v));
242            }
243            Value::Object(json_map)
244        }
245        GV::Path { nodes, edges } => {
246            // Convert path to a JSON object with nodes and edges arrays
247            let mut path_map = serde_json::Map::new();
248            path_map.insert(
249                "nodes".to_string(),
250                Value::Array(nodes.iter().map(grafeo_value_to_json).collect()),
251            );
252            path_map.insert(
253                "edges".to_string(),
254                Value::Array(edges.iter().map(grafeo_value_to_json).collect()),
255            );
256            Value::Object(path_map)
257        }
258        GV::Bytes(bytes) => {
259            // Convert bytes to hex string for JSON compatibility
260            let hex_string: String = bytes.iter()
261                .map(|b| format!("{:02x}", b))
262                .collect();
263            Value::String(format!("hex:{}", hex_string))
264        }
265        // For other complex types (timestamps, dates, etc.), convert to string representation
266        _ => Value::String(format!("{:?}", value)),
267    }
268}
269
270#[cfg(test)]
271mod tests {
272    use super::{execute_query, title_from_note_id};
273
274    #[test]
275    fn derives_titles_from_note_paths() {
276        assert_eq!(title_from_note_id("Vault/Projects/Plan.md"), "Plan");
277        assert_eq!(title_from_note_id("Loose Note.md"), "Loose Note");
278    }
279
280    #[test]
281    fn execute_query_reports_missing_initialization() {
282        let err = execute_query("RETURN 1").unwrap_err();
283
284        assert!(err.contains("not initialized"));
285    }
286}
