Files

tessellum_lib/
indexer.rs

1use std::collections::HashMap;
2use std::fs;
3use std::path::Path;
4use std::time::{Instant, UNIX_EPOCH};
5use walkdir::WalkDir;
6
7use crate::commands::extract_wikilinks;
8use crate::db::Database;
9use crate::models::FileIndex;
10use crate::search::SearchDoc;
11use crate::search::SearchIndex;
12use crate::utils::{extract_tags, is_hidden_or_special};
13
14/// Statistics about the indexing operation.
15#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
16pub struct IndexStats {
17    pub files_indexed: usize,
18    pub files_deleted: usize,
19    pub files_skipped: usize,
20    pub duration_ms: u128,
21}
22
23/// Vault indexer for syncing database with filesystem.
24pub struct VaultIndexer;
25
26impl VaultIndexer {
27    /// Perform a full sync of the vault with the database.
28    ///
29    /// This function:
30    /// 1. Walks the vault directory to find all .md files
31    /// 2. Compares with the database to find new/modified/deleted files
32    /// 3. Indexes new and modified files
33    /// 4. Removes deleted files from the database
34    pub async fn full_sync(
35        db: &Database,
36        search_index: std::sync::Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<SearchIndex>>,
37        vault_path: &str,
38    ) -> Result<IndexStats, String> {
39        let start = Instant::now();
40        
41        let mut files_indexed = 0;
42        let mut files_deleted = 0;
43        let mut files_skipped = 0;
44        
45        log::info!("Starting vault sync for: {}", vault_path);
46        
47        // 1. Get all files from filesystem with their modified times
48        let fs_files = Self::collect_filesystem_files(vault_path)?;
49        log::debug!("Found {} files in filesystem", fs_files.len());
50        
51        // 2. Get all indexed search files from database
52        let db_files: HashMap<String, (i64, bool)> = db
53            .get_all_search_files()
54            .await
55            .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to get indexed files: {}", e))?
56            .into_iter()
57            .map(|(path, modified, is_markdown)| (path, (modified, is_markdown != 0)))
58            .collect();
59        log::debug!("Found {} files in database", db_files.len());
60        
61        // 3. Build file index for link resolution
62        let file_index = FileIndex::build(vault_path)
63            .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to build file index: {}", e))?;
64        
65        // 4. Process each filesystem file
66        let mut docs_to_index: Vec<SearchDoc> = Vec::new();
67        
68        for (path, (modified_time, is_markdown)) in &fs_files {
69            let needs_index = match db_files.get(path) {
70                None => true,                                                 // New file
71                Some((db_modified, _)) => *modified_time > *db_modified, // Modified file
72            };
73            
74            if needs_index {
75                if *is_markdown {
76                    match Self::index_single_file(db, vault_path, path, &file_index, &mut docs_to_index).await {
77                        Ok(_) => files_indexed += 1,
78                        Err(e) => {
79                            log::warn!("Failed to index {}: {}", path, e);
80                        }
81                    }
82                } else {
83                    let title = Path::new(path)
84                        .file_name()
85                        .unwrap_or_default()
86                        .to_string_lossy()
87                        .to_string();
88                    docs_to_index.push(SearchDoc {
89                        path: path.clone(),
90                        title,
91                        body: String::new(),
92                        tags: Vec::new(),
93                    });
94                    db.upsert_search_file(path, *modified_time, false)
95                        .await
96                        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to update search file: {}", e))?;
97                    files_indexed += 1;
98                }
99            } else {
100                files_skipped += 1;
101            }
102        }
103        
104        // 5. Find and delete files that no longer exist
105        let fs_paths: std::collections::HashSet<&String> = fs_files.keys().collect();
106        let deleted_paths: Vec<String> = db_files
107            .keys()
108            .filter(|p| !fs_paths.contains(p))
109            .cloned()
110            .collect();
111        
112        if !deleted_paths.is_empty() {
113            log::debug!("Removing {} deleted files from index", deleted_paths.len());
114            let mut markdown_deleted: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
115            for path in &deleted_paths {
116                if let Some((_, is_md)) = db_files.get(path) {
117                    if *is_md {
118                        markdown_deleted.push(path.clone());
119                    }
120                }
121            }
122            if !markdown_deleted.is_empty() {
123                files_deleted = db
124                    .batch_delete_files(&markdown_deleted)
125                    .await
126                    .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to delete files: {}", e))?;
127            }
128            db.delete_search_files(&deleted_paths)
129                .await
130                .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to delete search files: {}", e))?;
131        }
132        
133        // Update search index in batch
134        if !docs_to_index.is_empty() || !deleted_paths.is_empty() {
135            let docs = docs_to_index.clone();
136            let deletes = deleted_paths.clone();
137            tauri::async_runtime::spawn_blocking(move || {
138                let guard = tauri::async_runtime::block_on(search_index.lock());
139                guard
140                    .index_batch(&docs, &deletes)
141                    .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to update search index: {}", e))
142            })
143                .await
144                .map_err(|e| format!("Search index task failed: {}", e))??;
145        }
146        
147        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis();
148        
149        log::info!(
150            "Vault sync complete in {}ms: {} indexed, {} skipped, {} deleted",
151            duration_ms,
152            files_indexed,
153            files_skipped,
154            files_deleted
155        );
156        
157        Ok(IndexStats {
158            files_indexed,
159            files_deleted,
160            files_skipped,
161            duration_ms,
162        })
163    }
164    
165    /// Collect all files from the filesystem with their modified times.
166    fn collect_filesystem_files(
167        vault_path: &str,
168    ) -> Result<HashMap<String, (i64, bool)>, String> {
169        let mut files = HashMap::new();
170        
171        if !Path::new(vault_path).exists() {
172            return Err("Vault path does not exist".to_string());
173        }
174        
175        for entry in WalkDir::new(vault_path).into_iter().filter_map(|e| e.ok()) {
176            let path = entry.path();
177            let path_str = path.to_string_lossy().to_string();
178            
179            // Skip hidden files/dirs (.git, .trash, etc.)
180            let rel_path = path.strip_prefix(vault_path).unwrap_or(path);
181            if is_hidden_or_special(rel_path) {
182                continue;
183            }
184            
185            if path.is_file() {
186                let is_markdown = path.extension().and_then(|s| s.to_str()) == Some("md");
187                if let Ok(metadata) = fs::metadata(path) {
188                    let modified_time = metadata
189                        .modified()
190                        .unwrap_or(UNIX_EPOCH)
191                        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
192                        .unwrap_or_default()
193                        .as_secs() as i64;
194                    
195                    files.insert(crate::utils::normalize_path(&path_str), (modified_time, is_markdown));
196                }
197            }
198        }
199        
200        Ok(files)
201    }
202    
203    /// Index a single file: read content, extract links, update database.
204    async fn index_single_file(
205        db: &Database,
206        vault_path: &str,
207        file_path: &str,
208        file_index: &FileIndex,
209        docs_to_index: &mut Vec<SearchDoc>,
210    ) -> Result<(), String> {
211        // Read file content
212        let content =
213            fs::read_to_string(file_path).map_err(|e| format!("Failed to read file: {}", e))?;
214        
215        // Get file metadata
216        let metadata =
217            fs::metadata(file_path).map_err(|e| format!("Failed to get metadata: {}", e))?;
218        
219        let size = metadata.len();
220        let modified = metadata
221            .modified()
222            .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to get modified time: {}", e))?
223            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
224            .unwrap_or_default()
225            .as_secs() as i64;
226        
227        // Parse frontmatter
228        let mut frontmatter_json_str = None;
229        let mut body_content = content.as_str();
230        
231        if let Some((yaml, _)) = crate::utils::frontmatter::parse_frontmatter(&content) {
232            body_content = crate::utils::frontmatter::strip_frontmatter(&content);
233            if let Ok(json) = crate::utils::frontmatter::frontmatter_to_json(&yaml) {
234                frontmatter_json_str = Some(json);
235            }
236        }
237        
238        let inline_tags = extract_tags(&content);
239        
240        let inline_tags_json_str = if inline_tags.is_empty() {
241            None
242        } else {
243            serde_json::to_string(&inline_tags).ok()
244        };
245        
246        let wikilinks = extract_wikilinks(body_content);
247        let resolved_links: Vec<String> = wikilinks
248            .iter()
249            .map(|link| {
250                crate::utils::normalize_path(
251                    &file_index
252                        .resolve_or_default(vault_path, &link.target)
253                        .to_string_lossy(),
254                )
255            })
256            .collect();
257        
258        let normalized_path = crate::utils::normalize_path(file_path);
259        // Index the file
260        db.index_file(
261            &normalized_path,
262            modified,
263            size,
264            frontmatter_json_str.as_deref(),
265            inline_tags_json_str.as_deref(),
266            &resolved_links,
267        )
268            .await
269            .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to index file: {}", e))?;
270        
271        db.set_note_tags(&normalized_path, &inline_tags)
272            .await
273            .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to update note tags: {}", e))?;
274        
275        db.upsert_search_file(&normalized_path, modified, true)
276            .await
277            .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to update search file: {}", e))?;
278        
279        let title = Path::new(file_path)
280            .file_name()
281            .unwrap_or_default()
282            .to_string_lossy()
283            .to_string()
284            .trim_end_matches(".md")
285            .to_string();
286        
287        docs_to_index.push(SearchDoc {
288            path: normalized_path,
289            title,
290            body: body_content.to_string(),
291            tags: inline_tags,
292        });
293        
294        Ok(())
295    }
296}
297
298#[cfg(test)]
299mod tests {
300    use std::sync::Arc;
301
302    use tempfile::tempdir;
303    use tokio::sync::Mutex;
304
305    use super::VaultIndexer;
306    use crate::db::Database;
307    use crate::search::SearchIndex;
308    use crate::test_support::TestVault;
309
310    #[test]
311    fn collects_filesystem_files_skipping_hidden_entries() {
312        let vault = TestVault::new()
313            .with_markdown("Inbox/Note.md", "# Note")
314            .with_markdown(".trash/Hidden.md", "# Hidden")
315            .build();
316        std::fs::write(vault.path().join("image.png"), "png").unwrap();
317        let note_path = crate::utils::normalize_path(&vault.path().join("Inbox/Note.md").to_string_lossy());
318        let image_path = crate::utils::normalize_path(&vault.path().join("image.png").to_string_lossy());
319        let hidden_path = crate::utils::normalize_path(&vault.path().join(".trash/Hidden.md").to_string_lossy());
320
321        let files = VaultIndexer::collect_filesystem_files(vault.path().to_str().unwrap()).unwrap();
322
323        assert!(files.contains_key(&note_path));
324        assert!(files.contains_key(&image_path));
325        assert!(!files.contains_key(&hidden_path));
326        assert_eq!(files[&note_path].1, true);
327        assert_eq!(files[&image_path].1, false);
328    }
329
330    #[tokio::test]
331    async fn full_sync_indexes_new_files_and_removes_deleted_entries() {
332        let vault = TestVault::new()
333            .with_markdown("Inbox/Alpha.md", "# Alpha\n[[Beta]]")
334            .with_markdown("Inbox/Beta.md", "# Beta")
335            .build();
336        let db_dir = tempdir().unwrap();
337        let db = Database::init(db_dir.path().join("indexer.sqlite").to_str().unwrap())
338            .await
339            .unwrap();
340        let search_dir = tempdir().unwrap();
341        let search_index = Arc::new(Mutex::new(SearchIndex::open_or_create(&search_dir.path().join("search-index")).unwrap()));
342
343        let first = VaultIndexer::full_sync(
344            &db,
345            search_index.clone(),
346            vault.path().to_str().unwrap(),
347        )
348        .await
349        .unwrap();
350        assert_eq!(first.files_indexed, 2);
351        assert_eq!(first.files_deleted, 0);
352
353        std::fs::remove_file(vault.path().join("Inbox/Beta.md")).unwrap();
354
355        let second = VaultIndexer::full_sync(
356            &db,
357            search_index,
358            vault.path().to_str().unwrap(),
359        )
360        .await
361        .unwrap();
362        assert_eq!(second.files_deleted, 1);
363
364        let indexed = db.get_all_indexed_files().await.unwrap();
365        assert_eq!(indexed.len(), 1);
366        assert_eq!(indexed[0].0, crate::utils::normalize_path(&vault.path().join("Inbox/Alpha.md").to_string_lossy()));
367    }
368}

tessellum_lib/
lib.rs

1pub mod commands;
2mod db;
3pub mod error;
4mod indexer;
5mod grafeo_projection;
6mod search;
7mod trash;
8pub mod models;
9mod utils;
10#[cfg(test)]
11mod test_support;
12
13use db::Database;
14use std::fs::{create_dir_all, OpenOptions};
15use std::io::Write;
16use std::path::PathBuf;
17use std::sync::Mutex;
18use tauri::Manager;
19pub use models::*;
20use search::SearchIndex;
21
22fn startup_error(stage: &str, message: impl Into<String>) -> std::io::Error {
23    std::io::Error::other(format!("startup failed at {stage}: {}", message.into()))
24}
25
26fn startup_log_path() -> PathBuf {
27    if let Ok(local_app_data) = std::env::var("LOCALAPPDATA") {
28        return PathBuf::from(local_app_data).join("tessellum").join("startup.log");
29    }
30    std::env::temp_dir().join("tessellum-startup.log")
31}
32
33fn append_startup_log(message: &str) {
34    let path = startup_log_path();
35    if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
36        let _ = create_dir_all(parent);
37    }
38    if let Ok(mut file) = OpenOptions::new().create(true).append(true).open(path) {
39        let _ = writeln!(file, "{message}");
40    }
41}
42
43#[cfg_attr(mobile, tauri::mobile_entry_point)]
44pub fn run() {
45    tauri::Builder::default()
46        .plugin(tauri_plugin_fs::init())
47        .plugin(tauri_plugin_opener::init())
48        .plugin(tauri_plugin_dialog::init())
49        .plugin(tauri_plugin_persisted_scope::init())
50        .setup(|app| {
51            let setup_result: Result<(), std::io::Error> = (|| {
52                let app_handle = app.handle();
53                let app_data_dir = app_handle
54                    .path()
55                    .app_data_dir()
56                    .map_err(|e| startup_error("resolve-app-data-dir", e.to_string()))?;
57
58                append_startup_log(&format!(
59                    "resolved app_data_dir: {}",
60                    app_data_dir.display()
61                ));
62
63                create_dir_all(&app_data_dir)
64                    .map_err(|e| startup_error("ensure-app-data-dir", e.to_string()))?;
65
66                let write_probe = app_data_dir.join(".startup-write-probe");
67                OpenOptions::new()
68                    .create(true)
69                    .write(true)
70                    .truncate(true)
71                    .open(&write_probe)
72                    .map_err(|e| {
73                        startup_error(
74                            "app-data-write-test",
75                            format!("{} (path: {})", e, write_probe.display()),
76                        )
77                    })?;
78                let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&write_probe);
79
80                let db_url = app_data_dir
81                    .join("vault.db")
82                    .to_str()
83                    .ok_or_else(|| startup_error("build-db-path", "failed to convert path to string"))?
84                    .to_string();
85
86                append_startup_log(&format!("resolved db_url: {db_url}"));
87
88                let db_url_for_init = db_url.clone();
89                let db_instance = tauri::async_runtime::block_on(async move {
90                    Database::init(&db_url_for_init).await
91                })
92                .map_err(|e| {
93                    startup_error(
94                        "database-init",
95                        format!("{} (db: {})", e, db_url),
96                    )
97                })?;
98
99                let search_dir = app_data_dir.join("search_index");
100
101                let search_index = SearchIndex::open_or_create(&search_dir)
102                    .map_err(|e| startup_error("search-index-init", e.to_string()))?;
103
104                // Manage graph connection state
105                app.manage(Mutex::new(()));
106
107                let app_state = models::AppState::new(db_instance, search_index);
108                let db_for_sync = app_state.db.clone();
109                app.manage(app_state);
110
111                // Initialize Grafeo graph database directly
112                let graph_db_path = app_data_dir.join("graph.grafeo");
113                match grafeo_projection::init_connection(graph_db_path) {
114                    Ok(_) => {
115                        log::info!("Grafeo graph database initialized successfully");
116
117                        // Perform initial sync on startup in background
118                        let grafeo_conn = std::sync::Mutex::new(());
119                        tauri::async_runtime::spawn(async move {
120                            match grafeo_projection::sync_full(&grafeo_conn, db_for_sync.as_ref()).await {
121                                Ok(_) => {
122                                    log::info!("Initial Grafeo sync completed");
123                                }
124                                Err(e) => {
125                                    log::warn!("Initial Grafeo sync failed: {}", e);
126                                }
127                            }
128                        });
129                    }
130                    Err(e) => {
131                        log::warn!("Grafeo graph database initialization failed: {}", e);
132                    }
133                }
134
135                log::info!("Database and graph initialized successfully");
136
137                Ok(())
138            })();
139
140            if let Err(err) = &setup_result {
141                let message = format!("{err}");
142                append_startup_log(&message);
143                eprintln!("{message}");
144            }
145
146            setup_result.map_err(|e| e.into())
147        })
148        .invoke_handler(tauri::generate_handler![
149            commands::notes::create_note,
150            commands::notes::get_or_create_daily_note,
151            commands::notes::trash_item,
152            commands::notes::trash_items,
153            commands::notes::list_trash_items,
154            commands::notes::restore_trash_item,
155            commands::notes::delete_trash_item_permanently,
156            commands::notes::read_file,
157            commands::notes::write_file,
158            commands::notes::search_notes,
159            commands::templates::list_templates,
160            commands::templates::create_note_from_template,
161            commands::vault::list_files,
162            commands::vault::list_files_tree,
163            commands::vault::ensure_feature_demo_in_empty_vault,
164            commands::clipboard::import_clipboard_files,
165            commands::clipboard::write_file_paths_to_clipboard,
166            commands::watcher::watch_vault,
167            commands::watcher::unwatch_vault,
168            commands::vault::rename_file,
169            commands::vault::move_items,
170            commands::folders::create_folder,
171            commands::links::get_backlinks,
172            commands::links::get_outgoing_links,
173            commands::links::get_all_links,
174            commands::links::resolve_wikilink,
175            commands::assets::resolve_asset,
176            commands::assets::save_asset,
177            commands::notes::get_all_notes,
178            commands::notes::get_all_tags,
179            commands::notes::get_file_tags,
180            commands::notes::get_all_property_keys,
181            commands::pdf_export::export_markdown_pdf,
182            commands::indexer::sync_vault,
183            commands::graph::get_graph_data,
184            commands::graph::execute_graph_query,
185            commands::vault::set_vault_path,
186            commands::search::search_full_text,
187            commands::search::search_tags,
188            commands::search::rebuild_search_index,
189            commands::search::ensure_search_ready,
190            commands::search::get_search_readiness,
191            commands::search::reset_search_readiness_attempts,
192        ])
193        .run(tauri::generate_context!())
194        .unwrap_or_else(|e| {
195            let message = format!("error while running tauri application: {}", e);
196            log::error!("{message}");
197            append_startup_log(&message);
198            eprintln!("{message}");
199        });
200}
201
202#[cfg(test)]
203mod tests {
204    use super::*;
205    use std::fs;
206    use tempfile::tempdir;
207    
208    use commands::extract_wikilinks;
209    use models::{AssetIndex, FileIndex};
210    
211    #[test]
212    fn test_extract_wikilinks() {
213        let content = "Check out [[Note 1]] and [[folder/Note 2|Custom Text]]\
214        . Also \\[[escaped]].";
215        let links = extract_wikilinks(content);
216        
217        assert_eq!(links.len(), 2);
218        assert_eq!(links[0].target, "Note 1");
219        assert_eq!(links[0].alias, None);
220        assert_eq!(links[1].target, "folder/Note 2");
221        assert_eq!(links[1].alias, Some("Custom Text".to_string()));
222    }
223    
224    #[test]
225    fn test_file_index_resolution() {
226        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
227        let vault_path = dir.path().to_str().unwrap();
228        
229        // Create test structure
230        fs::write(dir.path().join("Note1.md"), "content").unwrap();
231        
232        let subfolder = dir.path().join("subfolder");
233        fs::create_dir(&subfolder).unwrap();
234        fs::write(subfolder.join("Note2.md"), "content").unwrap();
235        fs::write(subfolder.join("Note1.md"), "duplicate name").unwrap();
236        
237        let index = FileIndex::build(vault_path).unwrap();
238        
239        // Test simple resolution
240        let resolved = index.resolve(vault_path, "Note2");
241        assert!(resolved.is_some());
242        assert!(resolved.unwrap().ends_with("Note2.md"));
243        
244        // Test that Note1 resolves to the one closest to root (shortest path)
245        let resolved = index.resolve(vault_path, "Note1");
246        assert!(resolved.is_some());
247        let path = resolved.unwrap();
248        assert!(path.ends_with("Note1.md"));
249        assert!(!path.to_string_lossy().contains("subfolder"));
250        
251        // Test path-based resolution
252        let resolved = index.resolve(vault_path, "subfolder/Note1");
253        assert!(resolved.is_some());
254        assert!(resolved.unwrap().to_string_lossy().contains("subfolder"));
255    }
256
257    #[test]
258    fn test_asset_index_resolution() {
259        let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
260        let vault_path = dir.path().to_str().unwrap();
261
262        fs::write(dir.path().join("cover.png"), b"png").unwrap();
263
264        let media = dir.path().join("media");
265        fs::create_dir(&media).unwrap();
266        fs::write(media.join("diagram.pdf"), b"pdf").unwrap();
267
268        let index = AssetIndex::build(vault_path).unwrap();
269
270        let resolved_cover = index.resolve(vault_path, "cover.png");
271        assert!(resolved_cover.is_some());
272        assert!(resolved_cover.unwrap().ends_with("cover.png"));
273
274        let resolved_pdf = index.resolve(vault_path, "media/diagram");
275        assert!(resolved_pdf.is_some());
276        assert!(resolved_pdf.unwrap().to_string_lossy().contains("media/diagram.pdf"));
277    }
278}

Files

tessellum_lib/
search.rs

1use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
2
3use regex::Regex;
4use tantivy::collector::{DocSetCollector, TopDocs};
5use tantivy::query::{AllQuery, BooleanQuery, BoostQuery, Occur, PhrasePrefixQuery, Query, TermQuery};
6use tantivy::schema::{Facet, Field, IndexRecordOption, STORED, STRING, Schema, TEXT, Value};
7use tantivy::{Index, IndexReader, IndexWriter, ReloadPolicy, TantivyDocument, Term};
8
9use crate::utils::normalize_path;
10
11pub struct SearchIndex {
12	index: Index,
13	reader: IndexReader,
14	fields: SearchFields,
15	index_dir: PathBuf,
16}
17
18#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
19pub struct SearchFields {
20	pub path: Field,
21	pub title: Field,
22	pub body: Field,
23	pub tags: Field,
24}
25
26#[derive(Clone)]
27pub struct SearchDoc {
28	pub path: String,
29	pub title: String,
30	pub body: String,
31	pub tags: Vec<String>,
32}
33
34impl SearchIndex {
35	pub fn open_or_create(index_dir: &Path) -> Result<Self, String> {
36		let (schema, fields) = Self::build_schema();
37		let index = if index_dir.exists() {
38			Index::open_in_dir(index_dir).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?
39		} else {
40			std::fs::create_dir_all(index_dir).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
41			Index::create_in_dir(index_dir, schema).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?
42		};
43		
44		let reader = index
45			.reader_builder()
46			.reload_policy(ReloadPolicy::Manual)
47			.try_into()
48			.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
49		
50		Ok(Self {
51			index,
52			reader,
53			fields,
54			index_dir: index_dir.to_path_buf(),
55		})
56	}
57	
58	pub fn index_dir(&self) -> &Path {
59		&self.index_dir
60	}
61	
62	pub fn clear(&self) -> Result<(), String> {
63		let mut writer = self.writer()?;
64		writer.delete_all_documents().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
65		writer.commit().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
66		self.reader.reload().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
67		Ok(())
68	}
69	
70	pub fn index_batch(&self, docs: &[SearchDoc], delete_paths: &[String]) -> Result<(), String> {
71		let mut writer = self.writer()?;
72		for path in delete_paths {
73			self.delete_path_with_writer(&mut writer, path)?;
74		}
75		for doc in docs {
76			self.delete_path_with_writer(&mut writer, &doc.path)?;
77			let mut document = TantivyDocument::default();
78			document.add_text(self.fields.path, &doc.path);
79			document.add_text(self.fields.title, &doc.title);
80			document.add_text(self.fields.body, &doc.body);
81			for tag in &doc.tags {
82				let facet = Facet::from(&format!("/{}", tag));
83				document.add_facet(self.fields.tags, facet);
84			}
85			writer.add_document(document).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
86		}
87		writer.commit().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
88		self.reader.reload().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
89		Ok(())
90	}
91	
92	pub fn delete_path(&self, path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
93		let mut writer = self.writer()?;
94		self.delete_path_with_writer(&mut writer, path)?;
95		writer.commit().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
96		self.reader.reload().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
97		Ok(())
98	}
99	
100	pub fn search(
101		&self,
102		query: &str,
103		tags: &[String],
104		match_all_tags: bool,
105		limit: usize,
106		offset: usize,
107	) -> Result<Vec<(SearchDoc, f32)>, String> {
108		let reader = self.reader.searcher();
109		let normalized = tokenize_query(query);
110		
111		let text_query: Option<Box<dyn Query>> = if normalized.is_empty() {
112			None
113		} else {
114			let mut clauses: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn Query>)> = Vec::new();
115			for term in normalized {
116				let title_term = Term::from_field_text(self.fields.title, &term);
117				let body_term = Term::from_field_text(self.fields.body, &term);
118				let title_query = PhrasePrefixQuery::new(vec![title_term]);
119				let body_query = PhrasePrefixQuery::new(vec![body_term]);
120				let boosted = BoostQuery::new(Box::new(title_query), 2.0);
121				let should = BooleanQuery::new(vec![
122					(Occur::Should, Box::new(boosted)),
123					(Occur::Should, Box::new(body_query)),
124				]);
125				clauses.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(should)));
126			}
127			Some(Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(clauses)))
128		};
129		
130		let tag_query: Option<Box<dyn Query>> = if !tags.is_empty() {
131			let mut tag_clauses = Vec::new();
132			for tag in tags {
133				let facet = Facet::from(&format!("/{}", tag));
134				let term = Term::from_facet(self.fields.tags, &facet);
135				let term_query = TermQuery::new(term, IndexRecordOption::Basic);
136				tag_clauses.push((
137					if match_all_tags {
138						tantivy::query::Occur::Must
139					} else {
140						tantivy::query::Occur::Should
141					},
142					Box::new(term_query) as Box<dyn Query>,
143				));
144			}
145			Some(Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(tag_clauses)))
146		} else {
147			None
148		};
149		
150		let query: Box<dyn Query> = match (text_query, tag_query) {
151			(Some(text), Some(tags)) => Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(vec![
152				(tantivy::query::Occur::Must, text),
153				(tantivy::query::Occur::Must, tags),
154			])),
155			(Some(text), None) => text,
156			(None, Some(tags)) => tags,
157			(None, None) => return Ok(Vec::new()),
158		};
159		let top_docs = reader
160			.search(&query, &TopDocs::with_limit(limit + offset))
161			.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
162		
163		let mut results = Vec::new();
164		for (score, address) in top_docs.into_iter().skip(offset).take(limit) {
165			let retrieved: TantivyDocument = reader.doc(address).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
166			let path = retrieved
167				.get_first(self.fields.path)
168				.and_then(|v| v.as_str())
169				.unwrap_or_default()
170				.to_string();
171			let title = retrieved
172				.get_first(self.fields.title)
173				.and_then(|v| v.as_str())
174				.unwrap_or_default()
175				.to_string();
176			let body = retrieved
177				.get_first(self.fields.body)
178				.and_then(|v| v.as_str())
179				.unwrap_or_default()
180				.to_string();
181			
182			let mut tag_values = Vec::new();
183			for value in retrieved.get_all(self.fields.tags) {
184				if let Some(facet) = value.as_facet() {
185					let raw = facet.to_string();
186					let trimmed = raw.trim_start_matches('/');
187					if !trimmed.is_empty() {
188						tag_values.push(trimmed.to_string());
189					}
190				}
191			}
192			
193			results.push((
194				SearchDoc {
195					path: normalize_path(&path),
196					title,
197					body,
198					tags: tag_values,
199				},
200				score,
201			));
202		}
203		
204		Ok(results)
205	}
206
207	pub fn indexed_paths(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>, String> {
208		let reader = self.reader.searcher();
209		let doc_addresses = reader
210			.search(&AllQuery, &DocSetCollector)
211			.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
212		let mut paths = Vec::with_capacity(doc_addresses.len());
213		for address in doc_addresses {
214			let retrieved: TantivyDocument = reader.doc(address).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
215			if let Some(path) = retrieved
216				.get_first(self.fields.path)
217				.and_then(|value| value.as_str())
218			{
219				paths.push(normalize_path(path));
220			}
221		}
222		Ok(paths)
223	}
224	
225	fn build_schema() -> (Schema, SearchFields) {
226		let mut builder = Schema::builder();
227		let path = builder.add_text_field("path", STRING | STORED);
228		let title = builder.add_text_field("title", TEXT | STORED);
229		let body = builder.add_text_field("body", TEXT | STORED);
230		let tags = builder.add_facet_field("tags", STORED);
231		let schema = builder.build();
232		(
233			schema,
234			SearchFields {
235				path,
236				title,
237				body,
238				tags,
239			},
240		)
241	}
242	
243	fn writer(&self) -> Result<IndexWriter, String> {
244		self.index.writer(50_000_000).map_err(|e| e.to_string())
245	}
246	
247	fn delete_path_with_writer(&self, writer: &mut IndexWriter, path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
248		let term = Term::from_field_text(self.fields.path, path);
249		writer.delete_term(term);
250		Ok(())
251	}
252}
253
254fn tokenize_query(query: &str) -> Vec<String> {
255	let regex = Regex::new(r"[A-Za-z0-9_-]+").unwrap();
256	let mut terms = Vec::new();
257	for cap in regex.captures_iter(query) {
258		if let Some(m) = cap.get(0) {
259			let term = m.as_str().to_lowercase();
260			if !term.is_empty() {
261				terms.push(term);
262			}
263		}
264	}
265	terms
266}
267
268#[cfg(test)]
269mod tests {
270	use tempfile::tempdir;
271
272	use super::{tokenize_query, SearchDoc, SearchIndex};
273
274	fn make_doc(path: &str, title: &str, body: &str, tags: &[&str]) -> SearchDoc {
275		SearchDoc {
276			path: path.to_string(),
277			title: title.to_string(),
278			body: body.to_string(),
279			tags: tags.iter().map(|tag| tag.to_string()).collect(),
280		}
281	}
282
283	#[test]
284	fn tokenizes_query_into_search_terms() {
285		let terms = tokenize_query("Alpha-beta, #tag and note_2");
286
287		assert_eq!(terms, vec!["alpha-beta", "tag", "and", "note_2"]);
288	}
289
290	#[test]
291	fn indexes_search_documents_and_filters_by_text_and_tags() {
292		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
293		let index = SearchIndex::open_or_create(&dir.path().join("search-index")).unwrap();
294		index
295			.index_batch(
296				&[
297					make_doc("Vault/Alpha.md", "Alpha Note", "project kickoff body", &["project", "work"]),
298					make_doc("Vault/Beta.md", "Beta", "meeting notes", &["meeting"]),
299				],
300				&[],
301			)
302			.unwrap();
303
304		let text_results = index.search("alpha", &[], false, 10, 0).unwrap();
305		assert_eq!(text_results.len(), 1);
306		assert_eq!(text_results[0].0.path, "Vault/Alpha.md");
307
308		let tag_results = index
309			.search("", &[String::from("meeting")], false, 10, 0)
310			.unwrap();
311		assert_eq!(tag_results.len(), 1);
312		assert_eq!(tag_results[0].0.path, "Vault/Beta.md");
313
314		let no_results = index
315			.search("unknown", &[String::from("project")], true, 10, 0)
316			.unwrap();
317		assert!(no_results.is_empty());
318	}
319
320	#[test]
321	fn clears_and_deletes_indexed_paths() {
322		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
323		let index = SearchIndex::open_or_create(&dir.path().join("search-index")).unwrap();
324		index
325			.index_batch(&[make_doc("Vault/Alpha.md", "Alpha", "body", &[])], &[])
326			.unwrap();
327		assert_eq!(index.indexed_paths().unwrap(), vec!["Vault/Alpha.md"]);
328
329		index.delete_path("Vault/Alpha.md").unwrap();
330		assert!(index.indexed_paths().unwrap().is_empty());
331
332		index
333			.index_batch(&[make_doc("Vault/Beta.md", "Beta", "body", &[])], &[])
334			.unwrap();
335		index.clear().unwrap();
336		assert!(index.indexed_paths().unwrap().is_empty());
337	}
338}
trash.rs - sourceSkip to main content

Files

tessellum_lib/
trash.rs

1use std::fs;
2use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
3use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};
4
5const TRASH_DIR_NAME: &str = ".trash";
6const MILLIS_PER_DAY: u128 = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
7
8#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
9pub struct PurgeReport {
10	pub deleted: usize,
11	pub skipped_invalid_name: usize,
12	pub errors: usize,
13}
14
15#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
16pub struct ParsedTrashName {
17	pub original_name: String,
18	pub parent_label: String,
19}
20
21/// Generates a unique trash name using the current naming format:
22/// "<stem-or-dirname> (<parent>) <timestamp>[.<original-ext>]"
23pub fn generate_trash_name(path: &Path, timestamp: u128) -> Option<String> {
24	let filename = path.file_name()?.to_string_lossy();
25	
26	let raw_parent = path
27		.parent()
28		.and_then(|p| p.file_name())
29		.map(|n| n.to_string_lossy())
30		.unwrap_or_else(|| "root".into());
31	
32	// Strip a previous trailing " (<something>)" chain to keep names readable.
33	let clean_parent = raw_parent.split(" (").next().unwrap_or(&raw_parent);
34	
35	if path.is_dir() {
36		Some(format!("{} ({}) {}", filename, clean_parent, timestamp))
37	} else {
38		let (stem, extension) = split_name_parts(&filename);
39		let suffix = extension.map(|ext| format!(".{ext}")).unwrap_or_default();
40		Some(format!("{} ({}) {}{}", stem, clean_parent, timestamp, suffix))
41	}
42}
43
44fn split_name_parts(name: &str) -> (&str, Option<&str>) {
45	match name.rsplit_once('.') {
46		Some((stem, ext)) if !stem.is_empty() => (stem, Some(ext)),
47		_ => (name, None),
48	}
49}
50
51fn with_collision_suffix(name: &str, collision_index: usize) -> String {
52	let (stem, extension) = split_name_parts(name);
53	match extension {
54		Some(ext) => format!("{stem} [{collision_index}].{ext}"),
55		None => format!("{stem} [{collision_index}]"),
56	}
57}
58
59fn strip_extension_for_parsing(name: &str) -> &str {
60	match name.rsplit_once('.') {
61		Some((stem, ext)) if !stem.is_empty() && !ext.contains(' ') => stem,
62		_ => name,
63	}
64}
65
66fn strip_collision_suffix_for_parsing(name: &str) -> &str {
67	match name.rsplit_once(" [") {
68		Some((stem, maybe_index)) if maybe_index.ends_with(']') => {
69			let digits = &maybe_index[..maybe_index.len() - 1];
70			if !digits.is_empty() && digits.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_digit()) {
71				return stem;
72			}
73			name
74		}
75		_ => name,
76	}
77}
78
79/// Builds a unique destination path inside `.trash`, avoiding name collisions when
80/// multiple items resolve to the same trash name.
81pub fn generate_unique_trash_path(trash_dir: &Path, source_path: &Path, timestamp: u128) -> Option<PathBuf> {
82	let base_name = generate_trash_name(source_path, timestamp)?;
83	let mut candidate = trash_dir.join(&base_name);
84	let mut collision_index = 1;
85	
86	while candidate.exists() {
87		let next_name = with_collision_suffix(&base_name, collision_index);
88		candidate = trash_dir.join(next_name);
89		collision_index += 1;
90	}
91	
92	Some(candidate)
93}
94
95/// Recursively renames children of a trashed directory with the same timestamp.
96pub fn rename_recursively(dir: &Path, timestamp: u128) -> std::io::Result<()> {
97	if !dir.is_dir() {
98		return Ok(());
99	}
100	
101	let entries: Vec<PathBuf> = fs::read_dir(dir)?
102		.filter_map(|e| e.ok().map(|entry| entry.path()))
103		.collect();
104	
105	for path in entries {
106		if let Some(new_name) = generate_trash_name(&path, timestamp) {
107			let new_path = path.parent().unwrap_or(dir).join(new_name);
108			fs::rename(&path, &new_path)?;
109			if new_path.is_dir() {
110				rename_recursively(&new_path, timestamp)?;
111			}
112		}
113	}
114	
115	Ok(())
116}
117
118/// Extracts the trailing Unix-milliseconds timestamp from a trash item name.
119/// Valid examples:
120/// - "Note (Folder) 1740681450123.md"
121/// - "Folder (Root) 1740681450123"
122pub fn parse_trash_timestamp(name: &str) -> Option<u128> {
123	let without_ext = strip_extension_for_parsing(name);
124	let without_collision = strip_collision_suffix_for_parsing(without_ext);
125	let (_, timestamp) = without_collision.rsplit_once(' ')?;
126	timestamp.parse::<u128>().ok()
127}
128
129pub fn parse_trash_entry_name(name: &str, is_dir: bool) -> Option<ParsedTrashName> {
130	let (without_ext, extension) = if is_dir {
131		(name, None)
132	} else {
133		let (stem, ext) = split_name_parts(name);
134		(stem, ext)
135	};
136	let without_collision = strip_collision_suffix_for_parsing(without_ext);
137	let (name_and_parent, timestamp) = without_collision.rsplit_once(' ')?;
138	if timestamp.parse::<u128>().is_err() {
139		return None;
140	}
141	let close_index = name_and_parent.rfind(')')?;
142	let open_index = name_and_parent[..close_index].rfind(" (")?;
143	let base_name = name_and_parent[..open_index].trim();
144	let parent_label = name_and_parent[open_index + 2..close_index].trim();
145	if base_name.is_empty() || parent_label.is_empty() {
146		return None;
147	}
148	
149	let original_name = match extension {
150		Some(ext) => format!("{base_name}.{ext}"),
151		None => base_name.to_string(),
152	};
153	
154	Some(ParsedTrashName {
155		original_name,
156		parent_label: parent_label.to_string(),
157	})
158}
159
160pub fn build_restored_destination_path(destination_dir: &Path, original_name: &str) -> Option<PathBuf> {
161	let original_path = destination_dir.join(original_name);
162	if !original_path.exists() {
163		return Some(original_path);
164	}
165	
166	let (stem, extension) = split_name_parts(original_name);
167	let restored_stem = format!("{stem} (Restored)");
168	let initial_name = match extension {
169		Some(ext) => format!("{restored_stem}.{ext}"),
170		None => restored_stem.clone(),
171	};
172	let initial_path = destination_dir.join(&initial_name);
173	if !initial_path.exists() {
174		return Some(initial_path);
175	}
176	
177	let mut index = 1;
178	loop {
179		let candidate_name = match extension {
180			Some(ext) => format!("{restored_stem} ({index}).{ext}"),
181			None => format!("{restored_stem} ({index})"),
182		};
183		let candidate_path = destination_dir.join(candidate_name);
184		if !candidate_path.exists() {
185			return Some(candidate_path);
186		}
187		index += 1;
188	}
189}
190
191pub fn permanently_delete_trash_entry(path: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {
192	if path.is_dir() {
193		fs::remove_dir_all(path)
194	} else {
195		fs::remove_file(path)
196	}
197}
198
199pub fn restore_trashed_names_recursively(dir: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {
200	if !dir.is_dir() {
201		return Ok(());
202	}
203	
204	let entries: Vec<PathBuf> = fs::read_dir(dir)?
205		.filter_map(|entry| entry.ok().map(|value| value.path()))
206		.collect();
207	
208	for entry_path in entries {
209		let is_dir = entry_path.is_dir();
210		let entry_name = entry_path
211			.file_name()
212			.and_then(|value| value.to_str())
213			.unwrap_or_default();
214		
215		let renamed_path = match parse_trash_entry_name(entry_name, is_dir) {
216			Some(parsed) => {
217				let parent_dir = entry_path.parent().unwrap_or(dir);
218				let next_path = build_restored_destination_path(parent_dir, &parsed.original_name)
219					.unwrap_or_else(|| parent_dir.join(&parsed.original_name));
220				fs::rename(&entry_path, &next_path)?;
221				next_path
222			}
223			None => entry_path,
224		};
225		
226		if renamed_path.is_dir() {
227			restore_trashed_names_recursively(&renamed_path)?;
228		}
229	}
230	
231	Ok(())
232}
233
234pub fn purge_expired_trash(vault_path: &str, retention_days: u64) -> PurgeReport {
235	let vault = Path::new(vault_path);
236	let now_ms = SystemTime::now()
237		.duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
238		.unwrap_or_default()
239		.as_millis();
240	purge_expired_trash_with_now(vault, retention_days, now_ms)
241}
242
243fn purge_expired_trash_with_now(vault_path: &Path, retention_days: u64, now_ms: u128) -> PurgeReport {
244	let trash_dir = vault_path.join(TRASH_DIR_NAME);
245	if !trash_dir.exists() {
246		return PurgeReport::default();
247	}
248	
249	let mut report = PurgeReport::default();
250	let retention_ms = retention_days as u128 * MILLIS_PER_DAY;
251	
252	let entries = match fs::read_dir(&trash_dir) {
253		Ok(entries) => entries,
254		Err(err) => {
255			log::error!("Failed to read trash directory '{}': {}", trash_dir.display(), err);
256			report.errors += 1;
257			return report;
258		}
259	};
260	
261	for entry_result in entries {
262		let entry = match entry_result {
263			Ok(entry) => entry,
264			Err(err) => {
265				log::warn!(
266                    "Skipping unreadable trash entry in '{}': {}",
267                    trash_dir.display(),
268                    err
269                );
270				report.errors += 1;
271				continue;
272			}
273		};
274		
275		let entry_path = entry.path();
276		let entry_name = entry.file_name();
277		let Some(entry_name_str) = entry_name.to_str() else {
278			report.skipped_invalid_name += 1;
279			continue;
280		};
281		
282		let Some(item_timestamp_ms) = parse_trash_timestamp(entry_name_str) else {
283			report.skipped_invalid_name += 1;
284			continue;
285		};
286		
287		let age_ms = now_ms.saturating_sub(item_timestamp_ms);
288		if age_ms <= retention_ms {
289			continue;
290		}
291		
292		let delete_result = if entry_path.is_dir() {
293			fs::remove_dir_all(&entry_path)
294		} else {
295			fs::remove_file(&entry_path)
296		};
297		
298		match delete_result {
299			Ok(()) => report.deleted += 1,
300			Err(err) => {
301				report.errors += 1;
302				log::warn!(
303                    "Failed to delete expired trash entry '{}': {}",
304                    entry_path.display(),
305                    err
306                );
307			}
308		}
309	}
310	
311	report
312}
313
314#[cfg(test)]
315mod tests {
316	use super::*;
317	use std::io::Write;
318	use tempfile::tempdir;
319	
320	fn days_to_ms(days: u128) -> u128 {
321		days * MILLIS_PER_DAY
322	}
323	
324	#[test]
325	fn parse_timestamp_from_valid_names() {
326		let ts = 1_740_681_450_123_u128;
327		let file_name = format!("Photo (Daily) {}.png", ts);
328		let file_with_collision = format!("Photo (Daily) {} [1].png", ts);
329		let dir_name = format!("Folder (Projects) {}", ts);
330		
331		assert_eq!(parse_trash_timestamp(&file_name), Some(ts));
332		assert_eq!(parse_trash_timestamp(&file_with_collision), Some(ts));
333		assert_eq!(parse_trash_timestamp(&dir_name), Some(ts));
334	}
335	
336	#[test]
337	fn parse_timestamp_invalid_names_are_skipped() {
338		assert_eq!(parse_trash_timestamp("no-timestamp.md"), None);
339		assert_eq!(parse_trash_timestamp("Bad (Parent) abc.md"), None);
340		assert_eq!(parse_trash_timestamp(""), None);
341	}
342	
343	#[test]
344	fn parse_trash_entry_name_extracts_original_file_name_and_parent() {
345		let parsed =
346			parse_trash_entry_name("Meeting Notes (Projects) 1740681450123.jpeg", false).unwrap();
347		assert_eq!(parsed.original_name, "Meeting Notes.jpeg");
348		assert_eq!(parsed.parent_label, "Projects");
349	}
350
351	#[test]
352	fn parse_trash_entry_name_handles_collision_suffix_for_files() {
353		let parsed =
354			parse_trash_entry_name("Photo (Assets) 1740681450123 [2].png", false).unwrap();
355		assert_eq!(parsed.original_name, "Photo.png");
356		assert_eq!(parsed.parent_label, "Assets");
357	}
358	
359	#[test]
360	fn parse_trash_entry_name_extracts_original_folder_name_and_parent() {
361		let parsed = parse_trash_entry_name("Drafts (Root) 1740681450123", true).unwrap();
362		assert_eq!(parsed.original_name, "Drafts");
363		assert_eq!(parsed.parent_label, "Root");
364	}
365	
366	#[test]
367	fn parse_trash_entry_name_rejects_invalid_names() {
368		assert_eq!(parse_trash_entry_name("no timestamp here.md", false), None);
369		assert_eq!(parse_trash_entry_name("MissingParent 123.md", false), None);
370	}
371	
372	#[test]
373	fn build_restored_destination_path_uses_original_name_when_available() {
374		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
375		let destination = build_restored_destination_path(dir.path(), "Note.md").unwrap();
376		assert_eq!(
377			destination.file_name().and_then(|value| value.to_str()),
378			Some("Note.md")
379		);
380	}
381	
382	#[test]
383	fn build_restored_destination_path_adds_restored_suffix_and_counter_for_files() {
384		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
385		let destination = dir.path();
386		fs::write(destination.join("Note.md"), "").unwrap();
387		fs::write(destination.join("Note (Restored).md"), "").unwrap();
388		
389		let resolved = build_restored_destination_path(destination, "Note.md").unwrap();
390		
391		assert_eq!(
392			resolved.file_name().and_then(|value| value.to_str()),
393			Some("Note (Restored) (1).md")
394		);
395	}
396	
397	#[test]
398	fn build_restored_destination_path_adds_restored_suffix_and_counter_for_folders() {
399		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
400		let destination = dir.path();
401		fs::create_dir_all(destination.join("Project")).unwrap();
402		fs::create_dir_all(destination.join("Project (Restored)")).unwrap();
403		
404		let resolved = build_restored_destination_path(destination, "Project").unwrap();
405		
406		assert_eq!(
407			resolved.file_name().and_then(|value| value.to_str()),
408			Some("Project (Restored) (1)")
409		);
410	}
411	
412	#[test]
413	fn permanently_delete_trash_entry_removes_top_level_directory() {
414		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
415		let trash_dir = dir.path().join(".trash");
416		let trashed = trash_dir.join("Project (Root) 1740681450123");
417		fs::create_dir_all(trashed.join("nested")).unwrap();
418		
419		permanently_delete_trash_entry(&trashed).unwrap();
420		
421		assert!(!trashed.exists());
422	}
423	
424	#[test]
425	fn restore_trashed_names_recursively_restores_nested_file_names() {
426		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
427		let project_dir = dir.path().join("Project");
428		fs::create_dir_all(&project_dir).unwrap();
429		fs::write(
430			project_dir.join("Child Note (Project) 1740681450123.png"),
431			"nested",
432		)
433			.unwrap();
434		
435		restore_trashed_names_recursively(&project_dir).unwrap();
436		
437		assert!(project_dir.join("Child Note.png").exists());
438	}
439	
440	#[test]
441	fn generate_unique_trash_path_appends_counter_when_name_exists() {
442		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
443		let vault = dir.path();
444		let trash = vault.join(".trash");
445		fs::create_dir_all(&trash).unwrap();
446		
447		let source_dir = vault.join("Folder A");
448		fs::create_dir_all(&source_dir).unwrap();
449		let source_file = source_dir.join("Note.md");
450		fs::write(&source_file, "note").unwrap();
451		
452		let timestamp = 1_740_681_450_123_u128;
453		let first_name = generate_trash_name(&source_file, timestamp).unwrap();
454		fs::write(trash.join(&first_name), "existing").unwrap();
455		
456		let unique_path = generate_unique_trash_path(&trash, &source_file, timestamp).unwrap();
457		assert_eq!(
458			unique_path.file_name().and_then(|name| name.to_str()),
459			Some("Note (Folder A) 1740681450123 [1].md")
460		);
461	}
462	
463	#[test]
464	fn expiration_boundary_is_strictly_greater_than_retention() {
465		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
466		let vault = dir.path();
467		let trash = vault.join(".trash");
468		fs::create_dir_all(&trash).unwrap();
469		
470		let now_ms = 2_000_000_000_000_u128;
471		let exactly_30d = now_ms - days_to_ms(30);
472		let older_than_30d = now_ms - days_to_ms(30) - 1;
473		
474		let keep_name = format!("Keep (Root) {}.md", exactly_30d);
475		let delete_name = format!("Delete (Root) {}.md", older_than_30d);
476		fs::write(trash.join(&keep_name), "keep").unwrap();
477		fs::write(trash.join(&delete_name), "delete").unwrap();
478		
479		let report = purge_expired_trash_with_now(vault, 30, now_ms);
480		assert_eq!(report.deleted, 1);
481		assert!(trash.join(keep_name).exists());
482		assert!(!trash.join(delete_name).exists());
483	}
484	
485	#[test]
486	fn purge_noop_when_trash_missing() {
487		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
488		let report = purge_expired_trash_with_now(dir.path(), 30, 2_000_000_000_000);
489		assert_eq!(report, PurgeReport::default());
490	}
491	
492	#[test]
493	fn purge_deletes_old_top_level_file_and_directory_only() {
494		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
495		let vault = dir.path();
496		let trash = vault.join(".trash");
497		fs::create_dir_all(&trash).unwrap();
498		
499		let now_ms = 2_000_000_000_000_u128;
500		let old_ts = now_ms - days_to_ms(31);
501		let new_ts = now_ms - days_to_ms(1);
502		
503		let old_file = trash.join(format!("OldFile (Root) {}.md", old_ts));
504		let old_dir = trash.join(format!("OldDir (Root) {}", old_ts));
505		let new_dir = trash.join(format!("NewDir (Root) {}", new_ts));
506		
507		fs::write(&old_file, "old").unwrap();
508		fs::create_dir_all(&old_dir).unwrap();
509		fs::write(old_dir.join("child.txt"), "child").unwrap();
510		
511		fs::create_dir_all(new_dir.join("nested")).unwrap();
512		// This nested item looks old, but cleanup is top-level only.
513		let nested_old = new_dir.join("nested").join(format!("Nested (Root) {}.md", old_ts));
514		fs::write(&nested_old, "nested old").unwrap();
515		
516		let report = purge_expired_trash_with_now(vault, 30, now_ms);
517		assert_eq!(report.deleted, 2);
518		assert!(!old_file.exists());
519		assert!(!old_dir.exists());
520		assert!(new_dir.exists());
521		assert!(nested_old.exists());
522	}
523	
524	#[test]
525	fn purge_counts_invalid_names() {
526		let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
527		let vault = dir.path();
528		let trash = vault.join(".trash");
529		fs::create_dir_all(&trash).unwrap();
530		let mut f = fs::File::create(trash.join("no timestamp here.md")).unwrap();
531		writeln!(f, "data").unwrap();
532		
533		let report = purge_expired_trash_with_now(vault, 30, 2_000_000_000_000_u128);
534		assert_eq!(report.deleted, 0);
535		assert_eq!(report.skipped_invalid_name, 1);
536		assert_eq!(report.errors, 0);
537	}
538}

create_folder in tessellum_lib::commands::folders - RustSkip to main content

create_folder

tessellum_lib0.1.0

In tessellum_lib::commands::folders

tessellum_lib::commands::folders

Function create_folder 

Source 
pub async fn create_folder(
    vault_path: String,
    folder_name: String,
) -> Result<String, String>
Expand description

Asynchronous command to create a new folder within a specified vault path.

This function performs the following operations:

  1. Sanitizes the provided folder name to ensure it is safe to use in file paths.
  2. Validates that the folder name is not empty after sanitization.
  3. Checks if a folder with the same name already exists at the desired location.
  4. Creates the folder if it does not already exist.

impl<T> Downcast for T
where T: Any,

Â§

fn into_any(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any>

Converts Box<dyn Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Box<dyn Any>, which can then be downcast into Box<dyn ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_rc(self: Rc<T>) -> Rc<dyn Any>

Converts Rc<Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Rc<Any>, which can then be further downcast into Rc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn as_any(&self) -> &(dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &Anyâ€™s vtable from &Traitâ€™s.
Â§

fn as_any_mut(&mut self) -> &mut (dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &mut Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &mut Anyâ€™s vtable from &mut Traitâ€™s.
Â§

impl<T> DowncastSend for T
where T: Any + Send,

Â§

fn into_any_send(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSend) to Box<dyn Any + Send>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

impl<T> DowncastSync for T
where T: Any + Send + Sync,

Â§

fn into_any_sync(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_arc(self: Arc<T>) -> Arc<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Arc<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Arc<Any>, which can then be downcast into Arc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.

sync_vault in tessellum_lib::commands::indexer - RustSkip to main content

sync_vault

tessellum_lib0.1.0

In tessellum_lib::commands::indexer

tessellum_lib::commands::indexer

Function sync_vault 

Source 
pub async fn sync_vault(
    state: State<'_, AppState>,
    kuzu_state: State<'_, Mutex<()>>,
    vault_path: String,
) -> Result<SyncResult, TessellumError>
Expand description

Synchronize the vault database with the filesystem.

This command should be called by the frontend after loading the vault path. It scans all .md files, indexes new/modified files, and removes deleted files.

extract_wikilinks in tessellum_lib::commands::links - RustSkip to main content

extract_wikilinks

tessellum_lib0.1.0

In tessellum_lib::commands::links

tessellum_lib::commands::links

Function extract_wikilinks 

Source 
pub fn extract_wikilinks(content: &str) -> Vec<WikiLink>
Expand description

Extracts all wikilinks from the given input string.

Wikilinks are denoted by the pattern [[...]], where â€œâ€¦â€ represents the content of the link. This function uses a statically compiled regex to find all occurrences and extracts their inner content.

pub async fn resolve_wikilink(
    state: State<'_, AppState>,
    vault_path: String,
    target: String,

Resolves a wikilink target to its full path. Uses the cached in-memory FileIndex for fast lookup without traversing the filesystem.

create_note in tessellum_lib::commands::notes - RustSkip to main content

create_note

tessellum_lib0.1.0

In tessellum_lib::commands::notes

tessellum_lib::commands::notes

Function create_note 

Source 
pub async fn create_note(
    state: State<'_, AppState>,
    kuzu_state: State<'_, Mutex<()>>,
    vault_path: String,
    title: String,
) -> Result<String, TessellumError>
Expand description

Creates a new note file in the specified vault directory with a unique name.

This function takes in a vault path and a title string to create a new .md file in the specified vault directory. If the provided title contains invalid characters or is empty, it is sanitized or defaulted to â€œUntitledâ€. If a file with the same name already exists, the function appends a numeric suffix to the filename to ensure its uniqueness.

impl<T> Downcast for T
where T: Any,

Â§

fn into_any(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any>

Converts Box<dyn Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Box<dyn Any>, which can then be downcast into Box<dyn ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_rc(self: Rc<T>) -> Rc<dyn Any>

Converts Rc<Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Rc<Any>, which can then be further downcast into Rc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn as_any(&self) -> &(dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &Anyâ€™s vtable from &Traitâ€™s.
Â§

fn as_any_mut(&mut self) -> &mut (dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &mut Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &mut Anyâ€™s vtable from &mut Traitâ€™s.
Â§

impl<T> DowncastSend for T
where T: Any + Send,

Â§

fn into_any_send(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSend) to Box<dyn Any + Send>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

impl<T> DowncastSync for T
where T: Any + Send + Sync,

Â§

fn into_any_sync(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_arc(self: Arc<T>) -> Arc<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Arc<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Arc<Any>, which can then be downcast into Arc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.

fn into_any(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any>

Converts Box<dyn Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Box<dyn Any>, which can then be downcast into Box<dyn ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_rc(self: Rc<T>) -> Rc<dyn Any>

Converts Rc<Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Rc<Any>, which can then be further downcast into Rc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn as_any(&self) -> &(dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &Anyâ€™s vtable from &Traitâ€™s.
Â§

fn as_any_mut(&mut self) -> &mut (dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &mut Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &mut Anyâ€™s vtable from &mut Traitâ€™s.

where T: Any + Send,

fn into_any_send(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Send>

Â§

fn into_any_arc(self: Arc<T>) -> Arc<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Arc<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Arc<Any>, which can then be downcast into Arc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.

impl<T> Downcast for T
where T: Any,

Â§

fn into_any(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any>

Converts Box<dyn Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Box<dyn Any>, which can then be downcast into Box<dyn ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_rc(self: Rc<T>) -> Rc<dyn Any>

Converts Rc<Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Rc<Any>, which can then be further downcast into Rc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn as_any(&self) -> &(dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &Anyâ€™s vtable from &Traitâ€™s.
Â§

fn as_any_mut(&mut self) -> &mut (dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &mut Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &mut Anyâ€™s vtable from &mut Traitâ€™s.
Â§

where T: Any + Send,

fn into_any_send(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Send>

impl<T> DowncastSync for T
where T: Any + Send + Sync,

fn into_any_sync(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_arc(self: Arc<T>) -> Arc<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Arc<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Arc<Any>, which can then be downcast into Arc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.

where T: Any,

fn into_any(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any>

Converts Box<dyn Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Box<dyn Any>, which can then be downcast into Box<dyn ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_rc(self: Rc<T>) -> Rc<dyn Any>

Converts Rc<Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Rc<Any>, which can then be further downcast into Rc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn as_any(&self) -> &(dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &Anyâ€™s vtable from &Traitâ€™s.
Â§

fn as_any_mut(&mut self) -> &mut (dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &mut Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &mut Anyâ€™s vtable from &mut Traitâ€™s.
Â§

where T: Any + Send,

Â§

fn into_any_send(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Send>

Â§

impl<T> DowncastSync for T
where T: Any + Send + Sync,

Â§

fn into_any_sync(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_arc(self: Arc<T>) -> Arc<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Arc<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Arc<Any>, which can then be downcast into Arc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.

    pub attempt_count: u32,

}

impl<T> Downcast for T
where T: Any,

Â§

fn into_any(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any>

Converts Box<dyn Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Box<dyn Any>, which can then be downcast into Box<dyn ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_rc(self: Rc<T>) -> Rc<dyn Any>

Converts Rc<Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Rc<Any>, which can then be further downcast into Rc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn as_any(&self) -> &(dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &Anyâ€™s vtable from &Traitâ€™s.
Â§

fn as_any_mut(&mut self) -> &mut (dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &mut Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &mut Anyâ€™s vtable from &mut Traitâ€™s.
Â§

impl<T> DowncastSend for T
where T: Any + Send,

Â§

fn into_any_send(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSend) to Box<dyn Any + Send>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

impl<T> DowncastSync for T
where T: Any + Send + Sync,

Â§

fn into_any_sync(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_arc(self: Arc<T>) -> Arc<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Arc<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Arc<Any>, which can then be downcast into Arc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.

where F: FnOnce(&Self) -> bool,

impl<T> Downcast for T
where T: Any,

Â§

fn into_any(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any>

Converts Box<dyn Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Box<dyn Any>, which can then be downcast into Box<dyn ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_rc(self: Rc<T>) -> Rc<dyn Any>

Converts Rc<Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Rc<Any>, which can then be further downcast into Rc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn as_any(&self) -> &(dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &Anyâ€™s vtable from &Traitâ€™s.
Â§

fn as_any_mut(&mut self) -> &mut (dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &mut Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &mut Anyâ€™s vtable from &mut Traitâ€™s.
Â§

impl<T> DowncastSend for T
where T: Any + Send,

Â§

fn into_any_send(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSend) to Box<dyn Any + Send>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

impl<T> DowncastSync for T
where T: Any + Send + Sync,

Â§

fn into_any_sync(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_arc(self: Arc<T>) -> Arc<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Arc<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Arc<Any>, which can then be downcast into Arc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.

tessellum_lib::commands::vault

Function list_files 

Source 
pub fn list_files(
    vault_path: String,
) -> Result<Vec<FileMetadata>, TessellumError>
Expand description

Lists all files and directories within the specified vault path and retrieves their metadata.

Â§Arguments

  • vault_path - A String specifying the path of the directory to scan.

Â§Returns

  • Ok(Vec<FileMetadata>) containing a vector of FileMetadata structs.
  • Err(String) containing an error message if the vault path does not exist.

In tessellum_lib::commands::vault

tessellum_lib::commands::vault

Function rename_file 

Source 
pub async fn rename_file(
    state: State<'_, AppState>,
    _grafeo_state: State<'_, Mutex<()>>,
    vault_path: String,
    old_path: String,
    new_name: String,
) -> Result<String, TessellumError>
Expand description

Asynchronous Tauri command to rename a file or folder.

Â§Parameters

  • vault_path: The root vault path for security validation.
  • old_path: The current path of the item to be renamed.
  • new_name: The new name for the item.

Â§Returns

  • Ok(String): The new path of the renamed item.
  • Err(String): An error message if the operation fails.

watch_vault in tessellum_lib::commands::watcher - RustSkip to main content

watch_vault

tessellum_lib0.1.0

In tessellum_lib::commands::watcher

tessellum_lib::commands::watcher

Function watch_vault 

Source 
pub async fn watch_vault(
    vault_path: String,
    handle: AppHandle,
    state: State<'_, AppState>,
) -> Result<(), TessellumError>
Expand description

Watches a directory and emits a debounced event to the frontend whenever a file within the directory changes.

This function initializes a file system watcher for the specified directory (vault_path) and listens for changes such as file creation, modification, or deletion. Upon detecting a change, the function emits a file-changed event to the frontend, debounced to prevent event flooding.

tessellum_lib::models

Struct AppState 

Source 
pub struct AppState {
    pub watcher: Mutex<Option<RecommendedWatcher>>,
    pub db: Arc<Database>,
    pub file_index: Arc<Mutex<Option<FileIndex>>>,
    pub asset_index: Arc<Mutex<Option<AssetIndex>>>,
    pub search_index: Arc<Mutex<SearchIndex>>,
    pub search_readiness: Mutex<SearchReadinessState>,
}
Expand description

Represents the application state that contains shared resources such as a file watcher and a database connection.

Â§Fields

  • watcher - A thread-safe, optional wrapper around a RecommendedWatcher instance. This watcher is typically used for monitoring file system events. It is wrapped in a Mutex to ensure safe concurrent access across threads.

  • db - A thread-safe, optional shared reference to a Database instance. The Database is wrapped in an Arc for shared ownership across threads. Internally, sqlx handles concurrent access through its connection pool.

  • file_index - Cached FileIndex to resolve links quickly without traversing the FS.

  • asset_index - Cached AssetIndex for media embeds.

FieldsÂ§

Â§watcher: Mutex<Option<RecommendedWatcher>>Â§db: Arc<Database>Â§file_index: Arc<Mutex<Option<FileIndex>>>Â§asset_index: Arc<Mutex<Option<AssetIndex>>>Â§search_index: Arc<Mutex<SearchIndex>>Â§search_readiness: Mutex<SearchReadinessState>

Struct FileIndex 

Source 
pub struct FileIndex { /* private fields */ }
Expand description

In-memory index of files in the vault for fast wikilink resolution.

ImplementationsÂ§

SourceÂ§

impl FileIndex

Source

pub fn build(vault_path: &str) -> Result<Self, String>

Build an index from a vault directory

Source

pub fn resolve(&self, vault_path: &str, link_target: &str) -> Option<PathBuf>

Resolve a wikilink target to a full file path. Returns the best match based on Obsidianâ€™s resolution rules:

  1. If the link contains a path (e.g., â€œfolder/Noteâ€), try to match that structure
  2. If multiple files have the same name, prefer the shortest path (closest to root)
  3. Return None if no match is found
Source

pub fn resolve_or_default(&self, vault_path: &str, link_target: &str) -> PathBuf

Resolve a wikilink target, or generate a default path if the target doesnâ€™t exist.

If the target can be resolved to an existing file, returns that path. Otherwise, returns a fallback path: vault_path / target.md.

Â§

Â§

tessellum_lib::models

Struct FileMetadata 

Source 
pub struct FileMetadata {
    pub path: String,
    pub filename: String,
    pub is_dir: bool,
    pub size: u64,
    pub last_modified: i64,
}
Expand description

Struct representing metadata information for a file or directory.

This structure contains details about a file or directory, including its path, filename, whether itâ€™s a directory, its size, and the last modified timestamp.

Â§Fields

  • path - A String representing the full path of the file or directory.
  • filename - A String representing the name of the file or directory.
  • is_dir - A bool indicating whether the path is a directory (true) or a file (false).
  • size - A u64 representing the size of the file in bytes.
  • last_modified - An i64 representing the last modified timestamp in Unix epoch time.

FieldsÂ§

Struct WikiLink 

Source 
pub struct WikiLink {
    pub target: String,
    pub alias: Option<String>,
}
Expand description

Represents a parsed wikilink from note content.

Â§Fields

  • target - The link target (e.g., â€œNoteâ€ or â€œfolder/Noteâ€)
  • alias - Optional display text after the pipe (e.g., â€œcustom textâ€ in [[Note|custom text]])

impl<T> Downcast for T
where T: Any,

Â§

fn into_any(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any>

Converts Box<dyn Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Box<dyn Any>, which can then be downcast into Box<dyn ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_rc(self: Rc<T>) -> Rc<dyn Any>

Converts Rc<Trait> (where Trait: Downcast) to Rc<Any>, which can then be further downcast into Rc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn as_any(&self) -> &(dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &Anyâ€™s vtable from &Traitâ€™s.
Â§

fn as_any_mut(&mut self) -> &mut (dyn Any + 'static)

Converts &mut Trait (where Trait: Downcast) to &Any. This is needed since Rust cannot generate &mut Anyâ€™s vtable from &mut Traitâ€™s.
Â§

impl<T> DowncastSend for T
where T: Any + Send,

Â§

fn into_any_send(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSend) to Box<dyn Any + Send>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

impl<T> DowncastSync for T
where T: Any + Send + Sync,

Â§

fn into_any_sync(self: Box<T>) -> Box<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Box<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync>, which can then be downcast into Box<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.
Â§

fn into_any_arc(self: Arc<T>) -> Arc<dyn Any + Sync + Send>

Converts Arc<Trait> (where Trait: DowncastSync) to Arc<Any>, which can then be downcast into Arc<ConcreteType> where ConcreteType implements Trait.

SourceÂ§

where F: FnOnce(&Self) -> bool,
